Residents have access to one-on-one "book a librarian" sessions by appointment. Programs for all ages round out the Library's offerings.

Name: Shasta Public Libraries
Description: Shasta Public Libraries is your safe, family friendly public library. We provide the public with current books through various media as well as FREE programs, events and technology services. We serve people who live throughout Shasta County online through our digital library and in person at our three library locations Anderson, Burney, and Redding.

Name: Manchester City Library Foundation
Description:
Mission Statement 
The Manchester City Library Foundation is a private, nonprofit corporation committed to providing supplemental support to the Manchester City Library. The Foundation reinforces the need for continued and increased public support for the library, and serves as a catalyst for attracting private funding from individuals, businesses, organizations and foundations in order to enhance library services to our community.

Name: IFLA IT Section
Description: IFLA IT Section Linkedin Page.

Name: CRAI Biblioteca de Ciències de la Terra (UB-CSIC)
Description:
The Earth Sciences CRAI Library provides comprehensive materials for teaching and research activities at the Faculty for the courses: Geology and Ocean Sciences.
 
Ownership of the library is shared with the Institute of Earth Sciences Jaume Almera (ICTJA, CSIC).
 
With more than 18.000 books, it also has a map library that contains 10,000 maps and 14,000 aerial photographs of Catalonia and Aragon. The library provides a wide range of electronic resources, as databases and electronic journals.

Name: Matilija Collective
Description: Our mission is to celebrate the diversity of our El Monte and San Gabriel Valley community by uplifting stories by Black, Indigenous, People of Color. We aim to create a space to reflect our people of color communities in the San Gabriel Valley, and build multi-racial solidarity together.

Name: Cozy Nest
Description:
Welcome to the Cozy Nest, where comfort and convenience blend seamlessly to create a home away from home. Our PG & Library offer a unique combination of a comfortable living space and a quiet reading sanctuary under one roof. As you step into our elegant lobby, you'll immediately feel at ease, and our well-stocked pantry, complete with a gas pipeline, ensures you can prepare your favorite meals at any time.

We understand the importance of staying connected, which is why we provide 24/7 open spaces and free WiFi, allowing you to work, study, or simply unwind online. Your safety is our priority, and to that end, we have installed CCTV surveillance in all washrooms, ensuring your peace of mind during your stay.

Every room in our cozy nest is fully air-conditioned, making it a perfect retreat from the outside world. Moreover, we have thoughtfully included power backup in each room, so you never have to worry about interruptions in your daily routines. Stay informed with our newspaper facility, keeping you up to date with the latest news and events.

Your well-being is important to us, and that's why we have installed RO drinking water units, providing you with clean and fresh water at all times. Each room also comes with a balcony, allowing you to bask in the natural beauty that surrounds our nest.

Whether you're a student, a working professional, or a traveler seeking a cozy and comfortable space, the Cozy Nest is your ideal destination. Come experience a harmonious blend of modern amenities and warm hospitality, making your stay unforgettable.

Name: Piscataway Public Library
Description:
Piscataway Public Library is a statewide leader in public library service, and was recently voted one of the 2016 “Best of the Best” libraries in Middlesex County. With a documented history of providing pioneering programs and services, Piscataway Public Library is an organization that nurtures leadership and innovation. 

Piscataway Township is located in Middlesex County, across the river from the Rutgers University New Brunswick campus, and near the center of Einstein’s Alley - central New Jersey’s research and technology corridor. Boasting a richly diverse population of over 58,000, Piscataway is the home of several large corporate residents, including Johnson & Johnson, Colgate-Palmolive, and IEEE. Piscataway was recently ranked among the top ten most diverse towns in New Jersey, with significant non-white populations including African-American (20%), Asian (36%), and Hispanic (12%).

Name: Maryland State Library for the Blind and Physically Handicapped
Description: The library is part of the National Library Service of the Library of Congress. We are the regional library for the state of Maryland and serve all eligible people with print disabilities. We have digital books, cassettes, Braille and large print. We provide programs, training, readers advisory. We serve the entire state.

Name: Green Hills Public Library District
Description: We are a public library serving the communities of Palos Hills and Hickory Hills.  The mission of the Green Hills Public Library District is to encourage lifelong learning and enrichment through the exploration of print materials and other media and to provide service and materials which will contribute to a well informed and literate community.

Name: Grand Forks Public_
Description: The Grand Forks Public Library is a cornerstone of the community, committed to meeting the public's evolving needs by engaging minds and transforming lives.  We support a mission to connect the community, enrich the mind and inspire the imagination. The Grand Forks Public Library offers a wide variety of free programs for all ages!

Name: Surpura Community Library, Jodhpur
Description: The Community Library in Surpura, Jodhpur has been established after months of dedicated efforts of the Legal Aid & Awareness Committee, NLU Jodhpur and with the support of a vast nexus of stakeholders.  At the heart of this initiative lies the understanding about the need and importance of reading and learning and an exposure to the world through its medium to the children of Surpura, since that is something that is often missing from academic curriculum at school level. It aims to ensure that varied and diverse knowledge is inculcated in students from an early age, as reading and learning can be truly achieved only through an informed and continued process of creating awareness and commitment in society.

Name: Guelph Tool Library
Description: The Guelph Tool Library is a not-for-profit organization that began in 2016.  We offer the community the chance to share an inventory of over 1200 tools and appliances. Available through a low-cost membership ($70/year), our tools include gardening supplies, small appliances, items for woodworking and home repair, and more. In addition to this, we offer programs and resources for tool usage and safety, food production & preservation, and sustainable living.

Name: Patchogue-Medford Library
Description:
The mission of the Patchogue-Medford Library is to provide resources and opportunities to empower, educate and entertain the community.

The Library is a School District Public Library serving the residents of the Patchogue-Medford School District. The District encompasses the Incorporated Village of Patchogue and the hamlets of North Patchogue, Medford and a portion of East Patchogue. The area is 15.5 square miles extending from the Great South Bay in Patchogue to Granny Road in Medford. Waverly Avenue and North Ocean Avenue form the western border of the district. The eastern border of the district extends from Roe Avenue and South Country Road in Patchogue to just east of White Pine Way in Medford.

Name: Bibliothèque d'archéologie et des sciences de l'Antiquité
Description: Specializing in prehistory and protohistory, archaeology and the sciences of the ancient and medieval world up to the year 1000, the library was created in 1997 by bringing together the holdings of several teams from the CNRS, Paris 1 Panthéon-Sorbonne and Paris Nanterre universities. The library is a member of the Frantiq national network and the Archéologie en Ile-de-France network. As part of Frantiq, it was awarded the "Collection d'excellence" label by the GIS CollEx-Persée in 2017. This scheme aims to promote and facilitate researchers' access to collections of excellence held by major scientific libraries. It participates in the Plan de Conservation Partagé (PCP) for Antiquity periodicals. The themes concerned are archaeology in France, ancient Egypt, Assyriology and oriental archaeology. Since 2020, we have been a partner of Mir@bel, a website that facilitates access to scientific journals and all their resources available on the Internet - from summary to full text, free or by subscription, as well as to its editorial policy.

Name: Biblioteka Polska w Amsterdamie
Description: Fundacja Biblioteka Polska ma na celu integrację Polaków w Holandii oraz szerzenie polskiej kultury za granicą.

Name: LOXION MOBILE LIBRARY NPC
Description:
Loxion Mobile Library 
Introduction: 

Language is the key that unlocks literacy, and literacy opens up possibilities for all kinds of new learning. Without sound language and literacy skills, children are likely to struggle throughout their school careers. On the other hand, when children become good readers and writers, their confidence and self-esteem grow, along with their appetite for learning. Put simply, children who read better learn better. In turn, children who make good progress in primary school are more likely to leave school with qualifications, pursue further education and ultimately find employment.

Loxion Library was established in 2020 by two youth from the Vrygrond community who saw the need to support young children with reading during Covid. It started with supporting children from a local pre-school and reading for children in the local playgrounds where they are normally just playing. It has grown from strength to strength where it support schoolchildren and young adults.
Currently we have 257 children in our database that we support with writing and reading skills. 
Reading is a system issue.  Systematic problems require systematic responses. Which means targeting all factors that hamper and stall the foundation of reading in young children and in the foundation phase.
Vision: 
‘’The book machine that comes to you’’
Our vision is to ensure that every school going age child in informal settlement, townships and rural areas has access to a book and to reading every year. We engage with children in their spaces where they feel comfortable.
Mission:
We do this by promoting, encouraging and supporting children with reading skills and thus creating a culture of reading in our communities. We engage young children in educational games, writing and reading skills in fun and enjoyable ways. We do this by working closely with schools, teachers, parents, caregivers, playgroups, libraries, health practitioners, and community leaders and government. We h

Name: Meescan
Description:
Disrupting the self-checkout industry with constant innovation through product and service. We’re here to help librarians and libraries of all shapes and sizes, to provide a better service to their patrons, by creating library solutions that solve problems and make self-checkout easier, simpler, and more affordable.

Different from our competitors, our customers aren’t just clients, they are partners, we face these challenges and create solutions together because we believe a better library is possible. With Meescan, you’re joining a revolution.

Our kiosks give patrons autonomy, privacy, and simplicity, while libraries enjoy simple installation, great functionality, and lower costs thanks to our innovative cloud-based architecture.

Our mobile checkout is built on a unique cloud-based design. The Meescan self-checkout app is an efficient and private way for patrons to borrow items from anywhere in the stacks. The Meescan app includes plenty of supplemental features like security integration, receipt printing, fee collection and even curbside pickup!

Meescan provides everything your library needs to provide a better self-checkout experience for patrons.

Name: BIBLIOTECA PUBLICA AC.
Description:
La Biblioteca Pública de San Miguel de Allende AC is a cultural center. Founded in 1955 in San Miguel de Allende, central Mexico. 

Its mission is to provide a wide range of educational and cultural activities.

La Biblioteca Pública programming is focused in 3 broad areas:
1. Arts and Culture
    The Biblioteca Pública provides free access to the largest bilingual library in the area. 

With a catalog of more than 55,000 titles, the Biblioteca has more than 16,000 members to whom we lend books.   

There are lovely spaces throughout the library for our members to discover a new favorite book.

-	We offer classes in arts and crafts, piano and guitar lessons, choir for children and adults, painting, sculpture, dance, yoga, etc.  More than 400 people take advantage of these workshops and classes every year.

-	We also offer bilingual entertainment in the form of musical concerts, plays, and films at very reasonable cost in our Teatro Santa Ana and Sala Quetzal.

2.	Education and Training:
-	We help our members increase their proficiencies in classes which include literacy, English and Spanish as a second language, sign language, computer technology, science club, mathematics, etc.

-	We fight to prevent the school dropout rate with our scholarship and mentor programs.  

As many as 100 high school and university students are granted scholarships each year based upon social and academic criteria. 

In addition to economical support, we provide the students with mentorship during their studies. 

In 2020, the Biblioteca delivered 20 laptop-computers to its university level scholars to help meet the new demand for remote education.

3.	 Community Building
-	We welcome our community in an inviting, safe environment.  Access to our facilities is unrestricted and free. 

We routinely offer lectures on a wide variety of topics that are of interest to residents and visitors alike.

Name: La Crosse Public Library
Description: Serving the La Crosse public area

Name: Bibliotheek Salland
Description:
Bibliotheek Salland is een laagdrempelige, publieke voorziening die mensen helpt bij hun persoonlijke ontwikkeling teneinde hun de kans te bieden volwaardig en zelfstandig aan de samenleving deel te nemen. De bibliotheek is ook een ontmoetingsplaats waar mensen terecht kunnen om te ontspannen, te leren, zich te informeren en kennis, ervaring en interesses met elkaar te delen.

De bibliotheek opereert vanuit een onafhankelijke positie, is toegankelijk voor iedereen en stelt in alles wat zij doet het ontwikkelingsperspectief van mensen centraal. Daarbij werkt zij samen met lokale partners, het bibliothekennetwerk en de gemeenschap. De bibliotheek draagt daarmee bij aan het welzijn van het individu en de (kennis)samenleving als collectief.

Name: Nyack Library
Description: Established in 1879, Nyack Library serves the residents of Nyack, Upper Nyack, South Nyack, Grand View, and Valley Cottage. Our trained staff can assist you in the use of the collection or direct you to other sources of information. If a desired item is not in the Ramapo Catskill Library System, our staff can request it through Interlibrary Loan from another library system. The collection of books, periodicals, and audiovisuals is enhanced with electronic access.

Name: LiberPal
Description: LiberPal is a social library platform revolutionizing the way we learn and share those learnings with others.

Name: Essex County Library
Description: Essex County Library is a public library system based out of 360 FAIRVIEW AVENUE WEST, SUITE G01, Essex, Ontario, Canada.

Name: Polarbibblo
Description:
Polarbibblo är en kreativ arena för barn på meänkieli, samiska språk och svenska. Här kan barn skriva och publicera berättelser, dikter och boktips med stöd av redaktörer. De kan rita och spela spel, ta del av det andra barn gjort och lösa kluriga quiz. Vår sajt främjar barns läslust, skaparglädje och språkutveckling.

Sajten drivs av Region Norrbotten i samarbete med folkbiblioteken i Norrbotten. Det är helt gratis att använda Polarbibblo.

***

Polarbibblon veppisivula lapset saavat mahollisuuen kehittää kielitaitoa. Apuna heilä oon aikuset retaktöörit. Lapset voivat kirjottaa runoja, kertomuksia, antaa kirjatipsiä ja saaja inspirasjuunia muitten lasten tekstistä ja pelata kielipeliä.

Polarbibblo ottaa vasthaan, auttaa lapsia niin ette heän tekstiä saatethaan julkasta näilä minuriteettikielilä.

Päämäärä oon ette enemmän lapsia saapii matkhaan kaiken sen rikhauen mikä löytyy näissä kansalisissa minuriteettikielissä.

***

Polarbibblos sáhttet mánát ovddidit gielaset, doaimmaheaddji doarjagiin. Dáppe sáhttet divttaid, muitalusaid ja girjecavgileamiid čállit. Siiddus sáhttet oažžut inspirášuvnna eará mánáid teavsttaiguin ja spellat giellaspealuid.  

Siiddus gávdno doaimmahusa sisdoallu iešguđet gillii. Polarbibblo sáhttá maid váldit vuostá, fállat doarjaga doaimmaheaddjis ja almmuhit našuvnnalaš unnitlogugielaid teavsttaid maid mánát leat čállán.  

Ulbmil lea veahkehit eanet mánáid oahppat našuvnnalaš unnitlogugiela, giella mii sáhttá leat riggodat.

***

Polarbibblon máná máhtti ållessjattuk dåjmadiddjij dårjajn åvddånahttet ietjasa gielav. Dánna máhtti tjállet divtajt, giehtojt ja girjjegätjástusájt, måvtåstuhteduvvat ietjá mánáj tevstajs ja spieledit giellaspelav. 

Webbabielen gávnnu redaksjåvnålasj sisadno umasse gielajda. Polarbibblo máhttá aj duosstot, vaddet dåjmadiddjedårjav ja almodit mánáj tevstajt dájda rijkalasj unneplågogielajda. 
 
Ulmme l doarjjot ienep máná åmasti boanndudagáv rijkalasj unneplågogiella merkaj.

Name: WT Cox Information Services
Description:
For over 45 years WT Cox Information Services has proudly offered the highest level of customer service standards in the industry. We are committed to partnering with our libraries to provide cost-effective, personalized serials management services along with integrated solutions such as Journal Finder (A to Z, Link Resolver an ERM) and eStats (Statistics and Analytics) allowing libraries to make informed collection development decisions. 

Experience Choice. Where extraordinary service happens.

Name: Woodstock Public Library
Description:
The Woodstock Public Library provides library & computer services for the residents of the Woodstock Community Unit School District #200 in McHenry county Illinois. Our service area includes Woodstock, Wonder Lake and the Bull Valley area. 

In addition to a collection of over 100,000 books, CDs, and DVDs; the library also has 37 public computer stations. All public stations provide internet, Microsoft Office 2007 and photo editing software. Customers can login using their library card numbers or with a guest number provided by the reference librarians. 

Computer training classes are offered on a regular basis. Topics include basic & intermediate level classes in Microsoft Word & Excel; Internet, computer file management. Classes are also offered in social media (Facebook, Twitter and LinkedIn) as well as Genealogy research .

Name: OWWL Library System
Description:
The OWWL Library System is a New York State chartered, cooperative public library system supporting library services and the forty-two public libraries in Ontario, Wayne, Wyoming, and Livingston Counties. 

Each OWWL Library is governed by its own board of trustees. A nine member board representing the forty-two member libraries oversees the administration of the System.

Name: Providence Athenaeum
Description: The Providence Athenæum seeks to enrich the literary, cultural, and intellectual pursuits of its members and surrounding community through extraordinary collections and innovative programming. Committed to the stewardship of our historic building, the physical culture of books, and the advancement of the humanities, the Athenæum welcomes diverse audiences and spirited conversation.

Name: Library Crossroads Consulting
Description: Library Crossroads, LLC is a New Jersey-based consulting firm helping public libraries solve problems and maximize potential through customized strategic plans, director searches, and web design services. Our team of consultants have decades of leadership experience in growth-oriented planning, recruiting and hiring talented library leaders, web design and development, and engaging and building community support.

Name: Pilot School
Description: Located in Wilmington, DE, The Pilot School teaches children ages 5  14 who experience languagebased learning differences.

Name: Wisconsin State Law Library
Description: We serve the legal information needs of the State of Wisconsin, attorneys and the public by providing the highest quality of professional expertise to facilitate equal access to the law. The Wisconsin State Law Library also manages the Milwaukee Legal Resource Center and Dane County Legal Resource Center.

Name: Greenwood Public Library
Description: The Greenwood Public Library actively enriches lives, promotes discovery, and fosters personal growth.

Name: ROSENBERG LIBRARY
Description: ROSENBERG LIBRARY is a public library extraordinaire located in our original 1904 building in Galveston, Texas. The Moody Wing opened in 1971 more than doubling the floor space. From the start the library included a children's department, history center and museum.

Name: Töölö
Description:
Töölö is an online children’s “cloud” library offering curated books, recommendations and physical delivery of books to your doorstep. Töölö means ‘Neighbourhoods’ in Finnish and that is what we aim to build - a thriving community of young readers in every neighbourhood across the country, by encouraging and empowering them to connect with each other and open the fascinating world of books to them.

Töölö is committed to the cause of building a library for children by crowd sourcing the idle books lying in every household of that community.

It is an online platform where children have access to a large and varied selection of books, peer recommendations as well as the chance to engage in a plethora of reading activities like storytelling sessions, author interactions and lots more!. One can order for books online and physical books then get home delivered and picked up once the child is done reading them.

While on the one hand, the platform aims at meeting the needs of voracious readers; it also aspires to kindle the joy of reading in children who are not naturally drawn to books. The platform is designed in a manner where one can easily “discover” curated books across a variety of genres.

After all, as J K Rowling herself said, “ If you don’t like to read, you haven’t found the right book! ”

Name: Marigold Library System
Description: Marigold Library System is a municipal collaborative that provides progressive library services to 43 municipalities, Stoney Nakoda and Siksika Nations, 36 member library boards, 35 libraries, 12 book deposits, 3 library lending lockers and over 340,000 people across south-central Alberta.

Name: Novi Public Library
Description: Novi Public Library is a vital community hub for enrichment, exploration and understanding.

Name: Oceanside Library
Description: Oceanside Library is a vibrant community hub dedicated to providing innovative and accessible library services to the Oceanside School District community. We empower lifelong learning, foster creativity, and connect individuals through a wide array of resources, programs, and events. Our library is committed to adapting to the evolving needs of our patrons, offering both digital and traditional resources to enrich the educational, cultural, and recreational experiences of our community.

Name: Ela Area Public Library District
Description: Ela Area Public Library District is located at 275 Mohawk Trail, Lake Zurich, IL, United States.

Name: Universitätsbibliothek Duisburg-Essen
Description:
Die Universitätsbibliothek Duisburg-Essen ist eine zentrale Einrichtung der Universität Duisburg-Essen. Als offener Wissens- und Lernort bietet sie ihren Nutzer:innen verlässlichen Zugang zu Literatur, Informationen und Daten und schafft Raum für Kommunikation. Die Angebote der UB Duisburg-Essen fördern Studium, Lehre, Forschung und den Wissenstransfer in die Gesellschaft. 

Impressum: 
https://www.uni-due.de/de/impressum.shtml

Social-Media-Netiquette:
https://www.uni-due.de/ub/netiquette.php

Name: Nebraska Library Association
Description: Supporting all library staff, advocates, and users across Nebraska. Home of the Nebraska Golden Sower Award.

Name: Chatstaff
Description:
Helping Your Patrons When You Can't
Chatstaff provides high quality coverage for chat reference services at prices that make sense. Your online resources are available 24/7 -- now you can offer your patrons help whenever they decide to use the library. Chatstaff is designed to work with LibraryH3lp software but can adapt to the software you use locally.

Name: Consortium of Swiss Academic Libraries
Description: The Consortium of Swiss Academic Libraries obtains licenses for scientific resources (electronic journals, eBooks and databases). It offers a broad range of services and supports the efforts to implement the national open access strategy. It therefore plays an instrumental role in guaranteeing the quality and competitiveness of Switzerland as a university and research hub.

Name: Publindústria, Lda. (Engebook)
Description:
A Engebook é a marca editorial da Publindústria para o comércio electrónico de conteúdos de engenharia e gestão.
 
Fundada em 1986, a Publindústria começou por ser uma empresa de comunicação vocacionada para a indústria transformadora, através da edição de revistas técnico-científicas.
 
Hoje, a Publindústria é uma empresa de referência na edição de informação técnico-científica junto da indústria, dos técnicos e profissionais de vários sectores industriais e da comunidade académica e científica em geral. Apostada, como sempre, em produzir e editar informação técnico-científica de rigor e elevada qualidade, para além da edição de revistas técnico-científicas, a Publindústria é hoje também editora de livros técnicos de engenharia e gestão tendo já acumulado um vasto portfólio de obras de referência em vários domínios.
 
Fiel ao compromisso inicial de satisfazer as necessidades de informação e comunicação da indústria, a Publindústria lança agora a www.engebook.com, uma plataforma de comércio electrónico de conteúdos de engenharia e gestão. Para além das obras publicadas pela editora, a Engebook reúne igualmente uma ampla oferta de um vasto leque de editoras estrangeiras especializadas em vários domínios do conhecimento

Name: Greensboro Public Library
Description: Welcome to the official Greensboro Public Library LinkedIn page, your Conduit to Destiny!

Name: SALINE COUNTY LIBRARY
Description:
The Saline County Library serves Saline County in Arkansas, which runs 99 miles from point to point. The Saline County Library consists of three facilities: the Bob Herzfeld Memorial Library (the headquarters for the system) in Benton, the Mabel Boswell Memorial Library in Bryant, and the East End Branch Library in East End.

In addition to the library facilities, a 24-hour electronic information center is available through this website with online resources and library services available using your library card number. Staff members are happy to give patrons a guided tour on what is available via the internet!

The Bob Herzfeld Memorial Library was opened in 2003 with 32,000 sq. ft. The Mabel Boswell Memorial Library was completed in the year 2002; the Ruth Zoch Children’s Wing was added on in the year 2007. The Mabel Boswell Memorial Library is 10,000 sq. ft. The East End Branch Library opened in the year 2024. The suite occupied by the East End branch is 3,000 sq. ft. and has been leased for three years. All three libraries provide wireless access computing as well as desktop computers.

The mission of the Saline County Library is to create unique, enriching experiences that put you at the center of our story.

Name: University of Texas Libraries
Description: The University of Texas Libraries employs a dynamic workforce of employees. From administrative staff to IT professionals to academic Librarians, UT Libraries employs full time workers as well as both undergraduate and graduate students.

Name: Public Library Of Brookline
Description: The Public Library of Brookline promotes discovery, connection, and opportunity for all residents. Through our diverse resources and innovative programs, we aim to enrich the lives of our citizens through free access to information in many different media and formats. We are a cultural destination and a site for learning and recreation, as well as a place where people come together to exchange ideas. We strive to be both a cornerstone of our community and a gateway to explorations of a wider world.

Name: REFORMA Colorado Chapter
Description:
REFORMA Colorado is a chapter of REFORMA National: The National Association to Promote Library and Information Services to Latinos and the Spanish-Speaking

Anybody can join, not only librarians!

Name: Bibliotecas Digitales
Description: Bibliotecas Digitales ofrece una plataforma en línea que facilita el acceso a contenidos especializados completamente en español; cuenta con un amplio catálogo enfocado en diversas áreas de estudio.
Location: Ciudad de México, Ciudad de México

Name: Arquivo Geral da Cidade do Rio
Description:
O Arquivo Geral da Cidade do Rio de Janeiro - órgão da Secretaria Municipal de Cultura da Prefeitura do Rio - foi instituído em 1567, na época da fundação da cidade. Durante os séculos seguintes, acumulou os documentos oficiais produzidos pela Câmara Municipal e, no período republicano, pela Prefeitura. Atualmente, também mantém sob custódia coleções de documentos particulares de prefeitos, administradores, professores, engenheiros e personalidades da vida carioca.

O acervo do AGCRJ, formado por documentos manuscritos e impressos, fotografias, gravuras, fitas, vídeos, livros e periódicos - pode ser consultado por qualquer cidadão interessado na história da cidade ou que necessite comprovar direitos, além de fornecer subsídios para a tomada de decisões no âmbito da administração pública.

Os documentos do AGCRJ permitem a reconstituição de amplos aspectos da história da sociedade carioca e das frequentes intervenções realizadas no seu espaço físico e social, ao longo dos mais de 400 anos de evolução urbanística. Constituem, portanto, um patrimônio de valor inestimável para a afirmação da identidade cultural do povo carioca.

Name: Morton Grove Public Library
Description: The Morton Grove Public Library serves the informational, professional, educational and recreational needs of Morton Grove's diverse, multicultural community. Morton Grove Public Library's building of 31,000 square feet houses more than 136,000 items, ranging from current best-sellers to the latest in audiobooks and DVDs, popular computer, history and medicine titles, as well as more than 400 magazines and newsletters.

Name: The Mobile Reading Room
Description:
‘The Mobile Reading Room’ is  a mobile library, with a reading program designed  to help foster a love of reading, give access to books, close the gap in comprehension and train volunteers on current methods in reading instruction.

The program was deemed necessary due to an obvious gap in comprehension amongst students at all levels especially in the areas of figurative language, inferencing and drawing conclusions. 

Each session also includes an art/activity component that will help participants express comprehension in different, and applied formats that can be transferred to all subject areas.

It is our hope that the individual schools and communities will eventually take ownership and ensure success of the program.

Name: Mountain Plains Library Association
Description: The Mountain Plains Library Association (MPLA) is a 12-state association of librarians, library paraprofessionals, and friends of libraries in Arizona, Colorado, Kansas, Montana, Nebraska, Nevada, New Mexico, North Dakota, Oklahoma, South Dakota, Utah, and Wyoming.

Name: New Rochelle Public Library
Description:
Situated in downtown New Rochelle, the New Rochelle Public Library (NRPL) offers a comprehensive collection that includes retrospective and current materials; up-to-date technology by which information can be accessed; and a wide range of community services and programs tailored to a diverse audience. Chartered in 1894, NRPL is a school-district library with its own operating budget and a Board of Trustees comprised of seven elected members. It has served the 81,500 residents of New Rochelle from its main library building in the downtown business district since 1979. 
A smaller branch, located in the park in front of the New Rochelle High School, 
was restored through a grassroots effort and reopened as the Huguenot Children’s Library in 1997.

Name: Alberta Law Libraries
Description: Alberta Law Libraries (ALL) is a provincial network of law libraries existing to provide research support and information services to the legal community, self represented litigants and all Albertans. Access to legal reference assistance and Alberta Law Libraries’ collections is available to all Albertans.

Name: Dinas Perpustakaan dan Kearsipan Kota Surabaya
Libraries for Ethiopia Fund (LiFEF)
Description:
ABOUT US
In Ethiopia, library access is extremely limited, making it a "library desert." Despite a population of 120 million, only about 20 public libraries are available. The Libraries for Ethiopia Fund (LiFEF) aims to address this issue by providing public libraries in underserved communities. As a registered 501(c)(3) tax-exempt non-profit organization, LiFEF promotes a reading culture and offers educational opportunities, especially for children and young adults.

Our libraries will offer not only books but also computers and a variety of educational and creative activities, such as mentoring programs, to broaden horizons and foster learning. Our goal is to create safe and secure places for self-expression and education, connecting people with books and promoting the vision of GENERATION READ.

OUR MISSION
In Ethiopia, only 33.1% of primary school children progress to secondary school. Our goal is to enhance literacy and education across the nation by creating libraries and utilizing existing educational resources. Our objective is to equip people and communities with the essential tools and knowledge to prosper and excel in today's society. Our aspiration is to inspire a new generation that adores learning and harbors an unyielding passion for knowledge.

OBJECTIVES
• Establish a suitable, inviting, and well-equipped space for students and the public to read, research, and explore opportunities for advancement.
• Promote a culture of reading and expand educational opportunities for children and young people.
• Fostering a safe and inclusive learning environment that inspires curiosity and personal growth.
• Empower children and young people with the tools they need to reach their full potential.
• Reduce school dropouts by ensuring access to books and educational resources.
• Cultivate a community passionate about reading and ignite a generation of learners.

Name: Association of Libraries of Czech Universities

Description:
The PMI Victorian History Library Inc. is a community owned and run library specialising in the history of Victoria, Australia.
The library collection is the only lending collection of its kind in Australia – with over 30,000 books for loan and over 40,000 items on site, including titles that are unavailable elsewhere. You can find information about all aspects of the history of Victoria including:

Company Histories
Design & Architecture
Family History/Genealogy
First Nations Australians
Theatre & Cinema
Town History
Transport
Visual & Performing Arts
We also provide a professional information service for our members, access to a number of indexes, directories and online resources, as well as organise and present educational activities and events.

The PMI is also the home of:

The Mechanics’ Institutes of Victoria
The Cinema and Theatre Historical Society and 
Victorian Railway History Library.

Name: Fundación Sancho el Sabio Fundazioa
Description:
La Fundación Sancho el Sabio Vital Fundazioa, con el apoyo de Fundación Vital, mantiene desde sus inicios en 1964 los mismos objetivos: recopilar, ordenar, conservar y difundir la memoria bibliográfica y documental referida a la cultura vasca. Asimismo desde su origen, el ámbito geográfico contemplado incluye los territorios actuales de la Comunidad Autónoma del País Vasco (Euskadi), Comunidad Autónoma de Navarra y País Vasco Francés dentro del Departamento de Pirineos Atlánticos, ampliado por razones históricas a las zonas limítrofes comprendidas entre el río Ebro al Sur, y el río Garona, al Norte. Su amplia trayectoria temporal, ha posibilitado la formación de uno de los fondos documentales vascos más ricos y completos, de forma que en su biblioteca se pueden encontrar: monografías, publicaciones periódicas, fotografías, manuscritos desde el siglo XV, material gráfico desde el siglo XVIII, mapas desde el siglo XVI o documentación familiar… En definitiva, una importante riqueza patrimonial que le ha convertido en un referente obligado para la investigación sobre la cultura vasca. De igual modo, en los últimos años se ha hecho un gran trabajo de digitalización, no solo para su conservación, sino también para que a través de las nuevas tecnologías, sus fondos sean accesibles desde cualquier parte del mundo. La Fundación Sancho el Sabio facilita la consulta y acceso a su fondo a través de:
- Orientación e información general.
- Información bibliográfica.
- Consulta en sala.
- Visitas guiadas
- Acceso a su catálogo automatizado: https://catalogo.sanchoelsabio.eus/
- Archivo y fototeca https://archinet.sanchoelsabio.eus
- Memoria Digital Vasca https://www.euskalmemoriadigitala.eus/

Name: National Library of Pakistan
Description:
Based on the ideological and cultural needs of the people of Pakistan and keeping in view the importance of the national library as the nation’s top most resource centre of its literary heritage and fountain head of all kinds of library development in the country, the following objectives have been laid down for the National library: -
1. To serve as legal depository for all Pakistani publications under copy right ordinance 1962.
2. To preserve national literary heritage for the use of present as well as future generations.
3. To compile and publish Pakistan national Bibliography and bibliographies in order to maintain national bibliographic control.
4. To serve as national/international book exchange center.
5. To serve as ISBN agency for Pakistan
6. To represent Pakistan at relevant international forums on behalf of government of Pakistan.
7. To promote resource sharing and bibliographic standards among libraries of Pakistan.
8. To organize trainings, workshops, conferences, and seminars for library professionals in the country
9. To extend advisory services on technical professionals issues of libraries in Pakistan
10. To collect what is significant in other literatures including selected scientific and technical materials;
11. Provision of reference and research services to ministerial libraries
12. Provision of free library services to the all citizen of Pakistan irrespective of geographical
13. Provision of free library services to the all citizen of Pakistan irrespective of geographical divisions and foreigners residing in Islamabad

Name: Chennai International Book Fair (Official)
Description:
The Chennai International Book Fair (CIBF) is organized by the Tamil Nadu Government with the vision of "Bringing the World to Tamil, Taking Tamil to the World," through global literature and cultural exchanges.
 
CIBF features a combination of International conferences, Rights trade, and Literary events. This B2B platform offers a unique opportunity for translators, writers, and publishers to meet in one place and engage in rights exchanges. The Government of Tamil Nadu successfully organized three editions of CIBF in 2023, 2024 and 2025. In CIBF 2025, 64 countries participated and an impressive 1354 Memorandums of Understanding were signed between primarily Tamil publishers and their global counterparts on exchanging translation copyrights.

To take this momentum forward, the fourth edition of the Chennai International Book Fair is organised from January 16 to 18, 2026 at Kalaivanar Arangam, Chennai.

Conformément au décret n° 2011-996, le SCD met en œuvre la politique documentaire de l'université avec une équipe de plus de 70 agents et une équipe d'appui de 30 étudiant·es.

Il coordonne les moyens correspondants et évalue les services offerts aux usagers. Cette responsabilité se décline en différentes missions : accueil des publics, acquisitions, signalement et communication des ressources documentaires, développement du numérique et de la contribution à l’information scientifique et technique, participation à la valorisation, favoriser la formation initiale et continue ainsi que la recherche, coopération avec les autres établissements et formation des utilisateurs.

L’accès à la bibliothèque et la consultation sur place de documents sont libres et
gratuits. L’accès à certains services ou collections nécessite une inscription préalable.

Name: BookHub
Description:
At BookHub, we believe books should find people — not dust. We’re a two-teen-led initiative that started with one simple realization: as avid readers, our shelves were growing, but the books just sat there after one read. Meanwhile, so many people lacked access to affordable books or had to buy new ones every time.

This doesn’t just limit learning — it impacts the environment, too.

Our solution? A simple platform where anyone can list their pre-loved books and new readers can buy them at low prices. Whether you're decluttering your shelf or hunting for your next read, BookHub makes it as easy as 1-2-3-Search, find, reserve.

But BookHub isn’t just a marketplace. It’s a movement — one that gives books a second life, reduces waste, encourages reading, and brings communities closer through shared stories.

Founded by two aspiring teens, Vedant Agrawal and Arav Jain, BookHub is a nonprofit built for a purpose — to help you read… hopefully more than just this About Us page.

Join the movement. Spread the word. Let the love of reading grow!

Name: Lavender Menace Queer Books Archive
Description:
Lavender Menace is a Community Interest Company established to promote and benefit the lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, non-binary, intersectional (LGBT+) community and their allies. 
To collect a physical archive of queer and LGBT+ books, especially those Lavender Menace Bookshop sold and those which are out of print, in order to keep them alive for readers in a way the publishing industry’s business model makes it difficult to do.
To create a database of these and other books, both older and newer.
To make this database interactive on a website with reviews and a blog about queer and LGBT+ books (those by or about queer and LGBT+ people or involved with queer and LGBT+ history).
To take part in or organise events such as meetings about queer and LGBT+ writing, history and theatre, and run bookstalls, in order to promote queer and LGBT+ writing and bookselling.
To highlight the collection’s books and (newer queer books) in the archive blog, and on social media such as Facebook, Twitter and Instagram, in order to encourage others to read them and write about them.
To collect ephemera associated with queer bookselling, especially in Scotland.
To build links with other queer and feminist archives, and heritage groups such as LGBT History Month, in order to promote dialogue and learn about new developments.
To raise funds to support the archive through grants, crowdfunding donations and sales of queer book-themed products..
To encourage donations of queer books from individuals and organisations.
To seek partnerships with institutions to secure the archive’s future as a space for the books, a research facility, and a forum for discussion and events around queer writing

Name: BiblioNext
Description:
De toekomst van uw bibliotheek begint vandaag!
 
De toekomst is Next: open, innovatief en betaalbaar.
Next is hét nieuwe bibliotheeksysteem van Nederland en is gebouwd op basis van KOHA. Dit open source systeem is door ons volledig aangepast aan de eisen van de Nederlandse bibliotheek en integreert met alle bestaande platformen, zoals de Online Bibliotheek, IBLV en het landelijke Datawarehouse. Is er een koppeling die nog niet bestaat? Dan bouwen wij die voor u! 
Next is zeer gebruiksvriendelijk: alle functionaliteiten zijn intuïtief en gemakkelijk bereikbaar. Bovendien bespaart u met Next flink in de kosten, waardoor u ruimte overhoudt in de begroting voor bibliotheektaken die écht het verschil maken. Én aangezien Next gebasserd is op open source software is in de meeste gevallen een Europese aanbesteding niet nodig.

Name: Florida Association of College & Research Libraries
Description:
The Florida Association of College and Research Libraries (FACRL) is the Florida state chapter of the American Library Association's (ALA) division of the Association of College and Research Libraries (ACRL).

FACRL strives to provide opportunities for professional development and networking while promoting the exchange of ideas and information.  FACRL is open to librarians from all types of academic libraries in Florida, including public and private colleges and universities, as well as specialized research libraries.

Name: Sewickley Public Library
Description:
A community and cultural resource since 1873, the Sewickley Public Library has been established to provide free quality service to residents of the Quaker Valley School District, Allegheny County residents, and qualified non-residents.

The Library offers a variety of materials and services to meet your educational, informational, and recreational needs. Owning over 90,000 titles - housing an ever-growing collection of fiction and non-fiction books, DVDs, CDs, audio books, magazines, and digital formats - the Library also has access to the materials of all other participating Allegheny County Library Association libraries to provide you with as much information as possible.

Name: Waukesha Public Library
Description:
The Waukesha Public Library is a 71,566 square foot facility located in picturesque Cutler Park in the geographic center of the city of Waukesha, Wisconsin. Established in 1896, the Library was the 16th Andrew Carnegie-funded institution in Wisconsin in 1903; five additions and renovations followed, with the most recent in 1988, 2005, 2010, and 2022.

Our Vision
The heart of a thriving city, the library connects and inspires the community to transform lives.

Our Mission
Waukesha Public Library champions the power of reading and learning to enrich, empower and engage all in our community.

Name: Bibliotheek Het Markiezaat (opgegaan in Bibliotheek West Brabant)
Description: Public library, communities involved are Bergen op Zoom, Woensdrecht en Steenbergen.

Name: Hinsdale Public Library
Description: The Hinsdale Public Library is a comfortable and inviting place to visit. Located right next to the Village Hall, north of Burlington Park, the Library is open 7 days a week, and online services are available 24/7. The Library offers free study rooms and meeting rooms, a spacious Quiet Reading Room, an interactive children’s area, and plenty of comfortable seating for reading or working. The Library also houses a fine art collection, featuring the work of prominent Illinois artists. Our service desks are staffed by knowledgeable professionals, offering prompt assistance using the latest resources.

Name: Exploleer - Grupo Animando a Leer
Description:
Grupo Animando a Leer
Somos un grupo de profesionales que apostamos por la lectura como el medio más efectivo para mejorar la calidad de vida y lograr la superación personal. De ahí que nuestro interés sea actualizar a quienes poseen experiencia en el tema del fomento de la lectura y formar promotores que tengan las bases para colaborar en esta gran tarea. Llevamos más de 15 años en el ámbito de la educación social, especializados en promocionar la lectura. Antes, lo hacíamos solo presencialmente. Actualmente contamos con tres proyectos virtuales en ejecución: Exploleer [ www.exploleer.com ], Diplomado Iberoamericano como Promotor de Lectura [ www.animandoaleer.com/tallervirtual.htm], blog:  Animación a la Lectura [ http://animacionlecturas.blogspot.com] y Golectura (https://www.golectura.com)

Name: The Little Library That Could
Description:
Our mission is to connect bilingual children living in Istanbul with a diverse collection of literature, bringing them hard-to-find resources and knowledge. 

The authentic bricks-and-mortar library experience teaches children responsibility, and gives them an inspirational alternative to screen-time, which has unfortunately become the norm in our post-COVID-19 world. 

Our broad collection of books (English & Türkçe, also Deutsch, Español, فارسی, Français, Italiano, русский) will bring the joy of reading to readers of all ages, and our cozy space already boasts over 7,000 titles, and is growing day-by-day.

Name: Missoula Public Library
Description: Missoula Public Library is a libraries company based out of 455 E MAIN ST, Missoula, Montana, United States.

Name: East Gwillimbury Public Library
Description: East Gwillimbury Public Library (www.egpl.ca) serves a growing community in the northern part of York Region, made up of a mix of urban areas and villages. We value creativity and pride ourselves on providing excellent customer and employee experiences with a mission to ignite our community through literacy, discovery, and connection. Branches are in the communities of Holland Landing and Mount Albert, with a third location opening in 2025.

Name: Cassidy Cataloguing Services, Inc.
Description: We are a quality-driven company producing MARC catalog records for publishers, firm libraries, state, court, and county libraries, and the law school community. We work expertly with MARC data manipulation, law library technical services consulting, Shelf-Ready processing, and PCIP - Publishers' Cataloging-in-Publication.

Name: American Corner Belgrade
Description:
American Corner Belgrade is a partnership between the U.S. Embassy in Serbia, the Belgrade City Library and Belgrade Youth Center. Our mission is to provide free access to current and reliable information about cultural, educational, economic and political life in the United States and to foster mutual understanding between Serbia and the United States through information services and ongoing activities.
Join our online programs and courses, meet career field specialists and native speakers, learn, share and discuss.

Name: SZTE Klebelsberg Library and Archives
Description:
Klebelsberg Library is the central library for all the faculties and schools of the University of Szeged,  or Szegedi Tudományegyetem in Hungarian, or SZTE in short. 
We provide traditional library services as well as new opportunities mainly to our target clientele: students (bachelor, master and graduate levels) and instructors/researchers of the University of Szeged.

Name: Sojourner Truth Library
Description:
The Library plays a critical leadership role in supporting and advancing a user-centered learning environment at SUNY New Paltz. As a team of courteous and knowledgeable librarians and staff, we treat users with respect and empathy at all times and serve all members of the academic community equitably, whether they are in the library or at a distance.

We promote and encourage the active and effective use of all library services and resources. We continuously expand information access and delivery by employing state-of-the-art technologies. We successfully instill information literacy skills in all New Paltz graduates through strong collaborative partnerships with classroom faculty. We provide a spacious, well-designed facility that offers ample and varied seating, lighting, and workplace options.

We work together cooperatively and pursue our professional growth to meet the challenges faced by contemporary academic libraries. We regularly assess the library and information needs of faculty and students across campus in order to meet these needs and the changing requirements of new academic programs.

VALUES:
Communication - We regularly publicize library services and resources regularly to the college community and share information with library colleagues.

Approachability - We carry out our responsibilities with a welcoming attitude.
Organization - We organize and maintain our facility, collections and systems to ensure ease of access.

Cooperation - We build and maintain cooperative working relationships with the college community and with one another to achieve library's mission and goals.
Respect - We treat all library users and one another with respect.
Excellence - We strive to provide the best information services and resources for all users.

Name: Gratefulbookworm
Description: Start attracting money, health and love in next 24 hours.

Name: Joint Council of Librarians of Color Inc
Bibliolima
Description: BiblioLima nació como un proyecto de investigación que buscaba estudiar las demandas, opiniones y perfiles de los usuarios bibliotecarios de seis bibliotecas municipales de Lima. Tras varios años y una pandemia de por medio, hoy queremos compartir los resultados científicos con el propósito de crear conciencia social sobre la problemática y empoderar a las bibliotecas públicas de nuestra ciudad. Si estás interesado, por favor, síguenos en Ig y Tiktok como @BiblioLima para ver más contenido!!

Name: Port Moody Public Library
Description:
Our mission is to connect people with ideas and information, to inspire imagination and a love of reading, and to facilitate lifelong learning and discovery.

Our vision is to be a welcoming, open and free community gathering place where all can reflect, learn, create, share, and debate.

Name: Ridgewood Public Library
Description: The Ridgewood Public Library serves all ages as the Village center for childhood literacy and lifelong learning.  Our expert staff welcomes you with books, cultural events, expert lectures, discussion groups, technology access and training, local history & genealogy, and multicultural services including ESL and book collections in numerous languages.

Name: Ascension Parish Library
Description:
Ascension Parish Library is an award-winning library system that offers traditional library services along with the newest, most innovative library resources relevant in this electronic age. These resources include 24/7 access to ebooks, audiobooks, movies, magazines, and more through the library’s online catalog and mobile apps.

We invite you to be a part of our ever-growing, always learning, Ascension Parish Library family. With five locations and a bookmobile, we’re in your neighborhood. Come in for a visit, meet our friendly library staff, and get your free library card today!

Name: Mead Public Library
Description: We connect people with ideas, resources and technology to educate, entertain and empower.

Name: Empower Library Skills Collective
Description:
Empower: The Library Skills Collective is a professional development platform created to help library staff at every level develop future-ready skills—without burning out.

We offer live and on-demand library training, expert-led workshops, and practical, inclusive learning pathways tailored specifically for library and information professionals. Whether you're just starting out, managing a team, or leading change in your library, Empower supports your professional growth, confidence, and wellbeing.

We believe:

Library skills are transferable skills that matter in every sector.
People-first training transforms teams and services.
Empowered library workers create stronger, more resilient libraries.

Join Empower and gain access to:

High-quality training for library professionals
Courses and webinars led by sector experts and guest specialists
A supportive community of librarians and information workers
Tools to apply learning in real-world library settings

From customer service and accessibility to leadership and project management, Empower equips you with the skills and support to thrive in today’s library world.

Explore our training: empowerlibraryskills.com
Collaborate or bring Empower to your organisation: support@empowerlibraryskills.com

Name: Library Development Forum (LDF)
Description:
A well developed society can not be imagined without the development of library . it directly helps in quality education, to bring positive social changes and agricultural/economic/development. Library is a reliable source of information for the community. It has an important role to play for sustainable development, protection of human rights, peace and community friendship and conflict management. It also helps and supports for self employment and life-time studies. In other words a library is a life-time school. Library also can play an important role as community development, resource and local research center.

An effort is made to establish Library Development Forum in view to take some institutional steps to develop, diversify and safeguard libraries in Nepal. Specially, the library will be working in coordination with the public and community libraries and other types of libraries.

The Library Development Forum does not only serve, it also works in bridging the gap between public and community libraries. It will play a vital role as a common mechanism, thereby contributing, diversification and safeguarding of all types of libraries. Mostly, our target areas will be rural areas. Our aim is to work on the basis of the theme, “One Village, One Library and One Family one member”. The library targeted to establish a well-equipped and with well-stocked books with modern facilities such as online library.

Name: Lane Medical Library
Description:
The mission of Lane Medical Library & Knowledge Management Center is to enable biomedical discovery by connecting people with knowledge. Through innovative means, we acquire, deliver, create, and integrate information to promote excellence in research, education, and patient care.

Our vision is to be global leaders in connecting people with biomedical knowledge.

Name: QM InterLaw Council
Description:
The QMUL InterLaw Council is an initiative of the QMUL School of Law and its affiliated societies, designed to maximise the efficiency of society sponsored events through partnerships and collaboration.

WHAT DO WE DO?

The InterLaw Council works closely with QMUL Law Societies and the School of Law to provide students with a reliable source of all QMUL law news by law students for law students. Through its newsletter as well as Instagram and Facebook pages, Interlaw is committed to ensuring students are aware of the latest events and opportunities available to them. Help them plan ahead and get involved. 

InterLaw's weekly LEGAL newsletter is the only platform that provides an overview of all law society events and advertise brand ambassadors.

WHO WE ARE.

Secretary Generals: Roxana-Daniela Toderascu, Ananya Mukherjee
Jr. Secretary Generals: Anna Viallefond, Sanya Chawla, Vedant Trivedi


CONNECT WITH US.

Interlaw.council@gmail.com
Instagram: QMUL Interlaw Council


GUIDELINES FOR PROMOTION. 

If you wish to inform students about an event or an opportunity, you can send an email to the InterLaw Council and we will directly liaise with the Undergraduate Law office to promote the event on your behalf. 

We only send out messages relating to undergraduate law students. 

We require at least a week notice period.

Name: BiblioConnect
Description:
O projeto Biblioconnect nasce com o intuito de promover discussões, bem como propor e divulgar conteúdos na área de biblioteconomia, Ciência da Informação, Arquivologia e Museologia. É composto por um grupo de bibliotecárixs de Mato Grosso do Sul e pretende, dessa forma, tornar mais conhecida a biblioteconomia do MS.

Explore, read, grow!

Name: Jones Library
Description:
We are public libraries serving Amherst, Massachusetts.

We offer library materials, programming, informational resources, access to technology, and much more. Our Special Collections include local history and genealogy materials, as well as manuscript collections featuring Emily Dickinson and Robert Frost. Our ESL program provides volunteer tutors, study materials, English and citizenship classes, and other services to adult immigrants in the Amherst area. Our branch libraries serve the residents of North Amherst and South Amherst.

We believe in the freedom to read, to learn, to discover, to change your life.

Name: Murrieta Public Library
Description: As a team with civic heart, the Murrieta Public Library is dedicated to providing a vibrant environment with open access to exceptional services, collections, and programs while continuing to promote the joy of reading for an empowered and successful community.

Name: Public Libraries In Jefferson County
Description: Public Libraries In Jefferson County is the name for the consortium of public libraries in Jefferson County. The administrative office of the organization is housed at Birmingham Public Central Library. There has been some type of countywide public library service since 1924; however, it was in 1978 that no-fee service for all citizens of Jefferson County was established. The organization was restructured as Birmingham Area Library Services in 1985 under the guidelines for systems issued by the Alabama Public Library Service. Renamed the Jefferson County Library Cooperative in 1997, the organization continues under its formal establishment as a not-for-profit educational organization. This enables JCLC to apply for grants, and to enter into joint purchasing agreements on behalf of its member public libraries. The Internal Revenue Service recognizes the organization as a 501(c)(3) tax-exempt educational organization. JCLC revenue is derived from State Aid to Public Libraries, the Jefferson County Commission, and fees for cooperative services paid by member libraries.

Name: Bibliotecas USIL
Description:
El Sistema de Bibliotecas USIL es un espacio dinámico de integración de recursos físicos y electrónicos, destinados al aprendizaje y la investigación, que contribuye a cumplir con la misión de la Universidad en “formar profesionales competentes y emprendedores, con responsabilidad social y con plena capacidad para desenvolverse nacional e internacionalmente”.
Por ello, gestionamos información con el fin de desarrollar habilidades informativas en nuestros usuarios.

Die Württembergische Landesbibliothek (WLB) ist die größte wissenschaftliche Bibliothek Baden-Württembergs.
Die WLB ist ein lebendiger Ort für Kultur und Wissenschaft. Sie bietet ein vielseitiges Veranstaltungsprogramm und zeigt mehrere Ausstellungen im Jahr.

Name: Music Library of Greece "Lilian Voudouri" - Μουσική Βιβλιοθήκη «Λίλιαν Βουδούρη»
Description:
The premises and resources of the Library are freely accessible to the public.

The Library is located at the Athens Concert Hall (Megaron Mousikes) and can be reached from the entrances of the building on Vas. Sofias Avenue or on Kokkali St.

The Concert Hall is situated at the corner of Vas. Sofias Avenue and Kokkali St.

You can get here by public transport from any part of Athens:
• By metro, it’s just two minutes from the Concert Hall Metro Station to the door of the Concert Hall
• By bus, buses Ε14, Α5 and 550 stop right in front of the Concert Hall
• By electrically driven bus, buses 10 and 3 stop right in front of the Concert Hall

Visitors to Athens can easily find the Concert Hall:
• From the airport you can take bus Χ95, or a 35 minute ride on the Metro will bring you to the Concert Hall
• From the railway station, it’s just 30 minutes to the Concert Hall Metro station
• From Piraeus it’s just a few minutes ride on the Metro.

If you come by car you can park in the Concert Hall’s new three-level parking facility, which has spaces for 750 cars. The entrance is on Kokkali St. and the facility is open from 6am to 2am every day. For more information, call 210 7282311.

Library is open on: Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday: 10:00-16:45
Wednesday: 10:00-19:00

It is closed at weekends, on public holidays, and during a certain time in August.
Closing dates are announced in time.

https://g.page/MusicLibraryGreece

Address: Vas. Sofias & Kokkali 1, Athens 11521
Tel.: +30 210 728 2778
Fax: +30 210 725 9196
Website: http://www.mmb.org.gr
email: library@megaron.gr

Name: Bibliotheek Katwijk
Description: Bij Bibliotheek Katwijk geloven wij dat iedereen een plaats in de samenleving verdient en staan we voor een bibliotheek waar je je kunt blijven ontwikkelen. Name: Media Archive for Central England
Description:
The Media Archive for Central England (MACE) is the screen archive for the East and West Midlands.

An independent company and registered charity, MACE preserve the moving image heritage of the midlands and provide affordable public access via their website and a range of community, education, not for profit and commercial projects.

MACE does this by selecting and acquiring, researching, cataloguing, curating and developing a collection of moving images and related materials which inform our understanding of the history and culture of the midlands and working to ensure that this collection is accessible both permanently and widely through the application of our preservation and curatorial skills.

MACE is a not-for-profit organisation providing, public repository services, professional advice on the care and preservation of this heritage (film, video and digital materials), support for community-based events and support for collection-related research

MACE are also specialists in the production of archive film DVD compilations and have recently won a FOCAL International Award for the Best Use of Footage in a Home Entertainment Release for 'From Headlines to 'Tight-Lines' - The Story of ATV Today. 

www.macearchive.org

Name: HTD Read & Lead
Description: Book Library, Book Subscription Service, Renting Books, Book Cafe, Readers' Lounge

Name: Bartlett Public Library District
Description: The Bartlett Public Library District plays an essential role in our community.  We offer access to eSources, downloadable books, music and periodicals and, more importantly, we work with our patrons to make sure they are familiar with all library services and how to use them.  The Library provides a friendly environment for group study and computer usage, and strives to create a welcoming environment for all.  The Bartlett Public Library District Board and staff go above and beyond to meet our patrons’ needs, and make sure that the Bartlett Library truly is the Best Place to Learn and Discover.

Name: Library and Information Association of Zambia (LIAZ)

This helps members to significantly contribute to the enhancement of the library and Information landscape in Zambia and to a greater extent, national development

Name: Biblioteca Județeană ”Octavian Goga” - Cluj County Libray
Description: County and city library in the same time, “Octavian Goga” Cluj County Library is a modern public organization which provides free access to information and knowledge, and also equal chances to education for all the citizens. At the new headquarters building and at its three branches are provided miscellaneous services and facilities: over 750,000 documents, on-line catalogue, reference services, children’s department, adults lending department, Community Information Center, American Corner, NATO Public Information Center, internet access, bookmobile, on-line radio, services for the disabled, voluntary activities. From 1995, it is partner and country coordinator in European projects (PLDP, LIBER-IMMS, ENTITLE etc.). The partnership with 63 local organizations vouches for its involvement in the community life. In 2006 a certificate for the quality management system (ISO 9001:2000) was obtained, our library being the first Romanian cultural public institution in possession of this certificate.

Name: Le Vilebrequin
Description:
Depuis juin 2022, au Vilebrequin, on y vient pour louer des objets (la palette est large : boule disco, tronçonneuse, fer à bricelets, et bien plus encore) de même sorte qu'on va à la ludothèque pour louer des jouets. Mais ici, on y vient également pour participer à différents cours animés par des professionnels. Et très bientôt, on y viendra pour bricoler et créer ensemble ou de manière autonome grâce à l'ouverture du FabLab.

En participant et en soutenant les activités du Vilebrequin, vous contribuez à répondre à l’urgence climatique et vous participez à une économie davantage circulaire.

Name: International Library Services PIKinc
Description:
We provide exceptional technology services to a wide range of libraries and the patrons they serve. Let our innovative solutions bring your library to the leading edge. We strive to do things that others don't even dream of and we'll settle for nothing less. 

Having been in the business for over 30 years, we live in the library industry every day. We don’t take third party products and try to shoehorn them into half-baked solutions. We start from the ground up and ask the questions that need to be asked to ensure a positive result. This guides our development process as we define the best balance of form and function for every product we create. Sometimes we can use an existing platform as a base, but only if we have the flexibility to enhance the usability to match the needs of our customers and their patrons. You can rest assured knowing that every PIKinc. solution starts with a healthy dose of innovation. We see beyond the traditional barriers that limit other companies. We strive to do things that others don’t even dream of. Our team will settle for nothing less.

Name: The Free Books Project
Description:
We bring books where libraries don’t and give them away!
 It’s our mission to improve access to literacy and connection for everyone—especially members of our community in marginalized or vulnerable areas. Through our various programs, we distribute books of all genres for all ages.
We can always use more Pop Up Librarians; please send us a message if you're interested in managing your own Pop Up Library or Book Ark.

Name: Michigan State University Libraries
Description: The MSU Libraries facilitate connections that support research, teaching, and learning in our local and global communities. The Libraries promote equal access to information and spaces for all. We lead meaningful initiatives in accessibility, diversity, equity, and inclusion, and we work in a culture of transparency, experimentation, and growth.

Name: Woodbridge Public Library
Description: The Woodbridge Public Library has always strived to provide the best quality of information service to the citizens of Woodbridge Township.

We celebrate Guernsey's unique identity with our Guernsey Photographic Archive, the finest collection of local newspapers in the Channel Islands, and a comprehensive collection of books and documents on Guernsey's language, history, and culture.
