Name: Beverly Public Library
Description:
The Beverly Public Library was established in 1855, three years after the Massachusetts Legislature became the first in the nation to authorize cities and towns to expend tax funds to support free public libraries. The Library was the successor to the Social Library, a private subscription library which traced its founding to a collection of books seized by Beverly privateers from a British merchantman during the Revolutionary War. Elizabeth P. Sohier, a trustee of the Beverly Public Library, led the fight to establish the first state library agency in the country, and served as the State Library Commission’s first secretary.

The Library first operated from rooms in City Hall. In 1913, the Library moved to its current building on Essex Street. The building was designed by architect Cass Gilbert in the Beaux Arts style. Gilbert was also the architect of the Minnesota State Capitol, the Woolworth Building in New York City and the United States Supreme Court. The Library was renovated and enlarged in 1993.

The Beverly Farms Branch was established in 1886 and moved into its current building in 1916. Bookmobile service was established in 1959. In 1981, the Beverly Public Library joined four other communities in establishing the first shared library computer system in Massachusetts.

Today the Library loans over 280,000 items annually to almost 27,000 regular borrowers. The Main Library collection consists of over 125,000 books and the Beverly Farms Branch of 22,000 books. In addition, the Library loans magazines, large print books, audiobooks, DVDs, ebooks, and admission passes to selected local museums. The Library provides reference materials from electronic databases, an online shared catalog containing over 1,000,000 titles, an online periodical index and full text service of 1,500 magazine titles. The Library further enriches the life of the Beverly community with its art gallery, lectures, concerts, story hours, book discussion groups, and exhibits.

Name: Guille-Allès Library
Description:
The Guille-Allès Library has been at the heart of life in Guernsey for 140 years. Founded by lifelong friends Thomas Guille and Frederick Allès in 1882, their original vision was for the Library to become a hub for culture, learning, and community – and well over a century later, our goal remains the same.

Through our vast collection of books, films, music, digital resources and other media, as well as frequent events for adults and children, we enrich lives and boost wellbeing across the community, with equality of access for all.

Name: Scarsdale Public Library
Description: Scarsdale's community hub, welcoming every patron and community member. Through our expansive collections and innovative engaging programming, the Scarsdale Public Library serves our mission to encourage the joy of reading, the exploration of ideas, and the pursuit of lifelong learning in children and adults.

Name: UTHSC Health Sciences Library
Description: The mission of the Health Sciences Library is to provide an environment conducive to student learning and the biomedical information resources necessary for teaching, research, service, and patient care, and to support efforts to improve the health of Tennesseans.

Name: Cass County Public Library
Description: A public library district serving Cass County, Missouri with branches in Belton, Harrisonville, Pleasant Hill, Archie, Garden City, and Drexel. The library also operates a Genealogy Branch in Harrisonville and a Bookmobile. Our mission is to provide informational, educational, and enrichment services and resources to the community through a comprehensive and efficient library system.

Name: Lakeland Library Cooperative
Description: Our mission is to strengthen our member libraries covering eight west Michigan counties by providing the means to share resources, services and expertise for the benefit of individuals and communities.

Name: Avon Lake Public Library
Description: The public library serving the city and school district of Avon Lake, Ohio, and neighbors.  Past and present employees can connect here.   Fast facts:  population served = 24,000 circulation = 400,000 items annually visitors 275,000 annually, 130,000 items in collection.  We have one location and we are not part of any consortia save the Ohio Digital Library for ebooks.  We are an OCLC lender and supplier.

Name: Wright Memorial Public Library
Description: Public Library located in Oakwood, Ohio

Name: Rolling Meadows Library
Description: The Rolling Meadows Library is a community center for learning, culture, and recreation. Established in 1959, the library owns over 211,000 items, circulates over 400,000 items annually, and averages 20,000 visits per month. We are a member of the Reaching Across Illinois Library System (RAILS). The Library's website connects, with direct links, to reference databases, book suggestions, programs for children and adults, our Kids Page, Rolling Meadows Historical File online, Business and Community Profiles, Investment Help, Encyclopedias, and other informational sites.

Name: Bibliotheek Almelo
Description: Bibliotheek Almelo is a company based out of Netherlands.

Name: Philadelphia Consortium of Special Collections Libraries, Inc.
Description: PHILADELPHIA AREA CONSORTIUM OF SPECIAL COLLECTIONS LIBRARIES INC is a libraries company based out of 2014 CHRISTIAN ST, PHILADELPHIA, Pennsylvania, United States.

Name: Calcasieu Parish Public Library (company page)
Description:
Thanks for visiting the Calcasieu Parish Public Library's LinkedIn company page. Our profile page, https://www.linkedin.com/in/calcasieuparishpubliclibrary, has a spirited tone, so get the best of both worlds by connecting to both pages. 
The Calcasieu Parish Public Library serves all the people who live in the parish with materials, information, and services through a network of branches that are conveniently located and easy to use. The library strives to help people make informed decisions, enjoy their free time, and continue learning all of their lives. The Library Board and Staff are committed to providing high quality, cost effective, equitable service that meets the needs of all parish residents.

Name: Califa Group
Description:
Our Mission: To provide cost effective delivery of services, programs, and products through a membership network of  libraries.

Califa Group is a nonprofit library membership consortium of more than 230 libraries and is the largest library network in California. Founded in 2004, Califa brokers and facilitates the procurement of library products; works closely with the California State Library in administering a number of statewide projects supported by federal Library Services and Technology Act funds, such as the enki Library ebook platform; offers Continuing Education training through the Infopeople project, and manages master contracts and pricing agreements with publishers and vendors. With years of experience and a network of professional contacts, Califa has demonstrated capacity and experience in grant program design, delivery, and management.

Our membership includes all sizes and types of libraries – academic, research, public, school, corporate, medical, law, and special. A Board of Directors, elected by and from the membership, governs Califa, which is based in San Francisco, CA.

Name: None
Description: C

Name: livre architecture btp
Description: La page s'intéresse à tout ce qui concerne le génie civil, le spécialiste technique et technique en construction

Name: Effat Library & Cultural Museum
Description: Welcome to our library, a dynamic hub of learning and innovation inspired by Queen Effat’s visionary legacy. As the founding mother of education in KSA, she championed holistic learning rooted in Islamic values and global collaboration. We honor her mission by offering exceptional access to knowledge, cutting-edge services, and vibrant spaces for creativity, cultural exchange, and intellectual growth. Join us in celebrating her enduring impact through academic programs, cultural events, and a commitment to lifelong learning. Follow us for updates that inspire and empower!

Name: Koios
Description: Koios promotes libraries by bringing search traffic to their websites and online resources. Using Google’s Ad Grants for Nonprofits program, Koios helps libraries reach outside their existing patron base to bring in new people — people searching online with Google.

Name: Clayton County Library System
Description: Clayton County Library System is a 7 branch public library system founded in Jonesboro, GA. The Clayton County Library System strives to contribute to the success of the citizens of our diverse community by offering a full range of library services that meet their informational, educational and leisure interests, fostering the love of reading in our youth and the lifelong pursuit of knowledge for all.

Name: Eastern Oklahoma Library System
Description:
The Eastern Oklahoma Library System is a multi-county organization providing public library service through fifteen libraries in six counties.

The roots of this service date back to the early 1900s as regional community groups came together to organize book collections and reading groups. These early efforts grew into local libraries, most of which struggled to serve their communities with limited funds.

The Oklahoma Library Systems Act of 1967 allowed libraries to work together to improve library services and resources by establishing library systems. Starting in Muskogee County, the Eastern Oklahoma Library System radiated out to include Adair, Cherokee, Delaware, McIntosh and Sequoyah counties.

The Eastern Oklahoma Library System continues to grow and adapt to meet the changing information needs of the communities that it serves.

Name: Bloomingdale Public Library
Description: Bloomingdale Public Library serves the village of Bloomingdale, IL, located approximately 28 miles northwest of Chicago.  The Bloomingdale Public Library has a rich history of progressive thinking and community involvement. From 30 years ago when the Library opened in two mobile classrooms to today's impressive state-of-the-art facility, the residents of Bloomingdale have been instrumental in improving Library services. With financial support, professional development, and time commitments, the Bloomingdale Public Library has evolved to a staff of over 60, providing a variety of services to patrons 68 hours each week in its 35,000 square foot facility.

Name: Livingston Parish Library
Description:
Livingston Parish Library was legally established by the Police Jury in 1946 as a demonstration library of the Louisiana State Library. The first library was located in the Livingston Parish Courthouse and opened its doors December 16, 1946. The first Parish Librarian was Annie S. Cowart with Louise Risley as Assistant. Both were graduates of the Louisiana State University School of Library Science.

Several Directors have served since 1946, including Mrs. Cowart, Willie Mae Seab, Odile Gill, Betty Dance, Marcia Perkins, Iva Ginn, J. G. Sibley, Austin Higginbotham, Eula Fontenot, Odelia Salassi, Allen Cunningham, and Wendy Bobo. Giovanni Tairov is the current director.

The current Library Board of Control completed a major building program for the library system, authorizing the construction of four new facilities to serve as regional branches in 2005. Those new facilities, completed in 2007, continue to see a tremendous amount of usage. Some branches have up to 900 visitors on a busy day.

The following branches are in operation today: Livingston Branch (Main), Denham Springs-Walker, Watson, Albany-Springfield, and South.

Much of the success and growth of the Livingston Parish Library system can be attributed to retired director Allen Cunningham and retired assistant director Alex Kropog as well as the Library Board of Control for their dedication to providing quality services and programming to the people of Livingston Parish. Along with the current Library Board, the current director and assistant director are working to continue that dedication to the growth and improvement of the public library system. The success of the Livingston Parish Library is directly connected to the citizens of Livingston Parish who, through their continued support of the library system as it grows and develops, have helped to make it into an award-winning system.

The Livingston Parish Library Board, administration and staff would like to say thank you to all of the people of Livingston Parish for their love and support of libraries, reading and learning.

Name: The Floyd County Library
Description: The mission of the Floyd County Library is to support the growth and creativity of an engaged, informed, and connected community.

Name: CTBHeritage Consulting Ltd.
Description:
CTBHeritage Consulting is run by Catt Thompson-Baum ACR, an Institute of Conservation (ICON) accredited conservator.  She has many years experience both in institutions such as the British Library, the Wellcome Library, Trinity College Dublin and The National Archives of England and Wales, as well as in private practise working with leading archive collections and academic and genealogical publishers.

Catt is one of the UK's leading experts in digitisation, ranging from small projects to mass digitisation of tens of thousands of documents.  Her specialisms, including collection surveys, assessment and treatment, workflows, team and project management, preservation advice, can be applied to any heritage project.


Between October 2017 and December 2023 she was  the Collection Care Consultant and Project Manager for Wiley Digital Archives.  She has worked on projects with Conservation by Design, the Commonwealth War Graves Committee and was Senior Collection Care Consultant for Find My Past for the digitisation of the 1921 Census. 

Since April 2024 she has worked part-time with the National Conservation Service as maternity cover for their Lead Preventive Conservator, with responsibility for members' environmental and pest monitoring programmes, exhibition install and de-installations and supporting many other aspects of their work such as surveys and due diligence checks. 

She is the author of "Large-Scale Digitization at The National Archives", Chapter 4 of "Book Conservation and Digitization: The Challenges of Dialogue and Collaboration" (ARC Humanities Press)
CTBHeritage Consulting Ltd.

https://www.wiley.com/en-us/network/research-libraries/libraries-archives-databases/archives/preserving-the-past-a-q-a-with-conservator-catt-thompson-baum

Wiley/Royal College of Physicians digitisation of William Caxton volumes: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3wuWh1haUHM

Name: Bookfeeding Project
Description:
Bookfeeding Project is an NGO aimed at establishing safe educational hubs and libraries in disadvantaged communities worldwide.
We develop our libraries in cooperation with local communities and organizations as part of already existing or independent projects. We aim to create self-sustainable and skills-sharing centers that promote human rights, empower young girls and women and support disadvantaged groups in the community. Our aim is to build centers that allow the community to grow and find new ways to improve their livelihoods.
Bookfeeding Project is registered in Germany (VR16809) and in OSCR Scottish Charity Registry (SC046248).

Name: Rochester Regional Library Council
Description:
The Rochester Regional Library Council is a multi-type library network serving libraries and library systems in the five-county Rochester region - Livingston, Monroe, Ontario, Wayne and Wyoming counties. It is one of nine regional library councils in the state of New York.

RRLC members include all types of libraries: academic, public, school (K-12), corporate, non-profit, and medical.

RRLC provides programs and services that enhance member library cooperation, service delivery and resource sharing. RRLC serves as a forum for the unified representation of library interests and helps members achieve together what could not be achieved separately.

Name: South Brunswick Public Library
Description: A public Library serving the South Brunswick Community

Name: Waterford City Council Library Service
Description: Waterford City Council Library Service consists of 3 branch libraries and a Library HQ, all based in Waterford City, Ireland. Branches are in Ardkeen Library on the Dunmore Road, Brown's Road Library and Central Library Lady, Lane in the City Centre. The HQ is in the Northern Extension Business Park, Old Kilmeaden Road, Waterford

Name: Woodridge Public Library
Description:
The Woodridge Public Library serves Woodridge, Illinois and surrounding communities by providing opportunities to learn, explore, and enjoy. Woodridge Public Library is a dynamic leader and valued partner in the community.
We are the center for innovation, lifelong learning and enrichment, and vibrant and customer-focused service.

Name: Westfield Memorial Library
Description: The Westfield Memorial Library serves the residents of Westfield, New Jersey. It is an active municipal public library with over 200,000 annual visitors who borrow 375,000 items, ask more than 10,000 reference questions, and attend more than 700 annual programs and events.

Name: Maakplaats 021
Description:
Technologie speelt een steeds grotere rol in het dagelijks leven van ons allemaal. We zijn er zelfs afhankelijk van geworden. En tegelijkertijd snappen we er steeds minder van. Om de razendsnelle ontwikkelingen de baas te blijven is het noodzakelijk de jonge generatie te laten spelen met technologie.

Maakplaats 021 is een netwerk van openbare makerspaces in de stad. Het getal 021 staat voor de 21st century skills, vaardigheden die we nodig hebben om succesvol deel te nemen in de veranderende maatschappij. Na schooltijd bieden we workshops voor kinderen en jongeren uit de buurt. Overdag is het een werkruimte voor scholen en creatieven.

Name: EUGENE CENTER FOR ETHNOBOTANICAL STUDIES
Description: EDELIC CENTER FOR ETHNOBOTANICAL SERVICES (formerly known as Eugene Center for Ethnobotanical Studies) is a 501(c)3 grass-roots non-profit educational public community trust promoting global awareness of & local acceptability of organic plant/fungi based ethnobotanicals & psychedelics.

Name: Universitätsbibliothek Heidelberg
Description: Die Universitätsbibliothek Heidelberg ist die zentrale Einrichtung für die Literatur- und Informationsversorgung der Universität Heidelberg. Neben mehr als 6 Millionen gedruckten Beständen, 150.000 elektronischen Zeitschriften, 900.000 E-Books entwickeln wir digitale Services für die Universität und darüber hinaus. Wir unterstützen Open Access und Open Science - den freien Zugang zu Wissenschaft. Ein Schwerpunkt unserer Arbeit liegt auf der Entwicklung von Publikationsdiensten und digitalen Editionen.

Name: Nassau Library System
Description: NLS is one of 23 public library systems in NY State. We are a cooperative library system, meaning that our 54 member libraries are autonomous, supported by local taxes, and governed by their own boards of trustees. We are essentially a consortium. Our service area is Nassau County on Long Island. All public libraries in the county are members of NLS.

Name: St. Thomas Public Library
Description:
Located in the heart of downtown St. Thomas, St. Thomas Public Library is your community hub for free access to technology, collections, services, resources, programs, and much more! As your library, we are committed to redefining what great library service looks like for St. Thomas. 

We hope you’ll stop by and visit us at 153 Curtis Street, St. Thomas, ON, Canada or visit us online at https://stthomaspubliclibrary.ca/ and see how the library can support your journey to lifelong learning.

Find us on:
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/stthomaspubliclibrary
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/stthomaspubliclibrary/
Twitter: https://twitter.com/stpl_library

Name: The Matthew E. Russell Foundation
Description:
Helping to Establish Literacy & Libraries in Rural Areas Around the World.

The purpose of the foundation is to provide an educationally enriched environment where children have access to advanced technology in order to facilitate their interest and passion for higher education and learning.

Name: La Biblioteca de los Libros Felices
Description:
La Biblioteca de los Libros Felices está ubicada en la Sede de Alicante del Colegio Notarial de Valencia.

En ella habitan más de 4.200 libros de los siglos XIX al XV (estos últimos son los llamados incunables) .

Si te apetece visitarla debes solicitarlo desde la página de REGISTRO, las visitas y las actividades son siempre mediante reserva, actualmente tenemos una lista de espera un poco extensa, pero estamos trabajando para agilizar las visitas.

Los grupos son de 9 personas (12 maximo) lo que explica que seguramente hasta dentro de unos meses no podamos contactar contigo para confirmarte la fecha y hora de la visita.

En el momento que recibamos tu solicitud te incluiremos "EN ESPERA" y te avisaremos en cuanto tengamos disponibilidad de recibirte.

La Biblioteca de los Libros Felices te ofrece la oportunidad de organizar tu evento cultural en un espacio diferente ubicado en el centro de Alicante o de acudir a eventos organizados.

Name: RelayRead
Description:
RelayRead has come into being with a mission to make Reading accessible to and affordable for everyone. RelayRead aims to promote book sharing amongst people so that everyone gets to read more by spending much less. RelayRead allows you to freely access books from RelayRead members around the country who want to share their books with others. With RelayRead, you can give out your books to other readers, and get books that are being offered by other readers. 

RelayRead is a great boon for students of all ages, who have to buy expensive textbooks, reference books, and books for exam preparations, and often these books get used for a limited period of time.

Enjoy door-step pick-up and delivery of books on RelayRead!

RelayRead operates in India.

Name: National Rehabilitation Information Center
Description:
National Rehabilitation Information Center (NARIC) is a library and information center focused on disability and rehabilitation research. NARIC is funded by the Administration for Community Living, National Institute on Disability, Independent Living, and Rehabilitation Research (NIDILRR). NARIC maintains a large collection of research literature focusing on disability and rehabilitation, the core of which is made up of NIDILRR-funded work. NARIC's information specialists offer information, referral, and document delivery services in English and Spanish.

NARIC is managed by BlackFish Federal, LLC/HeiTech Services, Inc., under contract number 140D0421C0021. Posting to or linking from this page does not constitute endorsement by NARIC or NIDILRR.

Name: Cabarrus County Public Library
Description: Cabarrus County Public Library is a department of Cabarrus County Government. The library system is composed of five branches in Concord, Harrisburg, Kannapolis, Midland, and Mt. Pleasant, North Carolina.

Name: Watertown Free Public Library
Description: The Watertown Free Public Library extends beyond its walls to connect people to ideas, information, education, creative opportunities, and to each other. While preserving the rich history of Watertown, the Watertown Free Public Library embraces the advancement of library service, inspires personal development, and promotes community. The library provides free recreation, entertainment, and enrichment. It welcomes and serves everyone.

Name: Crystal Lake Public Library
Description: Crystal Lake Public Library serves Crystal Lake, Illinois, a community of more than 40,000 people located in Northern Illinois. The Library is located at 126 Paddock St., Crystal Lake. The library's mission statement is: Helping people, of all ages and backgrounds, learn, enjoy, and better their lives.

Name: Cedar Park Public Library
Description: Cedar Park Public Library, Texas

Name: Your Library Hub
Description: Your Library Hub is a digital library platform in Uganda that was launched in early 2019 as a result of the outbreak of the COVID 19 pandemic. The main aim is: To establish and maintain a network of library users and other stakeholders throughout the country so as to enhance instant access and usage of information to all persons including those with disability. The digital library platform is well equipped and serves library users with books on-demand, electronic journals, electronic magazines, audio, visual, and audio-visual information materials. The platform today serves a number of users both within the country and outside the country.

Name: Biblioteca Suuralairua
Description: La Biblioteca Suuralairua, es un programa sin ánimo de lucro que busca proteger la lengua wayuunaiki al tiempo que persigue los objetivos de desarrollo sostenible relativos a la educación de calidad y vencimiento de la pobreza, Suuralairua busca garantizar y promover la formación de niños y niñas líderes Wayúu a través de la enseñanza en diferentes campos del conocimiento, reduciendo el analfabetismo.

Name: Bienenstock Furniture Library
Description:
Touch history... Design the Future!

Bienenstock Furniture Library, founded 1970, is a worldwide research facility and a center for design & collaboration. Significant among the Library's 5000+ holdings are an international collection of rare books published since 1640 – including complete original works of Chippendale, Sheraton, Hepplewhite, and Diderot's encyclopedia – plus modern international works. The Library operates a specialty bookstore selling significant contemporary books & out-of-print and hard-to-find volumes. Most Library services are free. The Library is open to the public and welcomes professionals & scholars for research, collaboration, meetings, seminars, lectures, and events.

Name: The Library of American Broadcasting
Description:
The mission of the Library of American Broadcasting is to preserve the history of radio and television, reflecting the people, policies, and programming. It is part of the Special Collections division of the University Libraries of the University of Maryland in College Park, Maryland.

The LAB holdings include recordings, books, pamphlets, periodicals, personal collections, oral histories, photographs, scripts, and vertical files. Additionally, there are a variety of resources on broadcast media as a business, with material from corporations and trade organizations, individual radio and television stations, executives, writers, performers, producers, and engineers.

The Library of American Broadcasting Foundation raises funds to help preserve fragile documents and recordings through digitization and other preservation methods and to support educational and research collaborations using the collection.

If you have broadcast-related materials you wish to donate, please visit our guide on How To Preserve Broadcast History: https://lib.guides.umd.edu/howtopreservebroadcasthistory

Name: شركة نقل عفش بالرياض : ويب موفرز
Description:
شركة نقل اثاث بالرياض تقوم بخدمة نقل العفش المنزلي  والمكتبي داخل وخارج الرياض .
افضل شركة نقل عفش بالرياض  توفر سيارات شحن الاثاث من الرياض الي أي منطقة فضلا عن توفير مواد تغليف الاثاث وتعد اقل أسعار شركات نقل اثاث بالرياض بين الكثير من مؤسسات و شركات نقل العفش بالرياض فأن كنت تبحث عن افضل شركات نقل اثاث بالرياض بأقل اسعار شحن الاثاث بالمملكة فلا داعي بضياع وقتك و اتصل بنا الان ليصلك مندوب الشركة والبدء في مراحل فك وتغليف وتعبئة الاثاث ونقلها الي المكان الجديد وتركيبها في اسرع وقت وبأقل تكاليف حيث تستخدم افضل شركة نقل اثاث و عفش في الرياض احدث معدات نقل الاثاث من مواد تغليف و سيارات مجهزة فضلا عن توفير الكراتين والبلاستيك للتغليف باستخدام خطوات نقل اثاث مهنية للحفاظ علي الاثاث من الفقدان  او الخدوشات او الضياع لهذا كانت شركتنا افضل شركة نقل عفش بالرياض.
لا داعي لضياع وقتك واتصل بنا الان ليصلك فريق عمل متميز وتمتع بمميزات شركة نقل العفش بالرياض وهيأفضل شركات نقل العفش بالرياض لاننا نهتم دائما الى ما يحتاجه عميلنا من حيث خدمة نقل الأثاث – نقل موبليا – نقل عفش – نحن من أوائل شركات نقل الأثاث لدينا خبرة متطورة في خدمات نقل الأثاث السكنى و التجاري و الفندقي بكل عناية ومهنية باستخدام أحدث الآليات في النقل لضمان أمان خدمة النقل في اقل وقت واقل تكاليف .

تقدم الشركة خدمة نقل عفش بالرياض بأفضل طرق النقل، و أقل تكاليف مع ضمان سلامة المنقولات من الأضرار و الخدوش أو فقدان أي شيء من هذه المنقولات وفى أسرع وقت ايضا وذلك لتوفير المال والوقت للعملاء أثناء عملية نقل أثاث بالرياض تعد الشركة من افضل شركات نقل الاثاث بالرياض وتقدم افضل خدمات النقل على ايدي فنيين مدربين وعمالة ماهرة منظمة لترتيب وتنظيم الاثاث بطريقة منظمة مع الفك والتركيب والتغليف , ونؤكد لكم اننا الشركة الاولى في المملكة العربية السعودية في مجال نقل العفش بافضل الطرق واحدث الوسائل .
ارخص اسعار نقل العفش بالرياض
حيث توفر الشركة(شركة نقل اثاث رخيص بالرياض) خصومات هائة لا توفرها الكثير من شركات نقل عفش الرياض تتراوح من بين 20 وتصل الي 30 % في بعص الاحيان,نحن موجودون في شركة نقل عفش بالرياض عي قمة شركات نقل العفش داخل الرياض  وذلك لما تقدمة الشركة من خدمات وكفاءة في العمل حازت علي قبول من كافة عملائنا.

Name: Onlibrary Studio
Description: En este programa nuestros invitados nos comparten de manera voluntaria los puntos más importantes que aprendieron de sus libros favoritos de marketing, negocios y desarrollo profesional, con el objetivo de incentivar la lectura, el intercambio de ideas y el desarrollo profesional.

Name: Ankara Adnan Ötüken İl Halk Kütüphanesi
Description: Ankara Adnan Ötüken İl Halk Kütüphanesi ilk olarak 25 Temmuz 1922‘de hizmet vermeye başlamıştır.

Name: Warren County Public Library
Description:
WCPL: for every chapter of your life!  Warren County Public Library's mission is to provide equal access to the discovery of ideas, the joy of reading, and the power of information. 

Lisa Rice Library
1225 State St, Bowling Green, Kentucky 42101

Hours	
Mon - Thu: 8:00 am - 8:00 pm
Fri - Sat: 9:00 am - 5:00 pm
Sun: 1:00 pm - 5:00 pm

Name: Colegio Colombiano de Bibliotecología Ascolbi
Description: Ascolbi agremia, representa y fortalece a los profesionales, estudiantes de Ciencia de la Información, Bibliotecología e instituciones bibliotecarias de Colombia, liderando la reflexión ética, disciplinar, el debate académico, científico, social y de política pública; la actualización profesional, el intercambio y generación de conocimiento con el fin de mejorar los procesos de adquisición, clasificación, descripción, conservación, acceso y apropiación social de la información y el conocimiento.

Name: Black Pearl Books
Description: A culturally curated collection promoting diversity. inclusion. equality.

Name: TOBB ETÜ Kütüphanesi
Description: Kütüphanemiz, 12 bin dolayında kitap içeren kuruluş koleksiyonu ile 2004-05 eğitim öğretim yılında hizmete açılmıştır. Ana binanın arka cephesinde iki kat üzerine 2000 metrekarelik bir alanda hizmet vermektedir. Ayrıca kütüphanenin önündeki fuayeler 24 saat açık okuma alanı olarak kullanılmaktadır. 2019-2020 eğitim öğretim yılı sonu itibariyle yaklaşık 69 bin cilt kitap ve 7 bin 88 ciltli dergiden oluşan basılı koleksiyon toplamda 76 bini aşmıştır. Koleksiyon, NLM kullanılan Tıp alanı dışında Kongre Kütüphanesi sistemine göre düzenlenmiş bulunmaktadır. Güncel veya arşiv mahiyetindeki koleksiyonlar üzerinden 42 bin kadar elektronik dergiye erişim sağlanmakta, elektronik olarak sağlanamayan 123 dergi için basılı abonelik sürdürülmektedir. Erişime sunulan 99 veri tabanı, söz konusu dergilerin dışında, 369 bin dolayında elektronik kitap, bildiri, tez, rapor gibi çeşitli bilgi kaynakları ve finansal veriler içermektedir. 20 dolayında günlük gazete, CD ve DVD ortamında sunulan müzik eserleri ve filmler kütüphane koleksiyonunun diğer ögeleridir.

Name: Long Island Library Resources Council
Description:
The Long Island Library Resources Council (LILRC) is one of New York’s nine Library Resources Systems.  

LILRC is a 501 (c)(3) tax-exempt not for profit organization. It is chartered by the New York State Board of Regents.  The Council was incorporated in 1966 and received its absolute Long Island charter in June 1971 as the Reference and Research Library Resources Council (3Rs) for Nassau and Suffolk Counties.   

The council is a regional multi-type library organization serving academic, special, public, and school libraries, library systems, museums and historical societies in Nassau and Suffolk counties. Our purpose is to enhance access to information, encourage resource sharing, promote innovation in libraries, and advocate for library interests.

Name: Rock Island Public Library
Description: Rock Island Public Library is a public library with a headquarters location (the "Downtown  Library") located at 401 19th St, Rock Island, Illinois, United States, along with the Rock Island Southwest Branch Library at 9010 Ridgewood Road, and our newest roaming location, the Library2Go mobile library bookmobile. Rock Island Library has been providing library services to the community since 1872.

Name: LLSDC
Description:
The Law Librarians' Society of Washington, D.C. (LLSDC) was established in 1939 for educational, informational and scientific purposes. 

It is conducted as a nonprofit corporation to promote librarianship; to develop and increase the effectiveness of law librarians; to cultivate the profession of law librarianship; to foster a spirit of ethical cooperation among members of the profession; and to provide for the further continuing education of law librarians. 

For more information, please visit our website at: http://www.llsdc.org; on Twitter at: http://twitter.com/LLSDC
and on Facebook: http://www.facebook.com/pages/LLSDC/175471305731.

Name: Normal Public Library
Description: The Normal Public Library is a valuable educational and recreational resource that enriches the quality of life for community residents. The Library is a catalyst for literacy, learning, creativity, and innovation. We connect, reflect, and engage our community and are strong stewards of our valuable resources.

Name: Yellowhead Regional Library
Description:
Established in 1971, the Yellowhead Regional Library (YRL) is a co-operative library system that provides bibliographic, client and communications services to member public and school libraries within the region. 

To provide its members with a broader spectrum of services, YRL partners with three other regional libraries in Alberta to form TRAC (The Regional Automation Consortium). This partnership allows for quicker circulation between member libraries and gives patrons access to an online catalogue with nearly 3,000,000 items and more than 800,000 titles. 

YRL currently serves 261,160 Albertans and 13,136 full-time equivalent students in 54 municipalities and four school divisions. For more information on the services we provide, please visit www.yrl.ab.ca.

Name: New Brunswick Free Public Library
Description: Incorporated in 1890, the library has been serving the community for over a century from its Carnegie building (completed in 1903) on Livingston Avenue.

Name: Yaşar Üniversitesi Kütüphane ve Bilgi Merkezi / Yaşar University Library and Information Center
Description:
Yaşar Üniversitesi’nin eğitim-öğretim ve araştırma faaliyetlerini desteklemek, öğretim elemanlarına, öğrencilere ve çalışanlarına her tür ve ortamdaki bilgi kaynağını sağlamak ve hizmete sunmak, kullanıcıların bilgiye erişim hizmetlerinden en etkin ve hızlı bir şekilde yararlanmalarını sağlamak ve ulusal-uluslararası ölçekte bilgi birikimi, kullanımı ve transferine destek olmak amacıyla kurulan Kütüphane ve Bilgi Merkezi Müdürlüğü, 2002 yılından beri aktif olarak hizmet vermektedir. 
1200 m2 kullanım alanı içerisinde 4 adet grup çalışma odası ve 500 kişilik oturma kapasitesine sahip Kütüphane ve Bilgi Merkezi eğitim-öğretim ve araştırma faaliyetlerine destek olmak amacıyla, Üniversitenin gereksinim duyduğu basılı, elektronik, görsel – işitsel bilgi kaynaklarını sağlayarak kullanıcıların hizmetine sunmaktadır.

Name: XIENIS

Name: Mitinet, Inc.
Description:
Mitinet makes the complex job of running a library simple.

We offer MARC data software tools and services. The company, founded in the late 1980's as Information Transform, led the way in automating MARC records for libraries during the initial conversion from print-based card catalogs to digital computerized databases. 

Mitinet provides library solutions which are personalized and high-tech.

At Mitinet, the focus is on cleaning up, organizing and optimizing your database and information management systems. The goal is to make your records orderly, your shelves tidy, and your books easy to find, leaving you free to focus on the most important job of all: helping children meet their educational goals.

Name: Fayetteville Free Library
Description: It is the mission of the Fayetteville Free Library to provide free and open access to ideas and information.  The Fayetteville Free Library is a member library of the Onondaga County Public Library system.  The FFL provides programs, events, and classes for the public.  We have computer labs, a cafe, wifi and community room.   We also have a state of the art digital media lab, as well as the first ever makerspace in a public library in the U.S.  Please visit www.fflib.org for more about the FFL and be sure to follow us on facebook for updates (http:www.facebook.com/fayfreelibrary) and on twitter (@fayettevillelib) for important information about programs and services.

Name: Arkansas State Library
Congregational Library & Archives
Description:
The Congregational Library began in 1853 when a small group of Boston clergymen donated 56 books from their personal collections. A century and a half later, it has become an internationally recognized resource for scholars, religious leaders, and local churches.

Today the Congregational Library & Archives is a thriving center for researchers of all kinds, from professional historians to church members curious about their roots — anyone wanting to understand more about a religious tradition that has deeply informed American culture. The Congregational story is about ordinary people doing extraordinary things, beginning with the seventeenth-century Puritans and continuing on through nineteenth-century abolitionists and social reformers to the work of modern-day Congregational churches toward a just and open society.

We believe that the stories of the past are an essential resource for twenty-first century life, providing depth and balance in an always moving world. In that spirit we are continually updating our learning opportunities. As an all-purpose library, we serve the professional scholars, graduate students, and genealogists who work in our historical collection, as well as the busy people who come here for seminars on Congregational history, walking tours of Boston's historic churches, or informal lunches with well-known scholars and popular authors.

The Congregational Library & Archives brings old and new together, carrying forward a tradition of care for the world's future by preserving and interpreting the stories of the past.

Name: Fairport Public Library
Description: Fairport Public Library, located in the Village of Fairport, New York, provides an array of services vital to job seekers, small business owners, and entrepreneurs. Free workshops are available to help with resume polishing, interview skills, networking, and more. Entrepreneurs and business owners will find a wide range of books, magazines, and databases to help launch your business -- legal guides, patent information, business plans,  marketing, and retaining employees are just some of the topics you’ll find here. Browse our new non-fiction for the latest business books, too!

Description: The mission of the Haverford Township Free Library is to provide residents of the Township of Haverford, as well as the public at large, with exemplary access to the broadest possible range of resources, programs and services that enhance and enrich their lives.

Name: Washington County Public Library System
Description:
The mission of the Washington County Public Library System is to provide the community with current resources for life-long learning, social exchange, pleasure and entertainment. 

A few of the services we offer the community:

► books, DVDs, audiobooks. Search our catalog via mobile with our Find It Virginia Boopsie app, va.boopsie.com

► free downloadable ebooks

► computer classes

► technology training on new devices, such as tablets and ereaders

► access to online research databases, free of charge

► extensive genealogical research materials

► online homework help for students

► test preparation materials

► meeting room space for community organizations

► exciting programs, such as Sunday with Friends series of speakers and Lego Play Days for children

► Wi-Fi access and public computer labs

We have notary service and fax service available in the Main Branch Library in Abingdon. We can find the government documents and forms you need on the internet, and print them for you for 15 cents per page.

Find out more about your library at http://www.wcpl.net.

Name: Convergent Library Technologies
Description:
We help libraries of all types leverage technology to better enable them to transition from transaction-based activities to community and institutional transformational endeavors.  
We help libraries deliver highly visible kiosk solutions like NovelBranch our mini-library kiosk used by public libraries for community outreach and by universities/colleges to provide high availability to in-demand collections like Course Reserves.  We streamline tasks behind the scenes in scheduling and cataloguing through our BookWhere and PeopleWhere software. We help you manage your PCs with the best PC booking and print management solutions available today.  We provide new services like a scanner that not only can replace your expensive copiers and produce digital copies to a variety of output options, but also which translate documents.  We can move your check-in activities to self-service with our economical and space saving intelligent return shelves.

Name: Oberlin College Libraries
Description: Supporting research and discoverability at Oberlin College and Conservatory.

Name: Williamsburg Regional Library
Description:
The Williamsburg Regional Library (WRL) is an award-winning regional library system that consists of the Williamsburg Library on Scotland St. in Williamsburg, the James City County Library on Croaker Rd. in James City County, the Stryker Center on N. Boundary St. in Williamsburg, and its Outreach department. 
WRL offers professional informational services, robust collections, and stimulating educational and cultural programs for all ages that inform and enrich the community. It is also a convenient, welcoming community center offering a variety of meeting spaces; business services like notary service and test proctoring; a makerspace with 3D and laser printers, Cricut machines, and sewing machines; a digital memory lab to transfer photos and film to digital; a newly renovated children’s room with many hands-on learning and play activities for families; and more.  
The Library serves residents of James City County, City of Williamsburg, and York County in Virginia.

Name: College of DuPage Library
Description: The mission of the Library is to support the educational goals and purposes of the College. This includes the academic program and the general information needs, diverse cultural interests, intellectual development, and professional growth of the entire College community. We gladly offer our services as a courtesy to the residents of Community College District 502 and the general public to the extent that resource limitations and legal restrictions allow.

Name: Upper Norwood Library Hub
Description:
The Upper Norwood Library Hub (UNLH) sits on the cusp of 5 London Boroughs (Croydon, Lambeth, Bromley, Southwark and Lewisham). 

Our professional library service is delivered by Lambeth Council, co-funded by Croydon Council and we also host the Crystal Palace Library of Things. 

We work in close partnership with library services, statutory and voluntary sector partners, connecting us to multiple communities and deeply rooting us into the heart of South London. 

Our library hub operates a co-created community programme focusing on sharing & learning, health & wellbeing, venue & performance & outreach activities. We promote equality through information, education, wellbeing and creative programmes that tackle inequalities, systemic barriers and widen participation to create open access. 

UNLH is managed by the Upper Norwood Library Trust (UNLT - an independent charity), formed in 2012 by local residents.

Name: Istituto Nazionale di Archeologia e Storia dell'Arte
Description: L’INASA opera dal 1918 per il progresso degli studi nel campo dell’archeologia e della storia dell’arte e per la tutela del patrimonio culturale. Il suo campo d’azione, attraverso il sostegno di enti pubblici e privati, spazia dall'elaborazione di progetti di studio alla valorizzazione dei beni archeologici e artistici.

Name: Wauconda Area Public Library
Description:
Welcome to the Wauconda Area Public Library! The library serves the communities of Wauconda, Island Lake, and portions of Lakemoor, Volo, Port Barrington, and Lake Barrington.

The purpose of the Wauconda Area Library is to provide and promote a variety of library resources and services in response to the informational, educational, cultural, and recreational needs of both the residential and business communities. In order to ensure the highest quality in achieving this purpose, the Library will utilize current technology and a courteous, well trained staff. 

The Library will provide a comfortable, attractive environment, easy access to its resources, and will actively promote its presence and services, encouraging input from the community and measuring performance at regular intervals.

Name: Editorial Legado
Description:
Editorial Legado se especializa en la producción editorial de Centroamérica.
Es una respuesta a la necesidad de difundir la producción intelectual centroamericana en espacios geográficos más amplios que la estrecha cintura del Continente. Aunque en una primera etapa se dará mayor énfasis de la producción editorial de Costa Rica y El Salvador, gradualmente cubrirá a todos los países de la región. Nuestro propósito es proporcionar al mundo la posibilidad de conocer el aporte de los centroamericanos en el arte, la ciencia y la tecnología que se refleja en los libros publicados por las editoriales públicas y privadas, de Guatemala, El Salvador, Honduras, Nicaragua, Costa Rica y Panamá.

Name: The Blue Olives
Description:
We know how challenging it is to pick up a book to read in this generation. Between busy schedules, indecision and confusion over which book to read, lack of a right environment and lack of motivation/encouragement... it's no wonder that many people give up on reading altogether!

But at TBO, we're committed to changing that narrative. We believe that every soul—young or old; middle-aged or just starting out—can become a master of their mind by building a community of readers with like-minds and confidence in one another.

TBO is proud to host online events where people can share ideas, thoughts, opinions, and tears and joys. 

We invite you to be a part of The Tribe of The Blue Olives!

Name: Marion Public Library
Description: Located in Uptown Marion, the Marion Public Library (MPL) serves readers, creators, learners, and dreamers of all ages. MPL attracts thousands of residents annually through programming activities, community events, and a variety of service offerings.

Name: FID Soziologie (SocioHub)
Description:
Der Fachinformationsdienst (FID) Soziologie wird seit 2016 von der Deutschen Forschungsgemeinschaft (DFG) gefördert und gemeinsam von der Universitäts- und Stadtbibliothek Köln, der GESIS – Leibniz-Institut für Sozialwissenschaften sowie vom Forschungsdatenzentrum Qualiservice betreut.

Im Mittelpunkt des FID Soziologie stehen die Förderung der Forschungskommunikation sowie die Verbesserung der wissenschaftlichen Informationsversorgung innerhalb der Soziologie.

Mit SocioHub bietet der FID Soziologie eine Kollaborationsplattform für die soziologische Fachcommunity. Die Schwerpunkte liegen auf Vernetzung, Open Science, Forschungsdatenmanagement und Recherche.

Website: https://sociohub-fid.de

Name: Newark Public Library
Description: Newark Public Library is a public library in the Finger Lakes region of New York. This library is affiliated with the library system that serves Newark, NY and it is a member of the Pioneer Library System. The collection of the library contains 43288 volumes and the library circulates 118892 items per year. This library serves a population of nearly 10,000 residents.

Name: University Library Zurich
Description: Since 2022, the University Library Zurich has united all UZH libraries under one organizational roof. We provide members of the University of Zurich with all the information resources they need for their studies and research at around 30 locations. We work closely with the Zentralbibliothek Zürich.

Description:
Fratelli Gionchetti boasts a hundred years of experience and is aiming at expanding its international market in Europe and worldwide. Our company is specialized in the creation of libraries and furnishings for libraries. We can find solutions for any type of environment, with a combination of practicality and elegance.

From the design stage, to the choice of materials, up to manufacturing, each step of the production process is completed by highly qualified personnel. The materials we use stand the test of time, and our designs are unique and become one with the surrounding environment.

Description: Bringing ideas, information, and learning to our community of Plainfield, IL.

Name: Universitätsbibliothek Freiberg
Description:
Die Universitätsbibliothek ist DIE zentrale Informationseinrichtung der TU Bergakademie Freiberg. Als Bibliotheks- und Hörsaalzentrum ein beliebter Treffpunkt der Studierenden sowie eines der wichtigsten kommunikativen Zentren auf dem Campus. Zahlreiche und vielfältige Arbeitsplätze, kompetente Beratung sowie ein umfangreicher Bestand an historischer und aktueller Literatur zum Universitätsprofil stehen unseren Nutzern gedruckt sowie online zur Verfügung.

Unsere 1765 gegründete Bibliothek ist nicht zuletzt auch für ihren hervorragenden Altbestand an geo- und montanwissenschaftlicher Literatur weit über Deutschland hinaus bekannt. Auch aktuelle Themen wie Open Science, Forschungsdatenmanagement oder Mixed Reality sind Teil unseres Aufgabenbereichs.

Name: Wisconsin Library Association
Description:
The Wisconsin Library Association is a nonprofit, professional membership organization that has provided service to the library community and Wisconsin’s citizens since 1891.  WLA represents librarians and library staff from school, public, academic and special libraries as well as students, trustees and library friends. Because of its broad membership base, WLA is concerned about the needs of all types of libraries in the state. WLA is a chapter of the American Library Association.

OUR MISSION
The Wisconsin Library Association engages, inspires and advocates for library workers and supporters to improve and promote library services for the people of Wisconsin.

Name: Alexandria VA Public Library
Description: We offer you access to the world of electronic information. Not only can you access our records of holdings and availability, but we offer access to full text articles from thousands of magazines, journals and newspapers through our online databases. You can search genealogy records with Ancestry Library Edition, borrow eBooks and eAudiobooks on Libby and download digital music with Freegal. Free public access to the Internet is available at all branches as well as free Wi-Fi access for laptops and mobile devices.

Name: IADT Library
Description: The mission of IADT Library is to support the teaching, learning and research activities of the Institute. The Library offers a wide range of seamless, high-quality, integrated information services, as well as a comprehensive print collection. The professional Library staff offer expert support to students and staff in the use of all resources and services.

Name: Digitale Bibliotheek voor de Nederlandse Letteren | DBNL
Description: De Digitale Bibliotheek voor de Nederlandse Letteren (DBNL) is een digitale collectie van teksten die behoren tot de Nederlandse letterkunde, taalkunde en cultuurgeschiedenis van de vroegste tijd tot heden. De collectie representeert het hele Nederlandse taalgebied en komt tot stand door een samenwerking tussen de Taalunie, de Vlaamse Erfgoedbibliotheek en de Koninklijke Bibliotheek te Den Haag.

Name: Rudersdal Bibliotekerne
Description:
Rudersdal Bibliotekerne er et litteratur- og videnshus, som tager udgangspunkt i det klassiske bibliotek, der udgør kernen i vores bibliotekstilbud og -services. Vi lægger vægt på, at biblioteket er rum for den demokratiske samtale, den gode debat og kulturelle fællesskaber. Og vi arbejder målrettet på at understøtte den sammenhængende indsats, når det handler om børns læsning og sprogudvikling.

Rudersdal Bibliotekerne huser tre fysiske biblioteker i Birkerød, Holte og Nærum. Med ’Det 4. Bibliotek’ kommer vi ud i hele Rudersdal kommune, som består af 10 byområder, og tilbyder alle borgere formidling uden for bibliotekets fysiske rammer. Man kan f.eks. møde os med Pop-up biblioteket, hvor vi har ladcyklen fyldt med litteraturtilbud. Og deltage i Biblioteket Live samt online læseklubber og litteratursnakke, hvor vi via skærmen bringer livestreamede forfattersamtaler, debat og fællesskaber helt hjem til borgerne.

Rudersdal Bibliotekerne består af et dedikeret medarbejderhold på godt 40 rudbibber, som samarbejder på kryds og tværs af fagligheder om at skabe det bedst mulige bibliotekstilbud til borgerne. Vores mission er at understøtte og fremme livslang læring, læsning, dannelse, demokrati og fællesskab. Du kan læse mere om vores vision og vigtigste strategiske indsatsområder i Rudersdal kommunes biblioteksstrategi ’Fælles om biblioteket’ her: 

https://rudbib.dk/sites/rudersdal.ddbcms.dk/files/files/page/strategi_version_10_final.pdf

Name: Bibliotheek Achterhoekse Poort
Description: De Bibliotheek Achterhoekse Poort is een openbare bibliotheek midden in de Achterhoekse samenleving. Met haar zes vestigingen in de gemeenten Aalten en Oude IJsselstreek biedt zij de inwoners ruimte voor ontmoeting, inspiratie en educatie. De Bibliotheek Achterhoekse Poort richt zich op het bevorderen van geletterdheid, leesplezier, digitale inclusie, digitaal burgerschap en een leven lang leren voor iedereen. Hierbij werkt ze samen met diverse partners, zowel lokaal, als regionaal en landelijk.

Name: Suffolk Public Library
Description:
The Suffolk Public Library offers over 300,000 books, books on CD, magazines, and digital books, movies, and music to educate, entertain, and explore from three library locations in addition to outreach services that include a bookmobile, Pop-Up Library and more.

Friendly and professional staff are available to assist in finding information, teaching classes from early literacy to STEM to technology, and connecting people and groups to each other.

Library Vision Statement

Create an informed, enlightened, and thriving community where all can achieve their highest potential.

Library Mission Statement

Connect individuals and groups to information, ideas and experiences.

Name: Bibnet
Description: Bibnet is een projectorganisatie van de Vlaamse Overheid. Met Bibnet wil de Vlaamse Overheid er voor zorgen dat bibliotheken ook in een digitale omgeving een meerwaarde kunnen betekenen voor hun publiek. De Vlaamse Overheid neemt met dit initiatief rechtstreeks de verantwoordelijkheid op, voor de positionering van de Vlaamse openbare bibliotheken in de digitale wereld.

Name: Ilmiduniya
Description: Ilmi Duniya is a platform where we share the inspiring stories of the Sahabah (companions of the Prophet), detailing how Islam spread across the world. We explore the profound sacrifices made by our beloved Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) and his companions, highlighting their dedication and the impact of their efforts. Through these narratives, we aim to deepen your understanding of our faith and the rich history behind its growth. Join us as we uncover the powerful legacy of sacrifice and devotion that continues to guide and inspire us today.

Name: Bibliotheek Hoeksche Waard
Description: Bibliotheek met vestigingen in 's-Gravendeel, Heinenoord, Klaaswaal, Mijnsheerenland, Numansdorp, Oud-Beijerland, Puttershoek, Strijen en Westmaas

Name: Lawrence Free Public Library
Description:
Our Mission

 The freedom to pursue knowledge is a foundation of our democracy. The Lawrence Free Public Library, an essential public institution, strives to serve as an accessible and responsive information and literary center for all residents of the Lawrence community. Through its evolving collection of materials and an abiding concern for its customers, the Library exists to preserve the free development and expression of ideas essential for an informed citizenry.

Name: Biblioteca Pública Episcopal del Seminari Conciliar de Barcelona
Description:
La Biblioteca Pública Episcopal del Seminari de Barcelona (BPEB) neix fruit de la fusió de dues entitats precedents: el fons bibliogràfic de les biblioteques dels col·legis que la Companyia de Jesús tenia a Barcelona –que després de la seva expulsió dels Regnes d’Espanya van passar a mans de l’Estat– i de la biblioteca de l’antic Seminari Conciliar de Barcelona. Tots dos fons es fusionaren l’any 1775 i van configurar l’inici de la gran biblioteca actual.

La BPEB és la biblioteca pública conservada més antiga de la ciutat (es crea l’any 1772 a partir d’un edicte del bisbe Josep Climent i Avinent). És una entitat dependent de l’Arquebisbat de Barcelona, que actua com a unitat d’informació de l’Ateneu Universitari Sant Pacià i de les facultats i institucions que en formen part (la Facultat de Teologia de Catalunya, la Facultat de Filosofia de Catalunya, la Facultat Antoni Gaudí d’Història, Arqueologia i Arts Cristianes i l’Institut de Litúrgia), i de l’Institut Superior de Ciències Religioses de Barcelona.

La BPEB disposa d’un fons bibliogràfic format per més de 370.000 volums especialitzats en ciències eclesiàstiques, filosofia i humanitats.

Name: International English Library
Description:
Founded in 1946,  we are the only public English library in North Rhine-Westphalia with a collection of over 26,000 publications from classic to contemporary, run by an international team of volunteers. The purpose of our organization is to foster a community around English books and culture. We believe that stories build bridges across cultures and that reading opens new doors!

The library can be found on the 1st Floor,  Mecumstraße 10, 40223, Düsseldorf, NRW, Germany.

Name: Orient Vibes
Description: Bienvenidos a Orient Vibes, donde charlaremos de libros de filosofía tradicional oriental que te servirán para mejorar en tus relaciones interpersonales, el nivel de conciencia y tu desarrollo hacia una mejor versión de ti mismo.

Name: Circulating Ideas
Description: Circulating Ideas facilitates conversations about the innovative people & ideas moving libraries through the 21st century.

Name: Portage County District Library
Description: Public library services for Portage County, Ohio.

Name: Brown University Library
Description: The Brown University Library is central to the Brown’s teaching and research mission. With a collection that includes approximately 7 million volumes (including over 2 million ebooks), nearly 150,000 online journals, more than 500 databases, and over 15,000 linear feet of manuscripts and archives, the Brown Library is one of the largest academic libraries in New England. In addition, Brown faculty and students may borrow from the collections of other Ivy libraries (some 90 million titles) as well as obtain materials from libraries around the globe through interlibrary loan. The Brown Library system consists of five campus buildings as well as the nearby Library Collections Annex (an off-campus high-density storage facility with a shelving capacity of 1.5 million volumes). The John D. Rockefeller, Jr. Library houses the Library’s collections for the humanities, social sciences, and fine arts. It is also home to the Center for Digital Scholarship, a hub of leading-edge digital projects, including the Mellon-funded Digital Publications Initiative. The Sciences Library supports research in the STEM fields, along with the George S. Champlin Health Sciences Library (located in the Alpert Medical School), which provides digital resources and infrastructure to medical students and faculty. Brown’s world-renowned special collections of rare books, manuscripts, archives, and ephemera are housed in the John Hay Library and the Library Annex. A Carnegie library, the exquisitely restored Hay offers extensive exhibition space, promoting engagement with Brown’s unique holdings for students, faculty, and the broader community. The Virginia M. Orwig Music Library consolidates Brown’s extensive music materials and is home to the Walter Neiman ‘46 Archive of Sound Recordings as well as more than 24,000 musical scores.

Name: کتابخانه دانشکده حقوق دانشگاه شهید بهشتی/ Library of Law Faculty of Shahid Beheshti University
Description:
کتابخانه دانشکده حقوق دانشگاه شهید بهشتی، در سال 1345 راهاندازی شده و قدمتی بیش از نیم قرن دارد. مجموعه ارزشمند کتابخانه، با جمعآوری بیش از  17123 نسخه کتاب فارسی و عربی و 7300 نسخه کتاب به سایر زبان ها، منبعی ارزشمند برای پژوهشگران است. کتابخانه علاوه بر این، خدمات دسترسی برخط به پایگاه های داده حقوقی ایرانی و غیرایرانی را فراهم کرده است.
افتخارما نه در گستردگی منابع، بلکه در تعهدمان به خدمت به حقوقدانان برجسته است که زمانی برای آموزش و پژوهش، از این کتابخانه استفاده کردند. این راه همچنان ادامه دارد و این افتخار برای ما باقیست که به بهترین دانشجویان حقوق در ایران، خدمات آموزشی و پژوهشی ارائه دهیم زیرا باور داریم که از میان آنان حقوقدانان بزرگی برخواهند خاست و خدمات ارزشمندی به مردم ایران ارائه خواهند داد.

“The Library of the Faculty of Law at Shahid Beheshti University was established in 1966 and has a history of over half a century. The library's valuable collection includes more than 17,123 copies of Persian and Arabic books, as well as 7,300 copies of books in other languages, serving as a valuable resource for researchers. Additionally, the library provides online access to both Iranian and non-Iranian legal databases.
Our pride lies not in the breadth of our resources, but in our commitment to serving prominent lawyers who have utilized this library for their education and research. This path continues and it is our honor to provide educational and research services to the top law students in Iran as we believe that outstanding lawyers will emerge amongst them who would provide valuable services to the people of Iran.”

Name: Community Libraries of Providence
Description:
Our Mission

Community Libraries of Providence serves the culturally rich people of Providence by adapting to their evolving reading and information needs with a diverse staff that delivers relevant library resources and programs, cultivates meaningful partnerships and promotes vibrant neighborhoods.

About Us 

Providence Community Library (PCL) serves more than 54,000 registered  patrons. Our nine library locations provide access to a wealth of resources, knowledge and opportunities for the people of Providence. Additionally, our mobile library takes the joy of reading and lifelong learning out on the road to schoolyards, city parks and special events. PCL is the largest library system in the state of Rhode Island and the city’s largest, free educational institution after the Providence Public Schools. PCL is a member of Ocean State Libraries network.

Our Funding

PCL receives approximately 85 percent of its funding from the State of Rhode Island and City of Providence. We work to raise the remaining 15 percent through generous contributions from charitable, corporate and individual donors. To learn more about how to support PCL, visit http://www.provcomlib.org/support-pcl .

Name: Campbell County Public Library, Kentucky
Description: Connecting you to big ideas wherever you are! Campbell County Public Library enhances lifelong learning by providing popular materials, programs, and services to our community.

Name: Barrington Area Library
Description:
Our 21st century library is a busy, friendly place that supports lifelong, active learning and an appreciation for creativity and innovation.  Customers of all ages and interests visit our library both in person and online, expecting to find excellent and informed service, welcoming, state of the art spaces for private work and group collaboration, programs and workshops that enrich and inspire, and the very best in books, movies, music, and research resources.

The Barrington Public Library District serves residents of all or part of Algonquin, Barrington, Barrington Hills, Deer Park, Fox River Grove, Hoffman Estates, Kildeer, Lake Barrington, Lake Zurich, North Barrington, Palatine, Port Barrington, South Barrington, Tower Lakes, and some unincorporated areas. It covers a 72-square-mile area, making it one of the largest library districts in Illinois.

Name: Librairie Vie d'Impact
Description:
La Librairie Vie d'Impact est une librairie axée sur la vente de bouquins en développement personnel & Business. 
Vous y trouverez des livres en finance personnelle, motivation, leadership, management, économie et finance, Marketing & Vente, entrepreneuriat,  gestion de carrière, etc

Name: CLiC (Colorado Library Consortium)
Description:
MISSION
Connecting. Energizing. Inspiring. Services for libraries throughout Colorado.

VISION
Helping libraries achieve greatness in their communities

CORE VALUES

Seek opportunities for collaboration 
Support growth and discovery
