In 2012, she launched www.StandUpLibrarianDesigns.com so she would have something literary fabulous to wear.  In just two years, her line of literary accessories has grown to include purses, cosmetic bags, fanny packs, bow ties and hats - all inspired by her favorite books and authors.  A combination of Pippi Longstocking and Sandra Bernhard with the style of Carrie Bradshaw, Meredith performs at library and book events across the country.

Meredith Myers, the “Stand-Up Librarian,” has a Masters degree in Library and Information Sciences from the University of South Florida and has been performing stand-up comedy since 2002 after checking out a comedy book from her local library.

Name: Audiolibrosmx
Description:
AudiolibrosMx nace en el año 2007 (como parte de Vive el Tiempo) en donde sus tres fundadoras aprovechando su experiencia y las innovaciones tecnológicas que surgen y se perfeccionan día con día pusieron muy en alto la mirada para lograr cumplir la meta de acercar a las personas a los libros, alimentando su imaginación, desarrollo, crecimiento personal y la empatía… buscando así un mundo mejor.

Autores clásicos y contemporáneos que tienen una gama infinita de riqueza para mostrarnos, finalmente están cerca de nosotros, de una manera accesible, grata y sencilla para escuchar.

Desde el inicio se pensó en ser parte de las estrategias para fortalecer la lectura de las personas débiles visuales, sin embargo, se reconoció desde el inicio también una falta de acercamiento a esta riqueza escrita por segmentos de población muy amplios, como aquellos que tienen jornadas laborales muy extensas, así como los que diariamente pierden horas en un medio de transporte.

AudiolibrosMx tuvo la inquietud por incidir en el fomento a la lectura y en la divulgación de contenidos interesantes y es así como a lo largo de más de 10 años hemos crecido nuestra producción, ganado convocatorias de divulgación y con presentaciones de nuestro material en México y en el extranjero.

Producimos audiolibros de calidad, con voces profesionales y expertas, musicalización de autores reconocidos, estudios de grabación y maquilas con los más altos estándares de calidad, para que tengas de una manera grata una aventura por el camino de las letras.

Name: Caelum project
Description:
CAELUM places university libraries in the forefront of crisis response through open science, supporting vulnerable communities and bolstering EU-Ukraine cooperation. The project’s aim is two-fold: a) upskill university libraries’ staff in innovative and interdisciplinary approaches to crisis response through EU-Ukraine universities collaboration, and b) promote and sustain university libraries-driven open knowledge and civic engagement among university students and staff for crisis response.

 

Thus, CAELUM brings to the fore the need for effective university libraries-driven crisis response by strengthening knowledge and best practices exchange between its EU and Ukrainian partners. Through its case studies, CAELUM is designed to empower and facilitate university library staff to transform libraries into hubs of mutual learning, support and engagement with vulnerable communities.

Our collection starts at the dawn of photography and spans over a century of astronomical data and the history of astrophysics. Central to our collection and mission are the individual and collective leagues of over 146 Women Astronomical Computers. These pioneering scientists broke boundaries and set the scientific foundations that we still build upon today. We are currently making our collection accessible online and in person like never before. 

We are a part of the Harvard College Observatory within Harvard University and a member of the Center for Astrophysics | Harvard & Smithsonian.

The Carlsbad Library Foundation encourages the involvement of patrons and donors to insure that the Carlsbad City Library facilities and programs are the finest in the country in fulfilling the cultural and educational needs of our community.

Literature and the arts flourished during the Renaissance due in large part to the tireless support of an enlightened patronage. Today, the Carlsbad City Library continues to grow with the same enthusiastic involvement of those who wish to create and preserve a lasting legacy of learning for future generations.

The Carlsbad City Libraries, the Dove and the Cole, both recently remodeled and the Learning Center are a community treasure. From the extensive collection of nearly 250,000 volumes to a wide range of online data services, from an imaginative gallery of art to a state-of-the-art auditorium, the Library has amassed a wealth of cultural resources from a long tradition of civic pride and responsibility.

Continuing in this tradition, the Carlsbad Library Foundation was created to stimulate the enlightened patronage of our time in providing for the future growth and development of the Library as it anticipates and responds to the ever-changing needs of our growing and diverse community.

Visit us online at www.carlsbadlibraryartsfoundation.org or connect with us on social media.

Name: Wamego Public Library
Description:
The Wamego Public Library is located at 431 Lincoln Ave., on the corner of 5th and Lincoln, in Wamego, KS.  The library has over 28,000 items available to borrowers, including books, large print books, magazines, newspapers, audiobooks, videos and DVDs.  The library is a member of the North Central Kansas Library System and participates in the Kansas Interlibrary Loan System, enabling patron access to materials throughout Kansas.  

The main floor of the library includes the adult and teen collections, the Kansas collection, reference materials, and quiet reading room. Additional services include 8 computers for public internet access, free wi-fi within the library, fax and copy services, microfilm machine and local newspapers on microfilm from 1867 to present.  The children's department and an on-going used book sale are both located on the lower level.  

Wamego Public Library offers a variety of free programs for all ages, including an adult book club, teen programs, after school activities, weekly preschool and toddler storytimes, and a summer reading program.

Name: Fundación IWO
Description:

Fue creada en el año 1925 en Vilna, el centro cultural de los judíos de Europa Central. En 1928 estableció la sede en Buenos Aires que comenzó a trabajar en forma independiente a partir de la Segunda Guerra Mundial durante la cual el IWO de Vilna fue saqueado y destruido.
La Fundación IWO mantiene una biblioteca, un archivo histórico y un museo, creando ámbitos para el estudio, la comunicación inter-generacional y el encuentro entre culturas. Fundación IWO ofrece cursos de ídish y cultura judía, talleres de capacitación en temas de patrimonio cultural, genealogía, presentaciones de libros, conferencias, seminarios y conciertos.
Location: Buenos Aires, Ciudad autónoma de Buenos Aires

Description:
THE LIBRARY
 
RCS Community Library is an independent, 501(c)3 nonprofit organization serving the residents of the Ravena-Coeymans-Selkirk Central School District in southeastern Albany County, New York State.  Its service area includes the southern part of the Town of Bethlehem (Selkirk, South Bethlehem, and part of Glenmont), all of the Town of Coeymans, and the southeastern corner of the Town of New Scotland (Feura Bush) in Albany County; and the riverside hamlet of New Baltimore in the Town of New Baltimore in Greene County. 

The Library's loan collection includes books for all ages, audio books on tape and on CD, graphic novels, videos in VHS and DVD formats, music CDs, and magazines.  Ebooks and audio books in many formats can be downloaded.  There are 5 public access computers in the library. They have Internet access; games; and word processing, spreadsheet and Powerpoint capability.  The library computers can retrieve medical information, magazine articles, newspapers, encyclopedias, literary criticism, practice exams, telephone directories, and other current information from subscription databases.  The Library also offers wireless Internet access.

Columbia University Libraries is a globally-recognized academic research library, serving one of the world's most important centers of research and learning. The Libraries build, sustain, and make discoverable collections that transcend traditional boundaries of format and domain, keeping pace with a dynamic and rapidly evolving information environment and creating unique opportunities for users to encounter global thought. As vital partners in the University's research and learning ecosystem, the Libraries connects users with rich and distinctive collections, fosters meaningful learning experiences, and provides innovative research support for a large, diverse user population.

Columbia University Libraries is the library system of Columbia University in the City of New York and one of the largest academic library systems in North America. With 15.0 million volumes and over 160,000 journals and serials, as well as extensive electronic resources, manuscripts, rare books, microforms, maps, and graphic and audio-visual materials, it is the fifth-largest academic library in the United States and the largest academic library in the State of New York.

Name: BRITISH AND IRISH LEGAL INFORMATION INSTITUTE
Description:
British and Irish Legal Information Institute (BAILII) provides individuals with access to a comprehensive set of British and Irish primary legal materials. BAILII included 90 databases covering 7 jurisdictions, along with a system that contains around 36 gigabytes of legal materials and around 297,513 searchable documents.


The databases on BAILII are derived from a number of sources: some of the data comes from existing free to air sites. Most of the databases are based on published and unpublished CD-ROMs or rely upon direct and indirect feeds by relevant courts, government departments, and other organizations. All of the data has been converted into a consistent format and a generalized set of search and hypertext facilities have been added.  The software and approaches that are used on BAILII have been provided with the assistance of the Australasian Legal Information Institute.


BAILII makes its website available on a subscription-free basis for the benefit of the public, including pro bono organizations, neighborhood law centers, students, and users in developing countries. It incurs substantial running costs in maintaining its database and website, and is dependent on a continuing flow of donations in order to remain in operation. Commercial users of BAILII and educational institutions are requested to make annual donations in order to assist BAILII in meeting its running costs.


BAILII is legally constituted in the UK as a company limited by guarantee and as a charitable trust. It is supported by a number of major sponsors and is assisted by many other organizations and individuals. The organization is hosted in the UK and Ireland by the Institute of Advanced Legal Studies, London and the Law Faculty, University College Cork.

ReCAP is jointly owned and operated by Columbia University, Harvard University, The New York Public Library and Princeton University. More than 17 million items are currently in ReCAP's care and they are used to fulfill approximately 250,000 requests for materials each year, from its partners and from libraries around the world.

Name: Algoritmo Criminale
Description:
di Pierguido Iezzi (Autore), Ranieri Razzante (Autore), con la prefazione di Ivano Gabrielli e postfazione di Antonio Nicaso, Edito da Il Sole 24 Ore. 
La criminalità organizzata ha rapidamente adottato le tecnologie digitali per espandere le proprie attività illecite, sfruttando innovazioni come criptovalute e ransomware per ottenere profitti miliardari. Le mafie tradizionali e nuovi gruppi criminali internazionali, come la ’ndrangheta e la mafia nigeriana, operano ormai in ambito digitale attraverso sofisticate truffe, frodi e riciclaggio di denaro. La lotta contro questa “Mafia 4.0” richiede un approccio globale e interdisciplinare, combinando indagini finanziarie e competenze tecnologiche avanzate per affrontare efficacemente queste minacce in continua evoluzione.

Name: Morton Public Library
Description:
The Mission of the Morton Public Library is to actively connect people to books, resources, and technology; and provide opportunities for learning, collaboration, and enjoyment. 

Library service in the Village of Morton began in 1922 when the Semper Fidelis club of Morton Community United Church of Christ organized donations and volunteers for a library. In 1925, the library became tax supported and the first library board was elected.

The current library building was designed by architect Don Westlake and constructed by Diversified Buildings, Inc. The original 9,500 sq. ft. building opened in 1982. In 1998, a 9,700 sq. ft. addition was completed. Many areas in the library are dedicated to donors who contributed to the Building Foundation. Additional funding included a Live & Learn construction grant from the Secretary of State. 

Hannah's Reading Garden was installed in 2015. 

In 2018 the library underwent a Refresh project, updating paint and carpet throughout the building and rearranging the interior spaces. This created an open and welcoming lobby, updated the front men's and women's restrooms, relocated children's services to the back of library - putting its collection together and gaining 700 sq. feet of space; creating a dedicated teen/young adult area and gained an extra meeting room by putting the Morton History Collection out on display. 

Governed by an elected board of seven trustees, the library serves residents of Morton and is a member of Reaching Across Illinois Library System (R.A.I.L.S.)

Name: One Vault
Description:
A resourceful library is a place to learn new things every day. At 'One Vault' our team is dedicated to providing a resourceful library with millions of e-books, e-journals, and e-theses from top-tier institutes all over the globe. In addition, a subscription to 'One Vault' facilitates the service of library management software. 

For Institutes:-
'One Vault' works with institutes in a revenue-sharing model. In this, a subscription to 'One Vault' facilitates an institute to store its e-resources in the 'One Vault' library database, which generates revenue when 'One Vault' is permitted to share those e-resources. Moreover, the institutes become able to access the millions of available e-resources in it. Also, service to convert the available paperback theses and old journals in the institute into electronic format is provided to the institutes. 

For Publishing House:-
A quarterly book exhibition in an institute is a conventional but effective medium to recommend a book in the institute's library. However, the more effective way is to online advertise your book by giving a glimpse of the book to potential readers. Keeping this in context, 'One Vault' categorises the readers into distinct categories and provides recommendations of e-books according to their tailored needs. In addition, 'One Vault' provides the library database to store the books, which when subscribed by users generates the revenue directly for the publisher(s).

- RFID Tag Insertion

- Stock Analysis

- EM strip removal 
- Desensitising

- Shelving and re-shelving

We help libraries ensure that they get the most out of their existing budgets offering them the opportunity to outsource labour intensive activities.

Name: University of New England Library Services
Description:
UNE Library Services advance learning, research excellence, and community engagement across the University. We empower students, faculty, and alumni through world-class collections, expert guidance, and innovative spaces that foster inquiry, collaboration, and lifelong discovery.

 We provide access to 150+ research databases, 2.3 million books and e-books, 169,000 journals, and 100,000 films. Our services include required textbooks, course reserves, free interlibrary loan, and statewide borrowing through MaineCat.

Our librarians provide personalized research assistance through chat, Zoom, email, phone, or in-person appointments. We provide information literacy instruction, subject-specific research guides, and support for search strategies, citations, publishing, and open access through DUNE: DigitalUNE.

 UNE Library Services include the Jack S. Ketchum Library in Biddeford and the Josephine S. Abplanalp ’45 Library in Portland—welcoming, flexible learning environments open day and night.

Our distinctive collections enrich scholarship and cultural understanding:
-UNE Art Galleries present contemporary exhibitions connecting art, science, and the humanities.
-The Maine Women Writers Collection preserves the creative legacy of women and gender-expansive authors from Maine and northern New England.
-Featherman University Archives document UNE’s history and evolution.
-New England Osteopathic Heritage Center (NEOHC) preserves over a century of osteopathic medical heritage.

 UNE librarians are leaders in state and national professional organizations, advancing open access, information literacy, and equitable knowledge sharing.
Follow UNE Library Services for research insights, digital scholarship updates, and access to innovative information practices.

Name: University Library in Bratislava
Description:
The University Library in Bratislava (ULB) is the oldest and largest research library in the Slovak Republic. It was established in 1919 as a library for the newly founded Comenius University. In parallel to its academic purpose it has also served as the national library until 1954. From the very beginning the Library gained the right to legal deposit. Since 1954 ULB has become a major research library that also serves the general public. The name University Library remains because it speaks of its historical purpose, funding and user background. Over the years the library transformed from a classical library to a modern research library and multifunctional cultural centre.

At present the Library contains approximately 2 520 000 publications. Its collections include:

Slovak Literature – a relatively complete collection of new books and periodicals based on legal deposit. In the years 1919/1920-1939 ULB was the only library at the territory of Slovakia entitled to legal deposit. Since 1920 a collection of Slovak literature written by Slovak authors living in Slovakia as well as abroad has been built. Since 1978 a collection of dissertations from the territory of Slovakia has been compiled at the Library.

Foreign Literature - a collection of foreign literature is composed of humanities, natural science and documents of universal character with special attention to secondary sources of information. Literature in world languages is being collected as well as in Slavic and other European languages. From among 15,500 publications acquired annually, foreign literature makes up 35%. Apart from classical publications, electronic documents are part of the collection as well. Secondary information sources are also available - national bibliographies, publication catalogues, bibliographic papers, the series of Science Citation Index as well as the PROQUEST database (1,100 titles of periodicals) with bibliographic information and full text citations.

Name: Waterford Township Public Library
Description: The Waterford Township Public Library is busy medium-sized public library in the center of Oakland County serving the 70,000 residents of Waterford and Lake Angelus, Michigan. Name: e-Ananse
Description:
e-Ananse is a new model of public library, deeply rooted in Ghanaian, black and African culture. Currently offered in three locations in Accra, the e-Ananse libraries seek to revitalise Ghana’s reading and story-telling culture by improving literacy and providing young people access to high-quality, contemporary literature. 

The libraries feature an extensive collection of works by African writers, in addition to smart technology hubs giving library members access to a range of online literary resources. 

Each library offers a unique combination of print books, state-of-the-art computing facilities, and live literature events, all celebrating Ghana’s pride in its African heritage.

Name: Scientific & Biomedical Information Centre
Description:
SBC institute is a non profit membership research organization specialised in Biomedicine, Management, Health care management and Chemistry. Founded in April 1995 and recent years, after closed in Greece, established as american domestic in Florida USA public charity in education sector with EIN Number: 35-2620990 under section 501(c)(3). Is member of National Network of Libraries of Medicine (NN/LM).

Belongs to LYRASIS (merger of PALINET and SOLINET networks) network in USA. 
Its aim is: 

a) the benefit of information and research in the sectors of Biomedicine, Social Sciences and Chemistry,

b) the collaboration with institutions and Organizations of Interior and Abroad and 

c) the Attendance of programs and World congresses that concern the science of information and research in the sectors that the Institute cover. 

Mission :
Scientific & Biomedical Information Centre's mission is to encourage the advancement of information and research in the sectors of Biomedicine , Management, Healthcare management and Chemistry. Our goal is to encourage the collaboration of educational institutes.

The Institute informs round a big number of subjects, World journalism from 1966 till today, Completed articles,  articles. SBC has approximately 3.500 e-journals, text books and (full-text) e-books. Practical handbooks of World Congresses, CITATION INDEXES, Online connections with Academic centres in the United States and Germany. The library participates in NILDE an online service that allows libraries to request and supply documents in a reciprocal manner. The library participates in EIFL ,working in collaboration with libraries in more than 60 developing and transition countries in Africa, Asia, Europe, and Latin America, EIFL enables access to knowledge for education, learning, research and sustainable community development.The library also participates in ILL SBN, a cooperative interlibrary loan and document delivery national library service (Servizio Bibliotecario Nazionale - SBN), the network Italian libraries promoted by the Ministry of Cultural Heritage and Activities and Tourism, with the cooperation of regional administrations and universities. It is coordinated by the Central Institute for the Union Catalogue of Italian Libraries and for Bibliographic Information (ICCU).

Name: Aloha Community Library Association
Description:
The Aloha Community Library Association was founded in 2011 by community members to address the lack of public library services in the Aloha-Reedville area of unincorporated Washington County, Oregon.

The library is a 501(c)(3) non-profit association providing public library resources and programs for the economically diverse community of Aloha. Some of our most popular offerings are free computer and internet access, volunteer tech tutors, a Read to the Dogs program for children, family story time program, and a book club for adults.

Volunteers are at the heart of the library, providing over 300 hours of volunteer support every month. Volunteers perform materials check in and shelving, serve on the board of directors, offer special programs, and manage the fundraising book sales and online sales program.

The library is now a member of Washington County Cooperative Library Services, but as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, continues to rely on donations, grants, fundraising, and book sales to meet the growing demand for library services in the Aloha community.

The North Logan Library offers the following:

MakerSpace: Empowering People to CREATE. A project media lab where you can scan photos, slides and negatives; transfer VHS to DVD or vinyl records to MP3; create/edit movies or screencasts; organize family history or capture oral histories. What will you make? 

Kindle Library: A collection of E-books and audiobooks that are loaded onto a set of Kindles and made available to check out just like a regular book. Each Kindle can hold hundreds of titles so instead of checking out only the latest title in a series, you can have the whole series at your fingertips.

Lynda.com: Do you want to master web design, photoshop, video editing or management? Learn by doing and follow the step-by-step video tutorials from Lynda.com. Library card holders may access all of anywhere, anytime. Don’t have a Library card? Visit us and access the videos on our public use computers or on your own device using the Library wifi.

Check-Out a GoPro and capture your next adventure!

The LLAGNY CLE Research Programming Service develops and offers continuing legal education programs with a research component for bar associations and related organizations by specifically customizing in-house research programs that fill the educational needs of your attorneys. LLAGNY fosters the profession by offering its members knowledge, leadership, and community that make the whole legal system stronger. 

LLAGNY is a Chapter of American Association of Law Libraries.

Name: The Swan Books on Wheels
Description:
We are Mrs. Kameswari Susarla along with our daughter, the founders of The Swan Books on Wheels, based in Bangalore duly supported by Mr. Shrini Susarla, husband and mentor. You people are following us for quite a time now, so we feel that it’s high time that we officially introduce ourselves and the main motive of our page.

(This post can be a bit long but we feel that it’s important for our audience to know who we are and why you should follow us. So please read till the end)

Firstly, thank you so much to everyone for supporting us. After understanding a very genuine problem faced by readers, we opened our mobile library. We understand that due to prevailing conditions and also because of age factors, many old age readers and even children are not able to get access to libraries and bookstores. Many people also don’t have much time to physically go to libraries and bookstores to borrow and buy books.

Hence, this genuine problem of readers has given birth to the idea of our mobile library, a library that forever belongs to its readers.

Our main motive is to make reading more accessible and empowering to all the people out there. No matter whether you’re a parent, old age person, children, corporate, worker, housewife, etc. the doors of The Swan Books are always open for you.

We are planning to open more branches of The Swan Books in other cities. We will be conducting various activities to cater to readers from all walks of life.

So if you’re one who wants to start your reading journey or if you’re are an avid reader who wants a comfortable space to discuss, borrow and buy books believe us The Swan Books is your forever go-to place.

We hope we all can read and grow together

Name: Lawyers à la dérive
Description:
Au cours de mon ascension professionnelle, à chaque étage où l’ascenseur s’arrêtait, je m’attendais à ce que les portes s’ouvrent sur un parterre de personnalités toutes plus brillantes les unes que les autres, qui auraient tôt fait de justifier le syndrome de l’imposteur dont je souffrais.
Or, à mon grand étonnement, je retrouvais, invariablement, à chaque étage, le même nombre d’opportunistes, de chanceux et de pistonnés, entourés de quelques personnes qui les surclassaient, mais qui n’étaient pas, fondamentalement, plus brillantes que celles qui sortaient du lot aux étages inférieurs.
Je comprenais alors que, plus que le talent, les facilités et le travail, la réussite professionnelle dépend d’autres facteurs : une détermination sans faille, une assurance implacable, et être capable, sans scrupules, de déloyauté, souvent, de cruauté, parfois.

Avocat d’affaires, Nicholas Beaulieu partage avec vous son expérience de Managing Partner du bureau parisien d’un cabinet d’avocats international de premier plan, un univers où se mêlent jeux de pouvoir et ambitions personnelles. Un mélange de 99 francs et de Wall Street dans l’univers du droit.

Name: Wirtschaftsclub im Literaturhaus Stuttgart
Description:
Der Wirtschaftsclub im Literaturhaus Stuttgart e.V. bringt Führungskräfte aus Wirtschaft, Kunst und Kultur zusammen und ermuntert zum Blickwechsel. Wer über Monitore, Timer und Konferenztische hinausblicken will, wer Wert darauf legt, sich seine Perspektive nicht unnötig einengen zu lassen, kann gar nicht darauf verzichten, sich mit Philosophie, Kunst, Literatur und anderen Themen zu befassen. Themen, die ja nicht nur Einfluss auf diejenigen haben, die sich ganz dem Schöngeistigen verschrieben haben.

„Für Entscheider in der Wirtschaft ist es heute wichtiger denn je, zu begreifen, dass es nicht nur Bilanzen sind, die die Welt zusammenhalten“, sagt Jörg Menno Harms, ehemaliger Vorstand des Wirtschaftsclubs im Literaturhaus Stuttgart e.V. und ehemaliger Chef von Hewlett Packard Deutschland „Gerade in der Welt der Wirtschaft brauchen wir mehr Phantasie und Kreativität, um Unternehmen und Mitarbeiter zu entwickeln und neue Perspektiven zu gewinnen.“

Mit dem Business-Lunch und Business-Dinner, jeden 2. Donnerstag im Monat, sowie öffentlichen thematischen Abendveranstaltungen bietet sich die Möglichkeit zum Austausch und zu interessanten Begegnungen. Seit 2009 vergibt der Club einen Literatupreis, der seit 2022 den Namen „Péter Horváth-Literaturpreis des Wirtschaftsclubs im Literaturhaus Stuttgart“ trägt: eine Auszeichnung für Autoren, die sich kritisch und literarisch anspruchsvoll mit Themen der Wirtschafts- bzw. Arbeitswelt auseinandersetzen. Zu den Preisträgern zählen Annette Pehnt, Felicia Zeller, Soeren Voima, Beqë Cufaj, Martin Schäuble, Matthias Nawrat, Martin Suter, Philipp Schönthaler, Arno Frank und Reinhard Kaier-Mühlecker.

Sus principales lineamientos orgánicos tienden a:

1. Jerarquizar la profesión bibliotecaria situándola en el status que le concierne, por la seriedad de sus estudios y proyección educativa

2. Defensa de los intereses propios del gremio, en todo lo relacionado a la carrera bibliotecaria, intrínsecamente: estabilidad y escalafón

3. Auspiciar el perfeccionamiento técnico administrativo de las bibliotecas, y favorecer su difusión como elemento inmediato de influencia cultural.

4. Establecer vinculación con otras asociaciones similares e integrar Federaciones y Asociaciones de Bibliotecarios, participando en seminarios y congresos.

Name: Lulu Memorial Library
Description:
Did you know that storybooks form the building blocks for language and critical literacy skills?  Research shows that storybooks stimulate the mind, spark creativity and imagination, and enhances critical thinking translating into academic success. Yet, most public primary schools in Kenya do not have libraries.
Libraries provide an informal set-up for children to make a connection with what they are reading, freely explore and imagine. The consistent interaction with storybooks coupled with the freedom of self-expression enables children to build a correlation between stories and real-life experiences, positioning them for lifelong learning. Lack of access to libraries not only denies children the gift of imagination but also the gift of dreaming and the desire to be more than that which is within their reach.

School libraries are more than just buildings that children are obligated to go to during library lessons. They are safe havens that serve as an escape from the harsh realities of life. They are a collection of stories that expose children to a world beyond their homes and communities. They are a symbol of hope that motivate children to continue striving despite all odds.

The Lulu Memorial Library project is a tribute in honour of a little girl who loved to read. LML serves as a centre of excellence and inspiration towards promoting a reading culture among children with limited or no access to books.

The LML board identified Korowe primary school in Nyando constituency, Kisumu county as a most at need public educational facility that promises the highest impact. Kisumu County has three libraries covering the vast student population.

Name: Whitefish Bay Public Library
Description:
Our Story
The Whitefish Bay Public Library. Established 1937. 

Re-established every day.

A library that is just about lending books seems so last century, doesn’t it?

That’s our philosophy at the Whitefish Bay Public Library, anyway. We believe that everything that we do, we do to make a difference in someone’s life. Creating connections. Building community. Collecting stories. Sure, we have books, but we’re SO much more.

We want our library to be a place of discovery and possibility that enriches your life … and we intend to give you the best service you’ve ever received. A friendly smile when you walk through our doors? Done. Treating you with the respect you deserve? Done. Offering plenty of ways to learn and interact with our community and entertain yourself? Done. A warm, welcoming space to spend some time? A website that keeps you informed about every fun and inspiring thing we’ve got going on? Done and done.

And don’t worry, we still have those serene spaces that you love when you want a bit of quiet.

The Whitefish Bay Public Library has been around since 1937, originally in the basement of the old Village Hall. A continuing theme of our library is how much our community has supported us … we’ve outgrown two locations in 80 years, at one point stuffing 55,000 items into a building designed to hold only 32,000! We moved into our current location in 2002, which was almost three times the size of our previous building and provided much more space for collections, study rooms, public spaces and community gatherings.

We’re proud of our lovely library and our incredible, talented staff. We’re especially proud of our successful history as a part of the Whitefish Bay community. But most importantly, we believe that our future together will be even more exciting. Come be part of it. 

Excite Your Mind.

Name: Kloth - Kernow Library Of Things
Description:
A Library of Things works like a normal library. But instead of borrowing books, you can borrow tools, kitchen equipment, camping gear in fact, pretty much anything. The kind of things that you might not own, only use occasionally, or perhaps, can’t afford. 

We want to move away from the idea that in order to use things we have to own them and having a Library of Things means that everyone in a community shares stuff as and when they need to, instead of storing things away in homes.

This year has shown more than ever how important community is. And supporting our project also helps as we hurtle ever closer to climate breakdown. Because a Library of Things also means buying less, repairing more and having greater respect for resources, our environment and our planet. 

In September, with help from Penryn Town Council, we opened the first KLOTH branch in Penryn. Local people have already donated some incredible things. Now, we’re crowdfunding to keep the project running and build up the library with some really useful, but expensive, things.

Description:
Osrednja knjižnica Srečka Vilharja Koper je osrednja območna knjižnica in deluje na območju Mestne občine Koper. Naloge območnosti izvaja za obalnokraško knjižnično regijo. Regijo sestavljajo območja delovanja naslednjih knjižnic: Mestna knjižnica Izola, Knjižnica Makse Samsa Ilirska Bistrica, Kosovelova knjižnica Sežana, Mestna knjižnica Piran in Knjižnica Bena Zupančiča Postojna.

Celoten fond knjižnice obsega gotovo 300.000 enot gradiva: monografskih in serijskih publikacij, avdiovizualnega in drugega gradiva. V Osrednji knjižnici Srečka Vilharja Koper delujejo poleg uprave ter službe za nabavo in obdelavo knjižnižnega gradiva tudi Študijski oddelek, Oddelek za mlade bralce, Domoznanski oddelek, Služba za izvajanje dejavnosti iz naslova območnosti, Oddelek za italijaniko, Potujoča knjižnica, knjigoveznica ter enoti v mestnih predelih Semedela in Markovec.

Poleg že ustaljenih knjižničnih uslug (izposoja na dom, čitalnice itd.), bralcem nudimo referalne informacije iz računalniških baz podatkov knjižničnega fonda institucij vključenih v COBISS (Cooperative Online Bibiliographic System and Services), kar meddrugim omogoča, da s pomočjo medknjižnične izposoje v zelo kratkem času bralcem priskrbimo gradivo tudi iz drugih knjižnic po Sloveniji in iz tujine.

Als zentrale Bibliothek ist sie für die landesweite Literaturversorgung der staatlichen Hochschulen in Bremen und Bremerhaven zuständig. Für die Freie Hansestadt Bremen versieht sie die Aufgaben einer Landesbibliothek. Im Zentrum des Universitätscampus gelegen, ist sie die zentrale bibliothekarische Einrichtung der Universität.

Sie besteht aus einer großen Zentralbibliothek und einigen dezentralen Bereichsbibliotheken, die bestimmten Fachbereichen bzw. Studiengängen räumlich unmittelbar zugeordnet sind.

An den Hochschulen stehen den Studierenden und Wissenschaftlern Teilbibliotheken zur Verfügung. Somit ist die SuUB an insgesamt 9 Standorten in Bremen und Bremerhaven vertreten.

Die Sammlungsschwerpunkte dienen der aktuellen Literaturversorgung von Forschung und Lehre und richten sich nach den Wissenschaftsschwerpunkten der Bremer Hochschulen.

Name: The Antique Motorcycle Foundation, Inc.
Description:
The mission of the Antique Motorcycle Foundation is to immerse the public, the media and the industry into the captivating world of vintage motorcycles and to establish appreciation, inspire active engagement, and promote support of the AMF and of the vintage motorcycle community.

Our vision is to cultivate passion for vintage motorcycles, facilitate advocacy for vintage motorcycling and create vintage motorcycling enthusiasts among new audiences and a new generation of riders.


Values:

- Passionate about the machines, the culture and the importance of vintage motorcycle preservation
- Eager to help others discover their own emotional connection with vintage motorcycling 
- Inventive in how we bring a compelling vintage motorcycling story to new audiences
- Youthful in our energy, attitude and approach (and ultimately, in our demographic)
- Confident about the future and our ability to secure it


Goals:

- Be the premier and impartial advocate for the organizations and businesses that support the vintage motorcycle community
- Secure and assure the financial and in-kind resources needed to support and sustain the mission, vision, initiatives and activities of the Antique Motorcycle Foundation 
- Leverage public exhibits to tell the AMF story boldly and with contextual relevance 
- Engage and enlighten the public about the significant and stimulating role motorcycling has played in culture throughout modern history
- Grow enthusiasm and passion among multi-generational audiences to assure the ongoing engagement in and enduring commitment to the world of vintage motorcycles and motorcycling 
- Promote evangelism among enthusiasts in the interest of attracting new blood to the vintage motorcycle community

Name: bookpal.in
Description:
BookPal is a new generation community library chain that provides a unique reading experience to book lovers with a wide range of books for every type of reader Toddlers to teens, Dabblers to Bookworms under a modern, vibrant ambience with effective use of technology. Like we love saying – At BookPal, there is a book for everyone in the family Proven practices from the retail industry, like warehouse backed operations with centralized inventory and some new generation technology usage like cloud hosted infrastructure with RFID enabled touch points combine to meet the ultimate goal of every library – Get every book a reader and get every reader his/her book Started by a team of Retail and Technology professionals, BookPal aspires to be the country’s first networked pan Indian community library chain catering to Indian reading requirements. 

Profile 
With thousands of books across different categories, BookPal offers a wide range of titles to its members in any age group. BookPal revolutionizes some of the routine book circulation activities of a library by introducing RFID based unmanned kiosks for members to choose a book and check-out from the library without any time consuming manual processing. Extending beyond the traditional book centric cataloguing and classification functions of a library, BookPal offers a range of reader centric services to its members including free door delivery, personalized reading recommendations and the luxury of maintaining a personal Read-Next-Register to queue up all the titles that a member wishes to read. 

Collections
The truly amazing range of Indian & International titles in the Toddler-to-Teen is specially designed to entertain and educate the young readers. For readers who prefer their regular dose of Fiction, BookPal offers a good mix of popular bestsellers as well as critically acclaimed, award winning titles. If you enjoy relating to your roots, the Regional collection of BookPal may bring back memories of your past.

Name: LDbaseRepo
Description: Welcome to LDbase, a NIH-funded collaboration between researchers and librarians to build a first-of-its-kind behavioral data repository containing decades of knowledge from educational and developmental sciences on individuals across the full range of abilities. LDbase is an open science resource for the educational and developmental science scientific communities, providing a secure place to store and access data, as well access materials about aspects of data management and analyses. LDbase is unique in that it is project oriented, rather than dataset oriented, meeting the needs of how our field thinks about data. What we mean by that is LDbase is hierarchical and flexible. Investigators start with a project page, and from there can add data, code, and documentation in any way that works for their work, including any level of nesting. Any dataset, code, and document can be added as a unique upload, or link to an external webpage. Every project, dataset, code, and document gets a unique citation, including doi if requested. The aim of LDbase is to be a powerful resource that opens up new areas of research and accelerates discoveries, inspiring innovative research that helps us understand how individuals learn, develop, are different from each other, learn new languages, respond to interventions, and interact with their communities.

Description:
Indian Valley Public Library, 100 East Church Ave, Telford PA 18969.

The IVPL reflects our Indian Valley community.  We value the positive impact our public library has on our children.  We value literacy and promoting a love of reading.  We value respect for each individual person and recognize the need to serve all.  We value community and the potential for our library to strengthen our democracy by inviting civic engagement and civil discussions.

The library serves five communities of the Souderton Area School District:  Franconia, Lower Salford, Salford, Souderton, and Telford.
 IVPL is an independent member of the Montgomery County Library District.  As an Access PA participant, the library serves all Pennsylvania residents under the Access-PA program.

Name: The Women's Library, Sydney
Description:
The Women's Library is a specialist lending library and community space in inner Sydney, Australia. This community-based library with its collection of books for, by, and about women, is a hub of feminist and lesbian activity. 

The Women's Library has been built on the efforts of volunteers and the donations of thousands of women since its establishment in 1992. It is fully managed and staffed by volunteers and the collection of donated books, journals, and DVDs numbers approximately 20,000 items.

Annual membership is open to women and women's groups that support the library's aims. The Women's Library gratefully acknowledges the support of the City of Sydney.

Name: NYU Health Sciences Library
Description: The mission of the NYU Health Sciences Library is to enhance learning, research and patient care by managing knowledge-based resources, providing client-centered information services and education, and extending access through new initiatives in information technology.

Name: Bibliotheek Leidschendam-Voorburg
Description:
Bibliotheek Leidschendam-Voorburg is een openbare bibliotheek in de gemeente Leidschendam-Voorburg met vestigingen in Leidschendam, Voorburg en Stompwijk.
Missie: Bibliotheek Leidschendam-Voorburg wil de zelfontplooiing onder alle lagen van de bevolking van Leidschendam-Voorburg bevorderen. 
Dit doet zij door mogelijkheden daartoe laagdrempelig toegankelijk te maken én door zelfontwikkeling actief te stimuleren bij groepen waarvoor dit niet vanzelfsprekend is. Door zelfontplooiing te stimuleren, wil de BLV bijdragen aan de sociale cohesie in de gemeente Leidschendam-Voorburg.

Name: Ghana Learning Project
Description:
Ghana Learning Project is a voluntary initiative formed in 2008.
We are committed to providing schools in Ghana with educational support and resources, primarily through collecting and transporting books from the UK.

We are currently looking to carry out our 2nd annual book collection, having successfully transported and distributed over 5,000 books amongst Ghanaian schools in July 2009.

Name: Stark Library Foundation
Description: Stark Library Foundation was founded in 2006 by a group of passionate volunteers determined to financially support the mission of Stark Library. SLF funds support library initiatives that strengthen early literacy, lifelong learning, and community engagement. The Foundation is tax-exempt as described in Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code.

Name: Tameside BIPC
Description:
Tameside BIPC offers free access to business and intellectual property databases and information, including market research, company data and information on intellectual property (IP), and organises learning and networking opportunities for local business.

We also deliver the Build a Business project within Tameside Libraries, offering workshops to entrepreneurs and existing businesses on how to start a successful business and develop skills such as customer research and marketing.

For our local events, visit: www.eventbrite.co.uk/cc/business-at-tameside-libraries-1457279

For information on Greater Manchester BIPC and the Build a Business Project, visit bipcgm.org and buildabusinessgm.org

Name: Cary Area Public Library District
Description: We are a public library helping our community by providing educational and recreational programming and materials for all ages.

Name: Bookthisbook
Description:
About Bookthisbook 

Bookthisbook is an online book rental platform that gives its customers a choice from a wide variety of rental books. The readers get new as well as used books with the facility of a vast catalog, fast and reliable delivery and convenient online shopping. The user benefits from a safe and secure online ordering experience along with the convenient payment option of cash on delivery. We deal in books belonging to various categories such as fiction, non-fiction, management, cookery, religion, astrology, history, children etc. Book readers will also find the idea of renting books to read as appealing as they won’t have to shell out the entire cost of the books to read them.


What’s different?

Most of the major eCommerce websites today are retailers. What separates Bookthisbook from its competition is that we provide books on rent. Online book renting is a relatively new concept in India and with the right promotion, we feel that book readers will also find the idea appealing. Giving books on rent will help our readers enjoy reading without burdening their pockets. We also help our readers to discover books from lesser known authors. This helps readers to read a wide variety of books that they usually might not read.


The Idea 

Bookthisbook is an online book rental platform that gives its customers a choice from a wide variety of rental books. The readers get new as well as used books with the facility of a vast catalogue, fast and reliable delivery and convenient online shopping. The user benefits from a safe and secure online ordering experience along with the convenient payment option of cash on delivery. We deal in books belonging to various categories such as fiction, non-fiction, management, cookery, religion, astrology, history, children etc. Book readers will also find the idea of renting books to read as appealing as they won’t have to shell out the entire cost of the books to read them.

Name: Kalambaka Library - KDK
Description:
Kalambaka Educational, Literary and Cultural Civil Not for Profit Company (KDK) renovated and refurbished the Municipal Library, located in the city center. The renovation project started in Summer 2013 and the new Library opened in Summer 2015. Through an agreement with the Municipality, KDK will manage and operate the new Library for a minimum of 5 and up to a maximum of 15 years, when the Library will revert to the management of the Municipality.
The new Library is a cultural anchor in the city, with a special focus on the needs of children and teens. There are 45 schools in the municipality of Kalambaka of which 14 are located in Kalambaka town. KDK’s goal, based on a comprehensive business plan, is for the Library to become a hub of creativity and opportunity, preparing the next generation of Kalambakiotes, as well as the population of the surrounding area, to thrive in the global future.
Please join KDK to help make this vision a reality. Enter our site for more information.

Name: Kankakee Public Library
Description: For over one hundred years, the Kankakee Public Library has been meeting the information needs of the residents of the City of Kankakee.

Name: Literature of War Foundation
Description:
The Literature of War Foundation was founded to get relevant, quality literature into the hands of our troops, both active duty and reserve. We facilitate free programming for those men and women who are currently serving in uniform.  We aim to foster an intellectually stimulating environment within the ranks by supporting free book clubs so that our servicemen and women are better prepared to serve their country. 

We are an all-volunteer, tax-exempt organization under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code (EIN 87-400-9027).

Name: Boyertown Community Library
Description: Boyertown Community Library is a non-profit organization that provides access to educational and recreational materials to encourage independent learning and life-long reading enjoyment. BCL offers programs for all ages and provides Wi-Fi, computer access, copies, fax and processes passport applications.

Name: Malta Township Public Library
Description: Public library serving the Malta Township community in Illinois.

Name: Thailand Knowledge Park
Description: Thailand Knowledge Park or TK Park was founded as a "prototype" of a knowledge hub for children, youth and the public. Aiming to respond to the pursuit of knowledge and lifelong learning, TK Park opted for the concept of "Living Library", created the learning space that fully equips with books, activity programmes and a variety of learning media. With "ongoing", "diverse" and "respond to need" reading activities and learning workshops, TK Park has become a crucial ingredient in thousands of children and young people's learning experience, a powerful force to build the innovative future learning ecosystem and make Thailand to become a "real" learning society.

Name: Novels from Nowhere
Description: This is the official page of Novels from Nowhere. All novels are written by one Author/ Mark M. Isaac, the first sequel available is "The Markenstein Legend". Action, adventures, war, romance, magic & legends all can be found in the sequel and in the up next novels. Follow us on our websites and Facebook page for more info and for the novels pdf files.

Name: Garrett Public Library
Description:
Mission: We connect, empower, engage, and honor our community.
Vision: To be an essential part of our community’s cultural, social, and economic wellbeing where people share and learn from each other.
Core Values:
Welcoming: We provide an inviting space; give friendly, helpful, and compassionate service, and share the belief that everyone counts and is treated with respect.
Knowledgeable: We respond to the needs of our patrons in an efficient, professional, and confidential manner.
Accessible: We strive to quickly provide patrons the collections and resources they need in formats they want.
Inclusive: We offer equitable access to materials, services, and resources on a wide diversity of topics and issues.
Rooted: We respect our long-standing tradition of Library services as we continue to grow and learn with our community.

Name: Sociological collection of the Metropolitan Ervin Szabó Library, Budapest
Description: The Sociological collection of the Metropolitan Ervin Szabó Library in Budapest is the biggest sociological collection in Hungarian Sociology. The department creates the bibliography of Hungarian sociology  /1900-1929, 1970-/. In the Sociological reading room there are ca. 10.000 books, reference books and journals in different languages. The website of the Sociological collection offers remote acces to the most important sociological databases, online and e-book collections for the registered users. Librarians of the department regularly take tutor programs for the library users.

Name: Library Center of the Ozarks
Description: Library Center of the Ozark’s rich history began in 1933, when a group of Presbyterian women united to form a study club. Motivated by a desire to provide books to the surrounding area, these women formed Taneyhills Library after receiving a donated copy of The Shepherd of the Hills. Taneyhills Library served the Taney County community as a private library for decades until January 2, 2024, when it became Library Center of the Ozarks, Taney County’s first public library. LCO now serves the Branson and Hollister communities by providing books, essential public resources, and engaging programs.

Name: Llibreria Els nou rals SCCL
Description: Som una cooperativa de treball per fer de la llibreria @Elsnourals un projecte que aposta pel comerç de proximitat i vol dinamitzar culturalment #Viladecans

Name: King County Law Library
Description:
The King County Law Library is open to the public to help serve the needs of those in King County. 

Without access to information there is no justice. King County Law Library aids all persons with their need for legal information by providing legal materials, training, education, and services in a welcoming and positive environment.

Name: Biblioteca delle Nuvole
Description: La Biblioteca delle Nuvole è la biblioteca del Comune di Perugia specializzata in fumetto e illustrazione, e una delle più grandi biblioteche italiane del settore. Il suo patrimonio di oltre 70.000 volumi – tra fumetti, manga, riviste specializzate, monografie, raccolte, libri illustrati – è gestito dai volontari dell’Associazione Umbria Fumetto.

Name: Sayville Library
Description: Sayville Library is a libraries company based out of 11 Collins Ave, Sayville, New York, United States.

Name: Florence - Lauderdale Public Library
Description: The Florence-Lauderdale Public Library is committed to developing quality programs, materials circulation, enrichment classes, and community relationships. Our knowledgeable staff of librarians, specialists, archivists, and volunteers are eager to help and share their expertise. FLPL has a used bookstore operated by the Friends of the Library and a cafe, Bookmarks Coffee Shop. Both use their proceeds to support the maintenance and growth of the library.

Name: COLLECTORS CLUB OF CHICAGO
Description: Our focus is on the documentation, preservation, advancement and promotion of philately through education, study, research and services. We are an all-volunteer, nonprofit organization comprised of advanced stamp collectors and postal historians from Chicago and nationwide.

Name: Rise Institute For Medical Education
Description:
RISE is a Nepali-registered organization that serves as a common platform where doctors and medical students can explore educational and career opportunities across different medical disciplines in different countries. In order to meet the new demands of globalized world, health care professionals are challenged to stay on the cutting edge of technological practice to provide the best services possible. Wehn RISE established 12 years ago it instantly became a dream destination for students and doctors alike; a place where creativity and cutting edge knowledge were being combined in new and existing ways for the benefit of NEpal and the medical community as a whole.

Now, 12 years later, some of Nepal's leading doctors, enterpreneurs and managers have further improved RISE with a new set of farsighted and innovative goals and objectives for the benefit of medical professionals everywhere.

Name: Salem-South Lyon District Library
Description: A Class IVsmall district library located in beautifully rural Salem Twp. We think big, imagine the possibilities and take our mission to educate, enrich, and empower our community very seriously. We provide exceptional customer service and link our community with resources.  Our library is also a destination spot: visitors come to experience our beautiful flower gardens surrounding our building and maintained by over 40 volumteers.

Name: Caroline County Public Library
Description:
The Caroline County Public Library brings people, information and ideas together to enrich lives, increase knowledge, and build community. 

The library is our Community Hub. The library is the one institution that is available to all citizens, free of charge, politically unaffiliated, and dedicated to learning for empowerment, growth and change.

Name: Livraria Portuguesa de Macau
Description:
Livraria dedicada à promoção, divulgação e comercialização de iniciativas editoriais redigidas em língua portuguesa. Destaque para obras em língua estrangeira relacionadas com a língua e cultura portuguesas.
Espaço multifuncional com galeria vocacionada para a difusão da arte e cultura de Macau e do espaço lusófono.

Propriedade do Instituto Português do Oriente, a Livraria Portuguesa fica situada na zona nobre da cidade de Macau e é um espaço de referência para turistas e habitantes de Macau.

Name: Otis Library
Description:
We are the public library serving Norwich, Connecticut. We are a full service library. Our mission: 

"Otis Library provides free and open access to information, ideas, and services that anticipate our community’s personal, educational, and professional needs. The library enriches our region by maintaining a safe and welcoming environment and by offering resources that promote lifelong learning."

Name: Irama Nusantara
Description: A nonprofit foundation dedicated to preserving and sharing Indonesia’s popular music history. Founded in 2013, it focuses on digitizing and researching materials from the nation’s recording industry, while making them accessible through an online archive, exhibitions, and publications. Through collaboration with communities and institutions, Irama Nusantara works to keep Indonesia’s musical heritage alive as part of the country’s cultural memory and ongoing creative history.

Name: Groton Public Library
Description: Welcome to the Groton Public Library. Look for programs and our library catalog at www.grotonpl.org

Name: Partnership Among South Carolina Academic Libraries (PASCAL)
Description: PASCAL is the Partnership Among South Carolina Academic Libraries. Serving over 230,000 students at 55 colleges and universities, PASCAL supports higher education in South Carolina through multiple programs including a Shared Library Services Platform for 53 institutions; statewide rapid resource sharing service; core electronic resources, including a robust shared e-book program; and collaborative collections. In addition to our key programs, PASCAL provides training and support to member libraries and seeks to foster greater collaboration within the South Carolina academic library community.

Name: Halifax Tool Library
Description:
The Halifax Tool Library aims to provide residents and community groups with everything they need to make projects happen. An electronic inventory system allows us to automatically track tools and display their availability online.

Who Needs a Tool Library?
Apartment dwellers and anyone who doesn’t have space to store tools. Students and temporary residents who would hesitate to invest in a large collection. Anyone who wants to try something new without spending hundreds on tools. You!

What Does it Cost?
Members pay an annual fee of $75, which gives them free access to all of our tools.

Who are we?
A bunch of enthusiastic people who want to make community projects easier.

The Tool Library is a registered non-profit society.

Name: Biblioteca Popular del Paraná
Description:
La Biblioteca Popular del Paraná es una asociación civil sin fines de lucro. Fundada en 1873, es una de las instituciones de promoción de la cultura más antigua de la República Argentina.

Ofrece el servicio de biblioteca pública en forma gratuita y constituye un lugar de encuentro para la sociedad civil de Paraná y Entre Ríos: cada año se realizan más de 100 presentaciones artísticas, culturales y educativas; y varias asociaciones civiles con los más diversos intereses tienen sede en su edificio.

La actual sede social de la Biblioteca fue inaugurada en mayo de 1910 y fue declarada "Monumento Histórico Nacional" en el año 2006 (Ley del Congreso Nacional Nro. 26.116).

Name: Psik
Description: Psik delivers a vast selection of non-fiction and prose e-books alongside digital magazines, to the academic realm and educational institutions. Our digital library brings thousands of high-quality literary resources, enriching the learning experience for students and scholars alike.

Name: Wood Library
Description: Wood Library provides exceptional public library service in a comfortable and safe environment where people can satisfy their curiosity, stimulate their imaginations, and connect to the local community and to the world.

Name: SOLINET
Description: Provider of library products and services

Name: Fort Frances Public Library Technology Centre
Description: A public library serving Fort Frances and the Rainy River District.

Name: Editorial Fonoll
Description:
L’Editorial Fonoll som una editorial independent ubicada a Juneda, a la comarca de les Garrigues (Lleida). Entre olivers i arbres fruiters, publiquem uns llibres que es nodreixen del territori i que cerquen el rigor i la qualitat dels textos i formats.

Les nostres arrels van fixar-se l’any 1997 quan, des de l’Associació Cultural Fonoll, un grup de persones compromeses amb el poble, la cultura i el país va decidir crear un segell editorial propi que recollís i participés d’una llarga tradició de publicacions locals. Des d’aquells inicis, el segell editorial va publicar literatura de proximitat, primer llibres de caire local i després també comarcal.

Name: Stowarzyszenie LABiB
Description:
LABiB to społeczność osób, które dostrzegają potencjał bibliotek i chcą go rozwijać będąc aktywnymi przewodnikami tego procesu. Działają, mają pomysły, realizują projekty. Chcą zaszczepić entuzjazm w innych.
Od stycznia 2019 działamy jako “Stowarzyszenie LABiB”.

Członkowie Stowarzyszenia projektują działania wspierające środowisko bibliotekarskie oraz kontynuują projekty, które rozpoczęto w latach ubiegłych, a które okazały się dużym sukcesem.
Wśród naszych projektów znajdują się:

BiblioLABy – lokalne zjazdy bibliotekarzy
program „Re-Animuj się”, w ramach którego Ambasadorowie LABiBu prowadzą szkolenia dla bibliotek będących laureatami konkursu
projekt “Praktyka dla praktyka” polegający międzybibliotecznych wymianach pracowników (http://praktykadlapraktyka.pl/)
“12 miesięcy tematycznych z labib.pl” – cykl webinariów i artykułów dotyczących tematów bliskich bibliotekarzom.
LABiB.pl to również portal społecznościowy stworzony dla ludzi pracujących w społecznościach lokalnych, nastawiony na wymianę praktycznej wiedzy i pomnażanie pomysłów. Portal daje możliwość zainspirowania się działaniami innych oraz dzielenia się swoimi doświadczeniami. Stanowi przestrzeń spotkań, poszukiwania rozwiązań i wspólnych eksperymentów. 
Stowarzyszenie Labib działa na rzecz bibliotekarek i bibliotekarzy, bibliotek i ich użytkowników w myśl zasady Dziel się wiedzą, mnóż pomysły. Jesteśmy aktywni, pełni entuzjazmu, gotowi na wyzwania i otwarci na współpracę oraz dzielenie się swoimi doświadczeniami.

LABiB powstał jako liderski program rozwoju dla pracowników bibliotek stworzony przez FRSI. Obecnie nazwa ta określa z jednej strony portal społecznościowy dla bibliotekarzy, labib.pl, który służy po to, by się wzajemnie wspierać, dzielić wiedzą i inspirować. Z drugiej strony jest to określenie sieci bibliotekarzy, dla których biblioteka to miejsce szczególne. Którzy dostrzegają jej potencjał, działają, mają pomysły, realizują projekty. Chcą zaszczepić entuzjazm w innych.

Name: Parkersburg & Wood County Public Library
Description: Your community library for work, study, and play. With a central library in Parkersburg and additional facilities in South Parkersburg, Williamstown, and Waverly, West Virginia, we are committed to offering the best resources to connect you with both the past and the future. We are a gathering place and gateway to enhance the quality of life of our community by fostering personal growth, learning opportunities, the joy of reading, and exploration of our stories.

Name: North Kingstown Free Library
Description: The North Kingstown Free Library is a municipal public library situated in a park-like, waterfront setting in North Kingstown, Rhode Island.  We exist to meet the changing and enduring cultural, educational, informational, recreational and research needs of our patrons.

Name: Hester J Hodgdon Libraries for All
Description: The Hester J. Hodgdon Libraries for all Foundation (HJH Foundation) supports sustainable community development and educational opportunities in rural Nicaraguan communities. Our public lending library and Mobile Project in San Juan del Sur, in collaboration with Rotary International and other service organizations, develop programs to strengthen local schools, support community development, and leadership, and offer educational and trade learning opportunities. The HJH Libraries for All Foundation is a Colorado-based, tax-exempt, charitable foundation that leverages the power of education and literacy to enhance community vitality.

Name: Gloucestershire Libraries
Description:
We’re the network of 32 council-run libraries and 8 community libraries across Gloucestershire.

From our nationally funded projects to our elaborately diverse events programme, Gloucestershire Libraries aim to better connect, support and engage our local community. 

Current projects include Ageing with Creativity, Space to Create and Family Focus. We also run a Greener Together initiative which focuses on building sustainability into our processes.

Want to discuss a partnership, collaboration or host an event in one of our spaces? Email us!

 libraries.development@gloucestershire.gov.uk

Name: WEST CHESTER LIBRARY ASSOCIATION WEST CHESTER PUBLIC LIBRARY
Description: WEST CHESTER PUBLIC LIBRARY is a public library located at 415 N CHURCH ST, WEST CHESTER, Pennsylvania, United States.

Name: ABF Franche-Comté
Description:
Voici la page du Groupe régional de l'ABF Franche-Comté. Vous y trouverez notre veille professionnelle, toute l'actualité du métier, l'emploi, les informations essentielles concernant les bibliothèques comtoises. 

Fondée en 1906 et reconnue d'utilité publique en 1969, l'ABF est la plus ancienne association de bibliothécaires en France. L'ABF est l’association de tou·te·s les bibliothécaires professionnel·le·s et bénévoles qui réfléchissent, débattent, se forment et promeuvent le rôle des bibliothèques dans la société.

2 000 adhérent·e·s : professionnel·le·s des bibliothèques ou concerné·e·s par le livre et l’univers numérique ainsi que les différents supports de culture et d’information (libraires, éditeur·rice·s, prestataires de services…), établissements ou collectivités (bibliothèques, centres de documentation, municipalités…). 

Un conseil national, composé des président·e·s des groupes régionaux qui définit la politique de l'association et élit le bureau et le·la président·e.

21 groupes régionaux qui mènent des actions de proximité en phase avec les spécificités de chaque région. 

17 commission thématiques : AccessibilitéS, Advocacy, Bibliothèques universitaires/Bibliothèques spécialisées, Bibliothèques en réseau, Comité d'éthique, Écologie, Formation, International, Jeunesse, Jeux en bibliothèque, Labenbib, Légothèque, Livr'Exil, Numérique, Ressources humaines, Vie de l'Association, Prix sorcières

Des relations internationales structurées dans le cadre de l'Ifla (International federation of library associations and institutions), d'Eblida (European bureau of library, information and documentation), de Liber (Ligue des bibliothèques européennes de recherche) et d'autres organismes internationaux de coopération.

Name: Simcoe County Law Association
Description:
The Simcoe County Law Association is an essential resource for the justice system in Simcoe County providing timely access to legal information, expert research support, and a quiet space for focused work.


The SCLA Courthouse Law Library is a member of an Ontario-wide  network that comprises of 48 courthouse law libraries. We deliver high-value services that support the legal professionals in Simcoe County, as well as those visiting our courthouses from other areas of the province.
Our collection includes legislation, case law, practice tools, and legal databases to support fair, informed decision-making. Our goal is to connect the Simcoe County legal community with the tools they need.

Name: The Apache Junction Public Library
Description:
At the Apache Junction Public Library, we connect our diverse community with knowledge, innovation, and opportunity. Whether you're seeking books, digital resources, tech access, creative programs, or lifelong learning—our doors (and devices) are always open.

With a commitment to service, inclusion, and curiosity, we support all ages through on-site programs, digital platforms, study spaces, makerspace tools, and more. We’re proud to enrich lives in the city of Apache Junction—one story, one skill, and one connection at a time.

Name: South Central Regional Library Council
Description: South Central Regional Library Council leads and advocates for Central New York member libraries by promoting learning, collaboration, and innovation. SCRLC is one of nine Library Councils that make up the Empire State Library Network (ESLN). SCRLC serves 74 members across 10,000 square miles in the counties of Allegany, Broome, Cayuga, Chemung, Chenango, Cortland, Delaware, Otsego, Schuyler, Seneca, Steuben, Tioga, Tompkins, and Yates. SCRLC provides services to 20 academic, 18 hospital, 25 corporate & non-profit libraries, 5 public libraries and public library systems and 6 school library systems and their members. SCRLC reaches over 500 libraries in its service area.

Name: Oravská knižnica Antona Habovštiaka
Description:
Oravská knižnica Antona Habovštiaka v Dolnom Kubíne je regionálnou kultúrnou, informačnou a vzdelávacou inštitúciou, ktorej zriaďovateľom je od roku 2002 Žilinský samosprávny kraj. V zmysle Zákona č. 183/2000 Z. z. o knižniciach a zriaďovacej listiny plní funkciu verejnej mestskej knižnice pre mesto Dolný Kubín a regionálnej knižnice pre územie okresov Dolný Kubín, Námestovo a Tvrdošín.
Oravská knižnica A. Habovštiaka v Dolnom Kubíne obsahom svojej činnosti, spoluprácou s kultúrnymi, výchovno-vzdelávacími, verejnými a spoločenskými inštitúciami, ako aj občianskymi a záujmovými združeniami pôsobí vo svojom prostredí ako kultúrne, výchovné, informačné a komunitné centrum s rozsiahlym používateľským zázemím. Zabezpečuje slobodný prístup k informáciám a poznatkom šírených na všetkých druhoch nosičov, svojou činnosťou vytvára podmienky pre rovnoprávne uspokojovanie kultúrnych, spoločenských a informačných potrieb a záujmov všetkých používateľov, podporuje celoživotné vzdelávanie a svojimi kultúrno-spoločenskými a výchovnými aktivitami sa osobitne zameriava na vekovú kategóriu detí a mládeže, aby na jednej strane formovala ich informačnú gramotnosť pri využívaní rôznych informačných zdrojov a na strane druhej podporovala záujem o čítanie, prehlbovala poznanie kultúrneho dedičstva, motivovala ich k vlastnej tvorivosti

Name: Il vaso di Pandora Società Cooperativa a r.l.
Description:
La Società Cooperativa a r. l. “Il vaso di Pandora” è nata nel febbraio 1998 ed è composta da soci professionisti laureati in discipline letterarie, linguistiche, storiche, artistiche, in particolare nell’ambito dei beni culturali, e specializzati in discipline biblioteconomiche ed archivistiche
I suoi soci sono specializzati nella catalogazione bibliografica e semantica originale e derivata del materiale bibliografico antico e moderno, monografico e seriale secondo gli standard di catalogazione internazionali ISBD (consolidated ed.),  le “Regole italiane di catalogazione “ (REICAT) e le “Anglo-American Cataloguing Rules” (AACR2) per la forma dell’intestazione. Inoltre sono esperti nella catalogazione di bandi, manifesti, fogli volanti, di materiale grafico, di spartiti musicali, non-book material, risorse elettroniche, e materiale cartografico.
La Società Cooperativa “Il vaso di Pandora”, negli ultimi anni, ha partecipato a progetti europei di digitalizzazione relativamente alla catalogazione del materiale e alla creazione dei relativi metadati di supporto alle immagini. 
Svolge lavori di acquisizione delle immagini relative alle copertine dei volumi, riordinamento e controllo dei file e metadatazione. 
In ambito archivistico si occupa di attività di ricondizionamento e catalogazione di archivi di deposito e storici

Name: Everlove & Associates, Inc.
Description:
Formerly known as Special Library Service, Everlove & Associates offers library services, website maintenance, intranet and database design, document retrieval, research and computer training throughout Florida.

Everlove & Associates had humble beginnings in 1976 when Nora Everlove theorized that for every firm large enough to employ a full-time librarian, there were fifty smaller firms with fifty smaller libraries. They, too, needed experience, talent and education - just not all the time. 

Since 1976, E&A has grown in clients, services and geographical area. Currently, we are the librarian at over 150 firms and help staff another dozen larger libraries. 

In this age of “the information explosion,” we are constantly upgrading and improving these services. All things are relative but we are one of the oldest and largest library consulting firms in the country! 

It’s exciting work - always in metamorphosis. Our services must be innovative to keep up with our client’s needs. Also, we have to be cost-effective to maintain our affordability. In short, we have to be as good as our clients!

Description:
FID Media ermöglicht Zugang
Bei fast allen Büchern und Artikeln zeigt der FID Media Ihnen an, ob das Medium bereits im Bestand Ihrer eigenen Hochschulbibliothek ist. Sollte dies einmal nicht der Fall sein, dann können Sie den Artikel oder eine Teilkopie mit einem Klick bestellen. Wenn Sie eine Ressource einmal gar nicht finden sollten, schreiben Sie uns – und wir kümmern uns.

FID Media lizenziert Ressourcen
Über das Portal des FID Media haben alle Nutzer:innen, die hauptberuflich in der Wissenschaft arbeiten, Zugriff auf spezielle FID-Lizenzen. Außerdem haben wir ein ständig wachsendes Angebot an eigens für den FID Media lizenzierten E-Books.

FID Media informiert über Neues
Jede Woche gibt es rund 40 fachrelevante Neuerscheinungen. Sie können neue Bücher mit einem Klick bestellen und wir liefern diese dann direkt an Sie, leihweise für 30 Tage.

FID Media berät zu Open Access, Open Science und Urheberrechtsfragen
Möchten Sie Open Access publizieren? Wir beraten Nutzer:innen und Verlage zu Publikationen und Forschungsdaten sowie Open Science allgemein. Sprechen Sie uns an! Die Servicestelle Urheberrecht des FID Media berät Forschende der Kommunikations- und Medienwissenschaft darüber hinaus in allen urheberrechtlichen Fragen.

FID Media bietet Publikationsinfrastrukturen
Über unser Repositorium media/rep/ stehen Ihnen die gewohnten Publikationsdienste zur Verfügung für Aufsätze und Monografien, aber auch für Blogs und Forschungsdaten. Künftig möchten wir besonders Erstveröffentlichungen unterstützen.

FID Media digitalisiert
Wir erweitern den Zugang zu fachspezifischen Ressourcen, indem wir ausgewählte kommunikations- und medienhistorische Bestände digitalisieren.

Name: Maine Tool Library
Description:
Access to tools enables good projects, and membership to libraries supports community sharing; Maine neighborhoods deserve both!

We are a group of committed Mainers who have been using our spare time to start a tool lending library in Portland, Maine. We include tool experts, library enthusiasts, community organizers and designers. We are people who know through personal experience how a tool library could support garden projects, community work parties, and kitchen adventures. We know this type of creativity and drive exists among Portland, ME area residents, so show us we’re right, and help us launch the first tool lending library in New England.

The Maine Tool Library - Portland (MTL) will be a not-for-profit lending library, circulating a collection of tools. Located at 224 Anderson Street, the library will initially be open on Saturdays from 9-1. The collection will include gardening, landscaping, home improvement, and kitchen tools. This entity will be a membership-based community resource; it is a library for tools instead of books. Tools are loaned on a weekly schedule, allowing patrons to check them out during the library’s open hours with the expectation that they will then return them a week later. Acquisitions are strategically selected by elected members with continual opportunities for member input. MTL is a volunteer-run, self-governed entity which aims to be an inclusive network among laborers, designers, makers, crafters, artists and D.Y.I.ers in the vibrant Portland community.

We are a project of the Resilience Hub and our location for our first year has been generously subsidized by Aikido of Maine. 

You can see the details of our governance plan, our budget, our logistics and development calendar, and our interim tool library board here: http://portlandmainetoollibrary.org
