Name: readova
Description:
The Last Page Movement – A Revolution for Readers

In an era of distractions, where attention spans are shrinking and deep thinking is fading, The Last Page Movement was born out of necessity. We are witnessing a global reading recession—a silent crisis where books are being replaced by fleeting digital content, and the ability to focus, reflect, and comprehend is diminishing. This is not just about books; it’s about the loss of deep thinking, creativity, and wisdom.

We believe that reading is more than a pastime—it is a fundamental pillar of progress. It sharpens the mind, fosters imagination, and nurtures critical thinking. Every great idea, every breakthrough, every revolution has its roots in the written word. Yet, as the world moves faster, fewer people are taking the time to sit with a book, to engage with its ideas, to turn the last page with a sense of fulfillment.

That’s why we call it The Last Page Movement—because reading is not about speed, but about completion. About savoring knowledge, one page at a time. About rekindling a habit that has shaped civilizations. This is a movement to bring books back into people’s lives, to inspire the world to read again.

And as we build this revolution, something exciting is on the horizon. Readova—a surprise coming soon. A platform designed for readers, by readers. A space where authors, publishers, and book lovers unite.

The journey begins now. One page at a time.

Name: ماذا لو
Description:
هذه طريقة تفكير جديدة وليست مجرد صفحة! و هو جهد مشترك ما بين أم و ابنتها ذات الثماني سنوات.
إن التحاور مع الأطفال لهو واحد من أمتع الفعاليات التي يمكن القيام بها، ويعتبر فتح الحوار مع الطفل وفتح حوار بين الطفل ونفسه، من أهم أهداف هذا الكتاب /هذه المدونة. وإذا استطعنا تشبيه إجراء محادثة مع الأطفال بإعداد طبق مميز من الطعام، فان هذا الكتاب يقدم لكم اقتراحات، حول المكونات التي يمكن استخدامها لإعداد هذا الطبق! لكنه في النهاية سيكون بطريقتكم الخاصة ونكهاتكم الخاصة.
وفيما يتعلم الأطفال عن الحياة كل يوم ويخضون تجاربا جديدة، فان أحد أهم التجارب هو كيفية بدء علاقات إنسانية صحية والحفاظ عليها. فسواء كنت والدًا أو فردًا من العائلة، أو مدرسًا أو صديقًا للطفل، فإن علاقتك معه، تمثل نموذجًا للعلاقات المستقبلية التي سينيها الطفل لاحقا، ولذا فان الانخراط في مناقشات ممتعة وصحية، يعلمه كيف يكون مشاركًا نشطًا وفعالا في الحياة كما يعلمه التفاعل مع الأشخاص المختلفين وأن هنالك أنواعًا مختلفة من الناس، لديهم أنماط حياة، وآراء مختلفة. الأمر الذي يساعد الطفل على فهم الاختلافات وقبولها، وتعلم قيمة التنوع والنقاش، حيث أن كل منا هو مكون فريد جدا من مكونات الحياة، كما أن لكل من صبغة خاصة، ومساهمة مميزة في هذه الحياة. وقد انطلقنا في تسمية هذا الكتاب “ماذا لو كنت حبة دونتس "؟ الذي أعددته بمشاركة ابنتي ذات الثماني سنوات، من هذا المبدأ، حيث أن كل حبة "دونتس" لها طعم منفرد وخاص ومميز، لكن جميع قطع "الدونتس" مصنوعة من نفس العجين، ونحن أيضا كأشخاص مختلفون جدا في تفاصيلنا لكن قالبنا واحد!
دمتم بحب!
سوزان جرار ونوار دينيز

Name: KPI Library / Бібліотека КПІ
Description:
Бібліотека КПІ є однією з кращих вузівських бібліотек України і Європи.
Заснована в 1898 році в Київському політехнічному інституті им. Iмператора Олександра ІІ,
сьогодні бібліотека КПІ обслуговує більше 45 тисяч читачів за рік, із них – 37 тисяч
студентів. Щоденно бібліотеку відвідує від 1300 до 1800 користувачів. Бібліотека має 15
спеціалізованих читальних залів на 1500 місць і 5 абоненементів: науково-технічної 
літератури, навчальної літератури, соціально-економічної і художньої літератури,
періодики. Фонд бібліотеки налічує майже 3 млн. приміриків видань, із них – половина
підручники і навчальні посібники видання та інформаційні ресурси з профілю
університету.

У бібліотеці представлені нормативно-технічна документація, література з питань
мистецтва, спорту, лінгвістики, культурології, народознавства. Бібліотека передплачує
більше 1000 назв періодичних видань за профілем університету.

Бібліотека є осередком культурного життя не тільки студентів, науковців та викладачів КПІ, а й простих киян. 
Музичні вечори, мотивуючі лекції, літературні зустрічі та інші цікаві заходи, що проводятся в бібліотеці  відкриті для всіх бажаючих.  

Бібліотека КПІ на сьогодні є доступною широкому колу читачів. З її фондами може
працювати будь-який житель нашої планети.

Name: Children's Design International Collection - Collection internationale Desseins d'enfants'
Shen Zhen jiayunxin technology co.,ltd
Description:
As the forerunner in testing and verification services for Chinese companies, Centre Testing International Group Co., Ltd. (CTI) is the leading comprehensive third-party agency specializing in testing,calibration, inspection, certification, and technical services through provision of a one-stop solution for clients of all types and sizes.

Founded in 2003 and headquartered in Shenzhen, CTI operates over 30 branches in mainland China, with more than 90 laboratories in the field of chemistry, biology, physics,mechanics, electromagnetics and more. Moreover, CTI operates branches in Taiwan,Hong Kong, Singapore and the United States to respond to the needs of a global economy. On October 30th, 2009, CTI was successfully listed on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange with stock code number 300012. CTI was the first company listed on ChiNext and the first testing and certification agency in China's domestic testing industry to be listed.

Based on our worldwide network and strong capabilities, CTI annually tests about 400,000 samples for nearly 90,000 customers. Among them are nearly 100 of the world's top five hundred companies. CTI provides services in the areas of industrial products, consumer goods, trade security and life science. Our sevice items include but are not limited to: hazardous substances testing, safety detection,EMC testing, environment safety testing, electronics reliability and failure analysis,material reliability and failure analysis, metal and nonmetallic materials analysis,textile, footwear and leather testing, toy testing, automobile materials and component testing, food, pharmaceuticals, cosmetics, feed and food contact materials testing,inspection and compliance, audit, measurement calibration and dimension inspection,semiconductor testing, maritime hazardous materials management, freight assessment,certification and training, application software development and testing.

Name: Casa Granada Properties
Description:
WWW.NICARAGUA-GUIDE.COM was created for people living in Nicaragua, those thinking about moving to Nicaragua, visitors or anyone wanting to know more about the real Nicaragua. There are many excellent guide books for the backpackers and casual tourists but here we hope to show you a personal Nicaragua with insights from people who chose to live in this wonderful country of cool mountains, sunny beaches, volcanoes, colonial cities and friendly people. Here you will find tips on how to become a resident, costs of living, health care considerations, community activities and much more. 

Everything you would like to know about living here: cost of living, medical facilities, buying building or renting, taxes, paperwork, doing business here, answers to what life is like here and more. Let us help you understand this beautiful country: its history, the regions, the people, things you should see, stories, topics, the government and photos. 

Learn about organizations helping Nicaragua such as where you can volunteer your time and services, contact information, typical projects. Find out more about what's happening in the community: local news, local organizations, personal stories and those little things you can never find. We even have our diaries from the day we decided to move here that shows the good, the bad and the ugly. And we provide contact info, anecdotes, useful websites, biographies, books and articles, maps, and other stuff.

Name: Boston Medical Library
Description:
The first Boston Medical Library (BML) was founded by Doctors John C. Warren and James Jackson in 1805. The Boston Medical Library was reconstituted in 1875 by Dr. James Chadwick with Dr. Oliver Wendell Holmes as its President. In 1960, the Boston Medical Library and the President and Fellows of Harvard College (Harvard University) entered into an agreement to combine the collections, services and administration of the Boston Medical Library and the Harvard Medical Library in a new building known as The Francis A. Countway Library of Medicine. Each of the two original institutions continues collecting and ownership of its holdings. The combined library ranks as one of the largest medical libraries in the world with one of the richest collections.

The Boston Medical Library is a physician and dentist non-profit organization incorporated in 1877. The BML mission, revised in 2004, is "to be a Library for the dissemination of medical knowledge, the promotion of medical education and scholarship, and the preservation and celebration of medical history, and thereby to advance the quality of health and healthcare of the people." The Boston Medical Library serves as a resource for the lifelong learning of practicing physicians of Massachusetts which has been its founding and continuing mission, besides serving the medical school faculties and students of Harvard Medical School, Boston University Medical School, Tufts University School of Medicine and the University of Massachusetts Medical School. In 1947, the Boston Medical Library formally became the library for the Massachusetts Medical Society (MMS).

A Board of Trustees half of which is appointed by the MMS manages the BML. This group meets regularly and has fiduciary responsibility for a significant endowment which provides partial support for the Countway Library as a whole. The Boston Medical Library is a 501(c)(3) tax-exempt, not-for-profit organization.

Name: Merida English Library
Description:
The Merida English Library (MEL) is a non-profit library and vibrant community and cultural center located in the heart of Merida, Yucatan, dedicated to promoting literacy, lifelong learning, and cross-cultural connection through English-language resources and programming.

Founded in 1994 when Elizabeth Dunkel stood up at an International Women’s Club brunch and said, “Let’s start an English library,” MEL was born out of a love for books and a desire to build community. 

With the help of Chloe Conaway de Pacheco and her lawyer to establish a non-profit civil association (A.C.), along with the generosity of Ellie Guidotti—who loaned her home as its first location—MEL began its humble beginnings with only a few donated shelves, some secondhand books, and boundless dedication.

In its early years, the library was built by volunteers shelving books, cataloging donations, and sharing their love of reading. Marilyn Estes Smith, who organized the reference section, later bequeathed her home to become MEL’s permanent residence located on 53rd street in downtown Merida.

From there, MEL quickly grew beyond books: from wine tastings and lively BBQ fundraisers to the famed Chili Cook-Off, to programs like Conversaciones con Amigos, MEL Nights, Story Time, and the Santa Ana Art Gallery Tours, the library has become a hub of learning and friendship.

Today, MEL remains entirely community-supported—receiving no government funding—and is managed by a team of dedicated staff and volunteers, and its Library Collection Committee (LCC) curates and maintains an ever-evolving collection of books, puzzles, and more.

MEL is more than a library. It is a labor of love, a space for connection, and a symbol of what’s possible when people come together to share knowledge, culture, and kindness!

 Follow our other social media accounts on Instagram – meridaenglishlibrary , Facebook – meridaenglishlibrary  , and YouTube - meridaenglishlibrary2258  for more information!

Name: A.N.G.E.L. Program
Description:
The ANGEL program exists to provide professional library services for elementary school and academy libraries in the Southern Union Conference of the Seventh-day Adventist church.

At ANGEL, books are purchased, cataloged, processed and shipped to schools, ready for the library shelves. Included with these services is access to ANGEL's online catalog, where each school can browse their library collection via the Internet. This helps to lessen the duty of library maintenance for many faculty and staff. A well stocked library is essential to every school, and so is teaching students to navigate their library’s resources. Now even the smallest SDA schools have the benefit of professional library services without the need to hire a trained library media specialist. These services also help many schools with accreditation programs, such as SACS.

Name: Katia van Bommel - aandacht voor taal
Description:
Onder mijn eigen naam ben ik werkzaam als projectleider, adviseur en conceptontwikkelaar op het gebied van taalontwikkeling, leesbevordering en laaggeletterdheid – een onderneming die ik het liefste omschrijf als energiek, betrokken en met de voeten in de klei. Dat ik mijn liefde en passie voor alles wat met letters te maken heeft mag overdragen aan anderen, maakt mij het gelukkigst. Dankzij mijn vele ervaringen, oneindige interesse en professionele deskundigheid kan ik door het ontwikkelen van leesplezier en het stimuleren van taalontwikkeling een enorm verschil maken in het leven van kinderen én ouders. Mijn persoonlijke werkwijze is analytisch, eigenwijs en sociaal; en dit alles met een flinke dosis humor – iets dat steevast gewaardeerd wordt door alle betrokkenen.

Meer info of een bakje thee drinken? Neem een kijkje op www.katiavanbommel.nl of neem contact op via info@katiavanbommel.nl

Name: Scintille Bookclub
Description:
Scintille Bookclub è un gruppo di lettura, nato nell’autunno del 2018, per chi ha tra i 25 e i 40 anni. Ci incontriamo all'Antica Farmacia San Filippo Neri, a Parma, una volta al mese e discutiamo del libro in lettura, ma anche del mondo dei trentenni, di ciò che ci accomuna, di quello che ci fa stare bene e di cose che invece detestiamo.

Abbiamo scelto di trovarci in un luogo non canonico, slegato dall'ambiente letterario per abbattere il timore reverenziale nei confronti della lettura e aprire le porte sia ai lettori più forti sia a tutti quelli che amano leggere, ma lo fanno con fatica. Il nostro approccio è pop e informale.

Oltre all’incontro mensile, Scintille Bookclub organizza anche un mini festival letterario – Scintille di editoria – che lo scorso anno si è tenuto nel mese di maggio, incontri con autori, workshop e corsi, appuntamenti per bambini e famiglie ed eventi dedicati ai libri. Tutti gli eventi proposti al di fuori dell’incontro mensile sono gratuiti e aperti al pubblico
di tutte le età.

Le fondatrici di Scintille sono Caterina Bonetti, Francesca Laureri e Camilla Mineo.

Caterina è autrice di Biblioprecaria blog. Un dottorato in italianistica, si occupa di progettazione, organizzazione eventi, copywriting e politica. Scrive su Gli Stati Generali, è autrice di una monografia.

Francesca è Blogger, creatrice di contenuti e organizzatrice di eventi, è laureata in lingue e appassionata di letteratura inglese. Su Instagram e sul suo blog parla di libri, cibo e viaggi.

Camilla è critico d’arte, giornalista pubblicista e curatore indipendente. È ideatrice e direttore artistico di Parma 360 Festival e Antica Farmacia+ e realizza progetti artistici in collaborazione con le aziende.

Acid in paper, ink corrosion in historical handwritten documents and notes, mould on books and files, damage caused by incorrect use or fire and water disasters - paper faces a variety of threats. As a company with a quality management system that has been certified to DIN EN ISO 9001, we guarantee excellent quality for all aspects of paper preservation.

Name: ClearVisionProject
Description:
ClearVision is a charity. We run a postal lending library of children’s books adapted to include braille as well as the print and pictures, so they can be shared by people with and without sight. We also have a unique collection of around 800 tactile image books, in which all the pictures are designed to be explored by touch. We loan books all across the UK and Ireland.
Our library's vitally important because it lets children with little or no sight learn to read and do homework with the support of their sighted family, friends and teachers, and adult braille readers to enjoy stories with sighted children. There are over 14,000 books in the collection, fiction and non-fiction, catering for children from birth until they’re independent readers. 
We add lots of new ClearVision books each month to make sure the collection is up-to-date and children with sight impairment can enjoy the same titles as their sighted friends. Our collection fully textured tactile books are loved by children who'll go on to read braille, but also by those who read large print but don't have enough sight to enjoy and image, and by children with additional disabilities, including complex needs.
Children learn to love books and become confident readers through shared reading experiences. Confident reading helps maximise educational attainment and lets children make the most of their opportunities in life. Our books let children and adults with sight impairment enjoy all the pleasure, comfort and excitement reading together brings.

SCKLS includes the twelve counties of Barber, Butler, Cowley, Harper, Harvey, Kingman, McPherson, Reno, Rice, Sedgwick, Stafford and Sumner and has a service population of approximately 762,058. There are 149 members participating and eligible for system services and programs. Membership and participation are voluntary, and each member library retains its local self-government and independence.

Name: Bayside Toy Library
Description:
Who are we?
Bayside Toy Library is a not-for-profit toy lending service (for children aged between 6 months and 10 years), which provides families with access to a variety of educational toys to assist with childhood development.

Established in 1985, it recognised two fundamental truths: toys cost a lot of money, and kids lose interest in them fast! With over 3,000 items on offer, BTL can fascinate your child for years to come at a very low cost. The philosophy behind the choice of toys is that they should be multi-functional and require kids to use and develop their own skills. Of course, they are also lots of fun!

The Bayside Toy Library (BTL) is managed by a voluntary committee of members. Committee members are elected annually at our AGM which is open to all members. 

Vision
Healthy, happy children

Mission
Providing access to high quality, affordable and fun toys that educate through play

What makes BTL unique?
BTL isn’t JUST a lending service. BTL is becoming a community space where families and carers can find answers to how to rise their children healthy and happy, through having fun. BTL is a space where people can share experiences and connect. 

BTL has sustainability to its core, practising and encouraging anything that will reduce the impact on our planet, so the generation we are educating can enjoy it. BTL has been around for more than 30 years – that’s a lot of playing time with children!

Name: James White Library
Description:
The James White Library (JWL) was designed to house two libraries. One was the Emmanuel Missionary College Library, which had its beginning in 1874 when Battle Creek College library collection consisted of 200 volumes which had either been donated or purchased with contributed funds. By 1901 when the college relocated to Berrien Springs, the collection had grown to over 3,000 volumes. The library soon outgrew its home on the second floor of the Administration Building, as it would also outgrow Griggs Hall. The other library collection was that of the Seventh-day Adventist Theological Seminary, which was established in Washington, D.C. in 1937. Numbering slightly over 2,100 books in 1940, the collection grew dramatically in the following two decades thanks to sound acquisitions and numerous donations. The 1960-61 academic year saw the relocation of the Seminary to Berrien Springs, Michigan, and a merger of the two collections under one roof the following year. When it opened in 1962, JWL housed over 120,000 volumes and 1,000 periodical titles.

JWL now houses over 750,000 volumes, in addition to 2,800 periodical subscriptions. The library also has a strong collection of non-print materials, and has made its catalog available on-line through the World Wide Web.

Name: Fondazione Marco Besso
Description:
La Fondazione Marco Besso fu istituita il 1° giugno 1918 da Marco Besso, triestino che aveva fatto di Roma la sua seconda patria.
Attuario di altissime intuizioni, da giovane funzionario delle Assicurazioni Generali giunse poi nel 1909 ad esserne il Presidente, è a tutt’oggi considerato una delle figure eminenti del settore della previdenza sociale. 
Visse gli anni del grande fermento risorgimentale, morale e culturale del Paese. Raccolse fondi per i mazziniani e sostenne il Comitato Nazionale Romano tanto da meritarsi, nel 1875, la medaglia di benemerito per la liberazione di Roma.
Al di là della sua professione e del suo impegno politico, coltivò e favorì, con intendimenti moderni ed eccezionale capacità organizzativa, studi economici, finanziari, umanistici e letterari: tra i suoi grandi interessi Roma e Dante. 
La Fondazione nasce dalla volontà espressa di diffondere la cultura con l’organizzazione di convegni, conferenze, visite guidate, cicli di lezioni ed attraverso l’accesso gratuito alla Biblioteca dislocata all’interno della sede in Largo di Torre Argentina n. 11 a Roma.

La Biblioteca, seguendo i filoni culturali del Fondatore, aggiorna le sue raccolte su Roma, Dante e proverbi, nonché sulla Tuscia, il cui fondo iniziale pervenne in donazione. Ospita inoltre, un cospicuo fondo di stampe antiche e fotografie riguardanti monumenti, vedute e luoghi significativi della città di Roma, molti dei quali oggi scomparsi, oltre ad immagini della famiglia Besso e dell'omonimo palazzo. Custodisce numerose pubblicazioni periodiche, circa 700, molte storiche e di notevole importanza per la conoscenza del periodo risorgimentale e post Unità d'Italia, oltre quelle afferenti allo stesso ambito di ricerca delle monografie. A tutt’oggi il patrimonio librario detenuto dalla Biblioteca ammonta a circa 70.000 volumi.

Rewrite your city's history, block by block.

Our public reading room and programs do more than preserve the important history of community media in Chicago. We believe that if people felt more of a connection to their neighborhoods, histories and cultural lineages and could actually see the common roots of creativity at work around them, this would result in a more active, supportive, collaborative, diverse and informed cultural landscape.

Published something? We'll catalog it. Want to get involved? Volunteer! Looking to learn more about a community, now or from another era? Visit us and use our online catalog to share stories about what you find.

Les différentes activités du CREC incluent la bibliothèque, les cours d'appui scolaire, les cours d'anglais, les cours d'informatique et le projet Parcours du Citoyen.
Le Centre de Recherche, d'Etude et de Créativité est situé à Godomey, en périphérie de Cotonou (capitale économique du Bénin). Il est dans la même enceinte que la communauté jésuite du Bénin. Historiquement le centre était situé dans les locaux de la communauté. En 2013 de nouveaux locaux ont été construits et inaugurés au début de l'année 2014. Le CREC dispose maintenant d'une grande bibliothèque, de nombreuses salles de formation, des bureaux, une salle informatique, une grande salle de conférence, sans parler de la grande terrasse sur le toit utilisé pour les événements ponctuels.

Name: Disciples of Christ Historical Society
Description:
The mission of the Disciples of Christ Historical Society is to preserve and proclaim the story of the Christian Church (Disciples of Christ) within the context of the broader Stone-Campbell tradition out of which it grows. Celebrating our past and telling its hard truths, we seek a church empowered to make history now and thus to shape a future ever more faithful to God’s dream of unity and justice for all. We are a general ministry of the Christian Church (Disciples of Christ) with the special mandate to serve all three major streams of the Stone-Campbell movement: the Churches of Christ, the Christian Churches/Churches of Christ, and the Christian Church (Disciples of Christ). We preserve records and artifacts common to our traditions and foster collaboration and conversation through historical research, publications, and lectures. 

Since 1941, Disciples of Christ Historical Society has preserved the many documents, books, periodicals, audio and visual materials, and precious artifacts that tell the story of the Stone-Campbell heritage. We have made these available for personal and academic historical research. We hold the largest collection of Stone-Campbell material in the world, including a large number of historic photographs and paintings, a great number of rare books and artifacts, biographical files and congregational, regional, and general church records and documents.

Name: Public Library - Varna (Pencho Slaveykov Regional Library)
Description:
“Pencho Slaveykov” Public Library in Varna was founded in 1883. It is a public institution supported by the Municipality and the Government. The library has been depository of the Bulgarian national literature since 1945. It is a regional library and centre for consulting other libraries in the North-East part of the country. 

Latest statistic have shown that library collections number over 870 000 volumes of books, periodicals and other materials. 30% of our users are under 18 years old. Library collects and offers books and periodicals, manuscripts and archival documents, albums, cartographic publications, official documents, standards, written music, records, audio and video cassettes, CDs and DVDs. In Regional History Dept. there is a unique and full collection of periodicals printed in Varna after 1880.

The Library started a process of digitization of its rare and unique collections of periodicals, post-cards, photos, maps etc several years ago. “Pencho Slaveykov” Public Library developed the Varna Digital Library repository which provides access to library’s and its partner-museums’ digitised materials.

The Library is a member of the Bulgarian Library Association and a partner in a number of European non-governmental cultural programs and international projects.

The Library is situated in a city of 400,000 inhabitants, with regional responsibilities in an area of significant change. An increase in the number of adults with only a lower-level education highlights the risks of social exclusion and lost local traditions and is of deep concern for the library.

Training activities for adults include cooperation with community groups serving seniors, maintaining collections and services of special interest to adults and workshops/one-to-one training on how to use new technologies. The last few years Public Library - Varna uses a METHOD OF ACTIVE-LEARNING for adults' trainings.

Name: Biblioteca do Real Consulado
Description:
La Biblioteca del Real Consulado fue la primera Biblioteca Pública en establecerse en la ciudad de La Coruña bajo el amparo del “Real Consulado de Mar y Tierra” con el beneplácito del Rey Carlos IV, en 1803 y abierta al público en 1806.

Nuestra fundación es la fundación cultural privada más antigua de España, inscrita el 31 de enero de 1807 en el Protectorado de Fundaciones Culturales. 

Declarada por la Xunta de especial interés para Galicia.

Es una biblioteca principalmente de fondo antiguo, con fondos bibliográficos, documentales y cartográficos, desde el siglo XV.  La mayor riqueza de libros corresponde a los siglos XVIII y primer cuarto del XIX. Considerada como un centro de consulta imprescindible para los investigadores de la realidad histórica coruñesa, gallega y universal en sus diversas manifestaciones. Colabora con todo tipo de instituciones  culturales de cualquier parte del mundo que lo solicite.

The Library of the Royal Consulate was the first public library established in the city of A Coruña, under the auspices of the Royal Consulate of the Sea and Land, with the approval of King Charles IV in 1803, and opened to the public in 1806.

Our foundation is the oldest private cultural foundation in Spain, registered on January 31, 1807, in the Registry of Cultural Foundations.

It has been declared by the Xunta (Regional Government of Galicia) as being of special interest to Galicia.

This is primarily a historical library, with bibliographic, documentary, and cartographic collections dating back to the 15th century. Its greatest wealth lies in books from the 18th century and the first quarter of the 19th century. It is considered an essential research center for scholars investigating the historical reality of A Coruña, Galicia, and the world, in its various manifestations. The library collaborates with all types of cultural institutions around the world upon request.

Name: NTLP - Not Typical Library Partners
Description:
NTLP was a private, member-driven non-profit corporation serving all types of libraries. Its mission was to advance libraries and the communities they serve through innovative information technology, education, and collaborative library support services. It did so primarily by:
* Facilitating collaborative library services,
* Advocating on behalf of libraries,
* Focusing on new opportunities for libraries,
* Advancing professionalism and high standards in library service, and
* Supporting sustainable and transformational projects in libraries.

NTLP began as North Texas Regional Library System, Inc. (NTRLS), a non-profit responsible for administering the system's operations grant from the Texas State Library and Archives Commision (TSLAC) which used LSTA funds from the Institute of Museum and Library Services. As the organization began to provide programs and services beyond the scope of government funding restrictions, it became necessary to seek funding from sources other than TSLAC. The organization took on the name North Texas Library Partners to distinguish the non-profit from the state-funded system and began offering additional services through funding from non-governmental sources. 

Although NTLP received funding from membership dues, grants, sponsorships, government contracts and donations, NTLP lost a significant source of its funding when the State legislature greatly reduced funding to the Texas State Library and Archives Commission and that organization eliminated the budget for all 10 regional offices of the State library system. By September 1, 2012, most of the regional offices were no longer in business. NTLP closed its doors permanently May 31, 2013.

Name: The Archibald Corbett Community Library, Arts & Heritage Centre
Description:
The Organisation and Leadership:

The Archibald Corbett Community Library, Arts and Heritage Centre (ACCLAHC), trading as The Corbett Community Centre, is a charity formed in 2016 by local residents to take over the management of the previously named Grade II listed Torridon Road Library from Lewisham Council, to save it from being closed down and develop it into a Community Centre for the Corbett Estate and surrounding areas, and providing a wide-range of free activities, events and local services to include the continuing presence of the Library, plus a new Performing Arts, Exhibition and Heritage Centre.

The Corbett Community Centre project is located at 103-105 Torridon Road, Catford, London, SE6 1RQ and currently the ACCLAHC board consists of nine trustee members, each well experienced with aspects of developing groups to work in partnership, to provide services for the overall benefit of the local community.

Building stronger community relationships:

Since its re-opening in October 2016, the Corbett Community Centre has continued to attract on average over 6,000 visitors per month (an increase of 30% on previous years Library usage), with over 1,000 members of the public regularly attending over 20 activities per week, which have since been developed since the opening. Essentially, bringing residents together and generating greater community cohesion with help and support from more than 70+ volunteers each week, engaged in running the library’s vital range of services and its increasingly popular range of activities.

The current and future development of the Corbett Community Centre is improving a vital community asset situated at the heart of the Corbett Estate and has become an essential, vibrant hub for the provision of services to the community.

Name: Archive Media Partners
Description:
Archive Media Partners’ mission is to assist archivists and librarians in coping with the pressures associated with the rapidly changing technological environment. We will assist your staff with dedicated training and customized manuals and provide consultation on copyright issues, licensing and revenue generation.

Our team employs archivists, information management experts, imaging professionals, librarians, and business researchers from a variety of backgrounds. We also specialize in processing archival collections of photographic and motion materials, both traditional and digital, as well as creative treatments. 

 Archive Media Partners also works with organizations that have been frustrated by the imaging process. We will take time to educate you about your choices and are available to repair imaging projects gone awry. We have no partnership with any software company, so recommendations are made only by the client’s need, not by our ties. You expect to add value with digital collection management. You expect to increase revenue, decrease retrieval time, increase efficiency, and know exactly what your intellectual assets are. Archive Media Partners can achieve this with you.

Name: Biblioteca Deportiva
Description:
Somos una iniciativa que busca el apoyo de empresas, fundaciones y personas que se identifiquen con nuestra propuesta y juntos hagamos de la lectura un espacio de recreación que combine la lectura y el deporte  con el objetivo de contribuir y solidificar la cultura de la lectura como una especie de bien o valor agregado a nuestras actividades a través de la capacidad de transformación cultural que posee el deporte como fenómeno extradeportivo, y rendir homenaje a nuestros héroes deportivos nombrando en su honor a la Biblioteca de la comunidad donde se desarrollaron; proveyendo a los niños/niñas, jóvenes y adultos de figuras que los ayuden a identificarse y buscar el desarrollo integral de sus actitudes y aptitudes.

Name: Lee Memorial Library
Description:
Lee Memorial Library is a nationally-ranked, service -oriented lending library located in Allendale, NJ, a modern suburban community of 6,500 residents nestled in the Saddle River Valley, 25 miles west of New York City. The library maintains a robust collection, circulating over 150,000 print and electronic materials annually while serving as a productive work space, a gathering place for programs and presentations, and an archive of historical materials.

The library staff and board of trustees are dedicated to promoting lifelong learning, offering access to technology resources, and providing professional services and tools that enrich, educate, and entertain our patrons.The borough of Allendale is home to the Celery Farm, a 112-acre nature preserve, Crestwood Park, an active leisure destination, and the John Fell House, a Revolutionary War era historic home, among other attractions.

Allendale’s renowned school system, vibrant local businesses, outstanding recreation programs and engaged community organizations complement the library in offering a supportive environment in which to learn, grow and prosper.

Name: Botany Bay Family History Society
Description:
We have a research centre and library premises in Caringbah, with extensive resources of books, microfiche, and computers with access to FindMyPast, Ancestry World Edition, The Genealogist, Internet History Resources and the British Newspaper Archives. We are also an affiliate Library for FamilySearch, permitting access to those digitised records otherwise only available at an FH Centre.

Ours is an active, friendly society of almost 400 members.

We publish a quarterly journal, The Endeavour, twice winner of the Nick Vine Hall Award.

We hold two meetings per month for members and guests. The first includes a guest speaker and the secnd meeting consists of a theme to assist members with their research.

We have nine Special Interest Groups available to members and visitors who meet regularly for mutual support, updates and help: DNA, Australian, English, Scottish, Irish, Writers, Reunion for Mac, Family Tree Maker and Legacy and a Groups.io forum for online discussion.

Botany Bay Connections is our Genealogy & Historical database website at http://www.bbfhsconnections.com.au/ . This is a project where you will find the names of many people from Australia and around the world with a connection to the Sutherland Shire with constant additions being made to the database.

Beginner classes are held each year and special interest talks from time to time.

We hold an annual Heritage Fair.

Our Members Interest Directory is available on our website as is our Library Catalogue.

Name: Librerías Libun
Description:
LIBUN (Fundación del Libro Universitario) es una institución peruana dedicada a promover la producción y difusión del libro académico y universitario en el país. Fundada con el objetivo de apoyar a autores y editores locales, LIBUN se ha consolidado como un referente en la industria editorial peruana, destacándose por su compromiso con la calidad y la diversidad del conocimiento académico.

En un mercado donde la competencia es intensa, LIBUN se distingue por su enfoque en la publicación de contenidos especializados y relevantes para la educación superior. La fundación no solo facilita la publicación de textos académicos, sino que también fomenta la investigación y el desarrollo de nuevas ideas en diversos campos del saber. Su capacidad para adaptarse a las nuevas tendencias y demandas del mercado editorial la posiciona como un líder innovador en su sector.

Open 7 days a week, the Millburn Library holds over 80,000 items in its collection including books, DVDs, audiobooks and more.

Name: Triskel Ediciones S.C.
Description:
Triskel Ediciones S.C. es una editorial independiente que comenzó su andadura a mediados de 2013. Somos un nuevo sello editorial que se está haciendo un hueco en el mercado con una filosofía de trabajo muy definida, respetar al escritor y al lector. La idea nació hacia 2011, y por supuesto todo surgió debido a la pasión por la lectura que poseen los socios que componen la editorial, pero un trabajo duro y serio hizo que el proyecto fuese estudiado durante más de un año. Finalmente Triskel Ediciones arrancó en Julio de 2013.

Los fundadores de la editorial tuvieron claro desde el principio porque una editorial basada en la publicación en digital y en papel con el sistema de pedido bajo demanda, podía ser factible. Y no es otro asunto que el gran “hueco” existente entre lo que es la autopublicación y las grandes editoriales. Los lectores se ven obligados a elegir entre precios de libros electrónicos muy baratos pero sin un trabajo mínimo de editorial que poseen las autopublicaciones, y los precios inflados pero con un buen trabajo editorial de las grandes firmas. A medio camino nos encuadramos nosotros, Triskel Ediciones, pues poseemos precios muy baratos, actualmente las obras se pueden comprar por tres euros en digital y de doce a 17 euros en papel, pero dando la seguridad al lector que hay un trabajo editorial detrás.

Otros signos que nos diferencia, es que apostamos de una manera clara y decidida por autores noveles pues son los que más problemas tienen para publicar, ya que las grandes editoriales no quieren arriesgarse, por lo que muchos talentos se ven abocados a la autopublicación. Nosotros le brindamos el apoyo editorial que todo escritor necesita. Además Triskel Ediciones promociona distintos cursos para autores noveles a través de Triskel Difusión, ayudándoles así en su formación. El respeto a los autores se completa con los porcentajes que se ofrece.

Name: Čitaj mi!/Read to me!
Description:
"Read to me!" is the first Croatian national campaign promoting reading aloud to children from the earliest age. It is organized by the Croatian Reading Association, Croatian Library Association - Children and Youth Services Commission, Croatian Paediatric Society, Croatian Association of Researchers in Children's Literature with support of UNICEF. 

The campaign was launched with the aim to continuously promote early literacy and reading in families over the next few years and to make reading aloud a part of daily routine in Croatian families with small children. Also, campaign’s aims are to make library services for babies and small children a part of regular services in public libraries and to intensify cooperation between librarians, pediatricians, speech therapists and other field experts in early reading aloud promotion.

The value of the campaign "Read to me!" is based on synergy and partnership of several professional associations that collaborate, share information and ideas to ensure the achievement of the objectives of the campaign. Teamwork and innovative approach, the concept of unity and cooperation among organizations is based on accountability, decision-making and volunteering.

[In Croatian]
"Čitaj mi!" prva je nacionalna kampanja za promicanje čitanja naglas djeci od rođenja.  Organizatori su Hrvatsko knjižničarsko društvo - Komisija za knjižnične usluge za djecu i mladež, UNICEF, Hrvatsko pedijatrijsko društvo, Hrvatsko čitateljsko društvo, Hrvatska udruga istraživača dječje književnosti, Hrvatsko logopedsko društvo. Cilj kampanje je poticati roditelje i druge odrasle da s čitanjem naglas djetetu započnu već od njegova rođenja i da s najmlađom djecom što prije počnu dolaziti u lokalnu narodnu knjižnicu.

Name: The Institute Library (New Haven Young Men's Institute)
Description:
The Institute Library is New Haven, Connecticut's oldest independent circulating library and one of the last remaining membership libraries in North America. Founded in 1826 with the purpose of "mutual assistance in the attainment of useful knowledge," the library provides its members with quiet reading areas, a large circulating collection of up-to-date literature, and rich opportunities to engage with each other and the surrounding neighborhoods of downtown New Haven.

In addition to serving its members, the Institute Library proudly opens its doors to all visitors with regular community programs and events from the popular Amateur Hour conversation series hosted by Joshua Foer and Jack Hitt to rotating art exhibitions on two floors.

Name: The Wagnalls Memorial Library
Description: The Wagnalls Memorial Library is a public library given to the residents of Lithopolis and Bloom Township, by Mabel Wagnalls Jones in memory of her parents Adam and Anna Willis Wagnalls. Adam was the co-founder of the Funk and Wagnalls Publishing Co. The Tudor Gothic structure is located in Lithopolis, OH and is the site for a variety of services: programs, meeting rooms providing space for community organizations, an auditorium used for cultural arts and travelogue films, and traditional library services.

Name: Foreword Library
Description:
Our team at Foreword aims to fill the lacuna in the hearts of readers all over Jakarta. We provide books picked up from all over the world, delicately loved, and carried back home for your enjoyment. Not only do we showcase a thoughtfully curated selection, but we hope to offer a comfortable space to indulge in it.

Our collection mainly consists of English books. The genres in our General Reading Room include Classics,  General Fiction,  Fantasy/Sci-Fi, Mystery/Thriller, Poetry, Drama, and Non-Fiction (mainly memoirs and books on books). Our Tweens & Teens Reading Room comprises middle-grade and young-adult Contemporary and Fantasy novels.

Our meeting room and tea room are also available as a space for you to work and collaborate.

Name: Magus
Description:
Magus Club de Lectura
Un refugio para quienes encuentran en los libros algo más que palabras. Magus es un espacio para lectores que buscan historias intensas, oscuras, enigmáticas y llenas de significado. Nos sumergimos en mundos donde la niebla oculta secretos, la poesía coquetea con lo siniestro y cada página es un conjuro que nos transforma.
Aquí no hay reglas rígidas, solo pasión por la lectura. Desde el gótico clásico hasta el terror contemporáneo, la fantasía oscura y la filosofía de lo extraño, exploramos historias que dejan marcas.
Si los libros son tu ritual y la literatura tu refugio, bienvenido a Magus.

Name: Northampton Area Public Library
Description:
This is the official LinkedIn page for the Northampton Area Public library, serving the Northampton Area School District in Eastern PA.

Mission:
To be the first point of access for information, self-enrichment, leisure, and social connections

Vision:
The Northampton Area Public Library strives to be a dynamic source of information, services, and programs in an ever-changing community, always something new 

History:
The first public library in Northampton opened its doors on August 23, 1965. It was located in the bay at the north end of the Northampton Memorial Community Center (next door). At the end of 1966 it was determined that the library location was inadequate for the public needs and a building fund was established by Dr. George A. Eichler, with a contribution on October 19, 1966, but the fund grew very slowly. 

In October 1971, Mr. and Mrs. George H. Schisler announced that they would provide $125,000.00 for the construction of an Area Public Library Building.  Mr. Jay Ferreira of Bond and Miller, Associates, was selected to be the architect for the building.  In October 1971, the Borough of Northampton deeded the land, located on Laubach Avenue, for the new building. On April 17, 1972, the charter of incorporation was issued.  The charter lists it as a nonprofit corporation which can accept tax deductible monetary gifts. The area it serves includes the boroughs of Northampton, Bath, and Chapmans, as well as the townships of Allen, East Allen, Lehigh, and Moore. On September 30th, 1973, the George H. Schisler Building of the Northampton Area Public Library, Inc., was dedicated in honor of Mr. and Mrs. George H. Schisler.

In 1984, George and Hattie Schisler donated funds for the construction of the Children’s Wing.  Seibert, Ferreira, Schlicting were selected as the architect and John F. Simock Inc. was selected as the general contractor for the addition.  The Children’s Wing was dedicated to George and Hattie Schisler on Sunday, October 27, 1985.

Name: DLM_bibliotheek
Description:
Het Damesleesmuseum is een van de grootste particuliere bibliotheken in Nederland. Het Damesleesmuseum – een in die tijd gebruikelijke aanduiding van een bibliotheek – werd opgericht in 1894. In die tijd hadden vrouwen geen toegang tot openbare bibliotheken. Het Leesmuseum voorzag dan ook in een behoefte. Momenteel heeft Het Damesleesmuseum circa 380 leden. Sinds 1974 kunnen ook mannen lid worden van de bibliotheek.

Het Damesleesmuseum biedt haar leden een grote collectie Nederlandse, Franse, Engelse en Duitse boeken; in totaal ruim 35.000 stuks. Bij de oprichting is besloten boeken in de moderne talen in de oorspronkelijk taal aan te schaffen. Incidenteel worden boeken in de Nederlandse vertaling aangekocht. De collectie wordt iedere maand uitgebreid met een selectie van nieuw verschenen boeken. Daarnaast is er een leestafel met Nederlandse en buitenlandse tijdschriften. 

In tegenstelling tot andere bibliotheken heeft Het Leesmuseum nauwelijks boeken weggedaan. De collectie beschikt dan ook over veel oude uitgaven en eerste drukken

Name: Traveling Toys, Inc.
Description:
In 2019, Mary Didiuk, PhD, and Diana Caty co-founded Traveling Toys, Inc., a 501(c)(3) non-profit. Our mission is to provide free access to high-quality, age-appropriate toys to promote play. By establishing toy libraries and play spaces, we seek to enhance the development, creativity, and learning of children
and teens. 

Traveling Toys has created 24 toy lending libraries within public libraries in CT and RI. Funding has been secured for the creation of six more. In 2023, our toy lending libraries circulated 22,000 toys and games. We significantly
expanded our reach in 2024 as we circulated 70,000 toys and games across
Connecticut. These toys and games can be borrowed for three weeks or played with on-site. All you need is a library card!

Traveling Toys provides the following services to support its mission:
• Identifies local funding sources.
• Applies for funding.
• Transfers awarded funds to partner libraries.
• Assists with toy selection and implementation.
• Participates in advertising efforts.
• Conducts follow-up assessments.

Our goal is to improve the quality of life for children, teens, and families. The positive impacts are:

• Educational: The toy library offers opportunities for free play, which has been
shown to improve reading, math, and science skills.
• Environmental: Reusing toys promotes the conservation of resources.
• Financial: Toy sharing eases the economic burden on families.
• Social: The toy library is a communal space allowing children and teens to share
toys.

2025 Highlights:
• Opened our 20th toy lending library at the Island Free Library, Block Island, RI
• Secured funding for 6 more toy libraries.
• Circulated 70,000 toys.
• Funded 3 play spaces at Saint Nicholas Children's Hospital in Lviv, Ukraine.
• Featured on NBC’s The Kelly Clarkson Show 6/2025.
• Funded the creation of a toy library at Pulaski Middle School in New Britain.

Name: @ Home Librarian
Description:
The @ Home Librarian blog is put together by me, Tibby Wroten. I started this blog several years ago just after my daughter was born. It was part of a plan keep in touch with the library world while I was home with her. Nearly seven years later I am still home with her most days, but have started up with other projects too.

At this point my blog has evolved into mostly posts about the books I’ve read. I write them to remind myself of what I thought, but also to offer my thoughts to other librarians who may be considering purchasing books for their libraries. This idea colors a lot of what I say in the reviews. I don’t do much plot synopsis or analysis, unless it will help me (or other librarians) decide if a book is right for their collections. I also like to keep parents in mind too, because I know it can be hard to find good books to have at home.

I am passionate about diversifying library collection and encouraging others to do the same. But I am white, cis, hetero, able-bodied, and female in a profession that is predominantly those things. I am still very much learning about diversity and how to talk about it and also trying not to take up too much space. Currently a lot of the books I am reviewing feature some sort of diversity because our collection is in dire need of it. My reviews are geared toward finding those books, talking about them and trying to make sure I point out the diversity in them so other librarians can draw on the reviews to build their own collections.

Occasionally I write about makerspaces and I’ll include reflections on conferences and various other things.

UNEC Kitabxana-İnformasiya Mərkəzi Azərbaycan Dövlət İqtisad Universitetinin (UNEC) aparıcı struktur bölmələrindən biri olub, təlim-tərbiyə və elmi tədqiqat prosesini tədris, elmi, metodiki ədəbiyyatla və informasiya materialları ilə təmin edən və bilikləri təbliğ edən mənəvi və intellektual əlaqələrin yayılması mərkəzidir.
Azərbaycan Dövlət İqtisad Universitetinin əsası qoyulduğu 1930-cu ildən başlayaraq universitetdə kitabxana da fəaliyyət göstərir. Lakin bir neçə dəfə universitetin bağlanması, başqa ali məktəblərin tərkibinə birləşdirilməsi, tədris binalarının yerinin dəyişdirilməsi nəticəsində kitabxana da fəaliyyətini dayandırmalı olmuş, fonduna böyük zərər dəymişdir. Sonradan kitabxananın fəaliyyəti 1966-cı ildən bərpa edilmiş və Əsaslı kitabxana kimi işini davam etdirmişdir. 15 aprel 2019-cu il tarixindən etibarən Əsaslı kitabxananın bazasında UNEC Kitabxana- İnformasiya Mərkəzi (KİM) yaradılmışdır.

UNEC KİM ümumilikdə, 80 736 nüsxə (2020-ci ilin hesabatına əsasən) Azərbaycan, ingilis, rus və türk dillərində kitab, tədris proqramı, dissertasiya, avtoreferat, elmi jurnal və digər kitabxana-informasiya resursları ilə oxuculara ənənəvi və elektron xidmət təqdim edir.

Mərkəzin strukturunda 6 kitabxana fəaliyyət göstərir.

Name: Chocorua Public Library
Description:
Welcome to the Chocorua Public Library – A Place Where Community and Literary History Meet

The Chocorua Public Library is one of the few privately funded libraries in New Hampshire open to the public. Managed by a dedicated board of trustees, passionate staff, and a supportive community, it has been a local treasure since its founding in 1888.

Originally established by Boston educators and businessmen to promote literacy among Chocorua’s summer visitors and year-round residents, the library began as a subscription-based service with a fee of just $1 per year. In 1891, access expanded to all Tamworth residents. Today, the library is free and open to all, offering books, technology, and community programs.

Our story is filled with unique historical ties. William James, known as the father of American psychology and a longtime summer resident of Chocorua, was an early supporter and visitor. Another fascinating chapter in our history features Hadley Hemingway, the first wife of renowned author Ernest Hemingway. After her marriage to Hemingway, Hadley went on to marry journalist Paul Mowrer, a Pulitzer Prize-winning correspondent who had deep roots in Chocorua. During their time here, Hadley served as the librarian at Chocorua Public Library, bringing her own love of books and a connection to the literary world.

The library’s physical home has also evolved. From its beginnings in the Chocorua Post Office to cramped quarters in the old Runnells Hall, the library finally found its permanent home in 1969 thanks to a generous bequest from Elizabeth Whittemore. Today’s library is warm and inviting—a true community living room filled with light, conversation, and of course, books.

Chocorua Public Library proudly blends small-town friendliness with big-library services. As part of local cooperatives and the New Hampshire State Library system, we continue to grow our resources and programs while honoring our rich history.

Name: Canndor Herbarium
Description:
Founded in 2016, Canndor is the world's first and only cannabis herbarium.

We are the industry’s go-to source for establishing plant records as a true history vault. As a herbarium, we create dried archived plant pressings of vegetative, flowering or whole plant material of your plants as a time stamped record of its existence. Such work is integral to the record of plant cultivation and required for cultivar definitions in the broader natural product space. It is now brought to cannabis through Canndor.

Herbaria are traditional houses of dried, preserved plant material, known as plant vouchers, that serve as a library of historical and biological records. They act as the foundation of real cultivar standards set forth by the cannabis community, as cultivar definitions, appellatory definitions and proof of claims with immutable, referenceable records. Herbarium specimens are physical proof of cultivation and give long-time plant stewards a claim to their material that outsiders cannot dispute.

As a world class industry herbarium, Canndor’s mission is to document and preserve records of cannabis plants grown in the industry, in order to establish historical evidence of existence, link plant creators with their creation, and establish broader cultivar standards in partnership with the community that stewarded these plants themselves

Name: Goodnow Library
Description:
GOOD THEN. GOODNOW
Goodnow Then.
The Goodnow Library opened its doors on April 4, 1863. The library was a gift to the town of Sudbury by Sudbury Native John Goodnow. Mr. Goodnow bequeathed $20,000 to the town for “purchasing and keeping in order a Public Library for the benefit of the inhabitants of [Sudbury].” He also gave three acres of land, and $2,500 to erect the building.

With 2,300 volumes in circulation, 3,100 square feet of space and one librarian (Samuel Puffer), the new library welcomed people of all ages, offering resources that went well beyond books. The library was also a space to socialize just as it is today.

The library began as an octagonal building with windows on all sides and a cupola on top. Additions were added in 1885, 1894, 1971 and finally the largest in 1999. Throughout all the library’s changes, the original 19th-century building remains. It has been carefully restored to resemble the 1863 structure, and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places.

Goodnow Now.
Today – as it was 150 years ago – Goodnow is a central destination for learning, pursuing interests, and meeting friends and neighbors. People come to see one another just as much as to learn from a program or enjoy a book.

While true to its founding mission, Goodnow is always evolving, according to the library’s director, Esme Green. Referencing Shiyali Ramamrita Ranganathan, the father of library science, she compares the library to a living organism, which is always evolving.

“Libraries are never static – they never have been and never will be – and Goodnow is no exception. We’re always collecting new materials and bringing in new people. And we’re evolving even faster than in the past because of rapidly changing technology. We’re embracing these advances and growing with the times to keep our constituents both informed and engaged.

The library has been doubling its budget for e-books, adding thousands of titles to its free and ever-growing collection.

Name: IFLA Social Science Libraries Section
Description:
The Social Science Libraries Section brings together librarians who lead social science libraries as well as those who work through other academic and government libraries to serve scholars working on the big issues of our time—how people behave, work in groups, and affect the world around us.

Our librarians work with social science disciplines such as anthropology, communications, demography, data librarianship, economics, global studies, gender and women’s studies, geography and cartography, history and philosophy, political science, psychology, sociology, religious studies, and social work.

We work in Latin America, Russia, Africa, Europe, Asia, the Middle East, and North America.

Our section also provides training for librarians who wish to develop their skill in using social science research methods like library ethnography, assessment, strategic planning, text and data mining, and statistical analysis.

sDrone 
The moments when the drones formed Glagolitic and Cyrillic letters evoked applause and emotional reactions from the audience. Particularly moving was the scene featuring the illuminated silhouettes of the two brothers, shining brightly in the sky and reminding everyone of the significance of their work, which remains the foundation of Bulgarian culture and identity to this day.

We would like to express our sincere gratitude to the Municipality of Sliven for their support and for making this special day even more festive and sacred. Through our innovative drone show technology, we successfully blended tradition with modern advancements, creating an unforgettable experience for all attendees.

Name: Vancouver Association of Law Libraries
Description:

VALL hosts substantive and networking events for members and students to promote information exchange, continuing education, and collaboration among librarians and staff throughout British Columbia.
 
Each year, VALL publishes the VALL Review, the association's feature publication that includes articles authored by our membership.
 
Membership is open to anyone who works in law libraries, works with legal information, or self identifies as a legal information professional.
 
The Annual Member is entitled to:
• access to the VALL website including the VALL Directory, the VALL Review, and the VALL Blog
• admission to substantive, educational sessions
invitations to networking events with other law library professionals and students
• volunteer to serve on the VALL executive
• apply for the Peter Bark Bursary after they have been a member for at least one year
• be considered for Lifetime Membership status.

Name: Geneva Public Library
Description:
The Geneva Public Library was built in 1912 as a Carnegie library. A 5,600 square foot addition was built in 1995, bringing the total square footage of the library to 15,200. 

The Library is open Monday through Saturday for a total of 46 hours per week. The library is currently staffed by one full-time and two part-time employees, and the gardens surrounding the library are maintained by the Secret Garden committee.

The Library maintains over 24,000 volumes. Resources include books, magazines, VHS and DVD movies, books on CD, and music CD’s. The library has eleven computers with wireless internet service and various software, a fax machine, and a copy machine. There are three meeting rooms available to the public for various uses.

The Library offers programs and activities year round, including LEGO Night the fourth Thursday of each month from 6:00 to 8:00 p.m., Food For Fines program each May, summer reading program from mid-May to to July 31, and a used book sale for one week the beginning of December, just to name a few.

The Genealogy Society of Fillmore County meets in the Small Meeting Room of the Library on the first Monday of each month at 2:00 p.m.

Name: ARTUR IMAGES GmbH
Description:
Since 1996, ARTUR IMAGES, has been dedicated to gathering the greatest architectural imagery from around the
world and publishing it to those in search of the highest artistic and technical quality in that guild of photography.
Over the years, as we moved from original transparency film to an all digital online archive, we’ve seen a lot of
things change, but one thing has remained constant: our commitment to collect and hold in stock the defining art of
architectural photography in general and the one of our photographers in particular. 

The foundation of our archive is the work of such masters of photography as Heinrich Heidersberger, Klaus Frahm,
Tomas Riehle, Roland Halbe, Zooey Braun, Victor Brigola, Jochen Helle and many many others.
To maintain as a vital source of imagery we are looking at the world of photography with open eyes and arms
and continuously subjoin the work of photographers that can meet our rigorous standards.

All projects are presented in respect of the expertise of each individual photographer as an observer, recognising
him as one that not simply records work, but interprets it, is helping all of us, including the architects to understand
what has been created. Whenever possible, we emphasise work that has been created very close to the architect
to benefit from the relationship between an architect, as a building's prime author, and an architectural
photographer, as the visual interpreter of its ultimate shape and form, that can be greatly influenced by their
mutual respect for each other's work.

We wish to enable clients, visitors and sole admirers of our archive to appreciate architecture in context through
the eyes of our photographers and carry on their experiences beyond our reach into their oikos.

For this we will ambitiously pursue our goal to more and more create the most significant archive of historic and
contemporary architectural photography available to you.

Name: Univerzitetna knjižnica Maribor
Description:
Univerzitetna knjižnica Maribor (UKM) na temeljih dolge zgodovine – ustanovljena je bila namreč leta 1903 kot knjižnica Zgodovinskega društva za slovensko Štajersko – uspešno prepleta tradicijo in sodobne informacijske tehnologije, uporabnikom ponuja bogat knjižnični fond in prostor za študij in prosti čas ter spodbuja znanje in inovacije. 

Poslanstvo knjižnice je večplastno. Je osrednja knjižnica Univerze v Mariboru, ki podpira izobraževalno, znanstvenoraziskovalno in umetniško delo in ustvarja podstat za njihov razvoj, koordinira knjižnično dejavnost Univerze v Mariboru in povezuje visokošolske knjižnice univerze v enoten sistem. Je tudi domoznanska knjižnica za Maribor in severovzhodno Slovenijo, ki širi védenje o lokalni zgodovini in aktualnem dogajanju ter sodeluje pri oblikovanju kulturne podobe mesta in univerze. UKM odlikuje edinstvenost posebnih zbirk, dragocena gradiva ponuja skupnosti v digitalizirani obliki. Je tudi druga depozitarna knjižnica, saj trajno hrani pisno in kulturno dediščino Republike Slovenije.

Name: Shelbyville-Bedford County Public Library
Description:
The Shelbyville-Bedford County Public Library serves residents in Bedford County, Tennessee. Established in 1948 by the Veteran of Foreign Wars Ladies Auxiliary, what was known as the Bedford County Memorial Library was once housed in the unused passenger waiting room of the SC&StL Railway depot. Although The American Legion Auxiliary worked to initiate a library in the 1930s, records indicate it was burned in the courthouse fire of 1934. In 1964, when the General Services Administration provided use of the former post office as a public library, the remodeled building was named in honor of dedicated supporter of The American Legion Auxiliary's original efforts to form a library, Argie Cooper. In 2014, what is now the Shelbyville-Bedford County Public Library broke ground for it's own home where it currently stands.



Today, the Shelbyville-Bedford County Public Library has become a valuable resource for residents offering a vast digital collection including audio books, e-books, DVDs, magazines, movies, and TV shows in a variety of formats. The library circulates thousands of items a year and has librarians skilled in local resources and all aspects of reading for pleasure and research. Free to the public are multiple computers and Wi-Fi available. The Children’s and Teen departments feature weekly activities for all ages. The library offers local history archives, an extensive historical newspaper collection, and information helpful in researching genealogy.

Conference sessions are proudly presented in both English and Spanish and include live translation. Seguimos Creando Enlaces 2021 will be offered in a virtual format due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

Seguimos Creando Enlaces 2021 is presented by the Southern California Library Cooperative in partnership with the SERRA Library Cooperative, California State Library, Library 2.0 Virtual Conference Series, and the School of Information at San José State University. This conference is supported in whole or in part by the U.S. Institute of Museum and Library Services under the provisions of the Library Services and Technology Act, administered in California by the State Librarian.

Librarians, library staff and library school students are encouraged to attend.

Name: Carnegie Free Library of Connellsville
Description:
Carnegie Free Library is a member of Fayette County Libraries and was the 13th library that Andrew Carnegie commissioned in the United States. It is a member of the WAGGIN network system, a shared and integrated library catalog comprised of Fayette, Washington and Greene county libraries.

Carnegie Free Library’s mission is to meet the educational, informational and recreational needs of the community. The library connects the community to a wide range of materials and resources available for lifelong learning, personal development and the pursuit of knowledge.

The library opened its doors in on April 30, 1903, due to the generosity of Andrew Carnegie, who believed that education is the cornerstone of success. At that time, Connellsville was the leading coke producer in the world and immigrants were flocking here to work in the mines and coke works. The library was named to the National Register of Historic Places in 1981. Library patrons now have access to more than a million volumes including magazines, e-books, audio books, music and movies through the WAGGIN network.

Carnegie Free Library is also home to Cafe Carnegie and the Connellsville Area Historical Museum.

Společnost KP-SYS byla založena v roce 1995. Od svého vzniku se plně věnuje vývoji knihovních systémů. Postupně jsme začali poskytovat služby všem typům knihoven, kde zvláště specializované knihovny ocenily široké možnosti konfigurace a úprav systému, a také i naši připravenost jej dále rozvíjet dle individuálních požadavků zákazníka

Robustní databázový základ – Oracle, Firebird
Variabilita nasazení – servery Windows i Linux
Třívrstvá architektura zajišťuje bezpečnost a spolehlivost systému

Ve všech verzích programu zpracování všech typů dokumentů, exporty a importy dat, spolupráce se soubornými katalogy, lokalizace systému, definovatelné klávesové zkratky a vzhled programu, naplánované úlohy jako např. automatické odesílání předupomínek, zálohování, aktualizace programu. Tiskový modul s robustním návrhářem tiskových sestav, Z39.50 klient, kompletní správa výměnných fondů, regionální systémy. Plná integrace RFID.

Podporované protokoly a standardy:  
MARC21, UNIMARC, MARCXML, ISO2709, ISO960 – vše v UNICODE
Z39.50 (i update), OAI-PMH, NCIP, RFID,SIP2
RDA, AACR2 – včetně automatické kontroly interpunkce a pravidel RDA
LUCENE – fulltextové vyhledávání

A především – trvalý a nepřetržitý vývoj systému

Name: Nicolet Federated Library System
Description:
The Nicolet Federated Library System (NFLS) is a state-funded organization assisting 42 member public libraries in providing better services to the people of northeastern Wisconsin since 1976.

NFLS is one of 16 public library systems in the state of Wisconsin and is organized under the WI Dept. of Public Instruction. Each system is governed by an independent board of trustees and funded by state aids to carry out the service requirements set forth in Chapter 43 of the Wisconsin Statutes.

NFLS' 42 members are the public libraries of 8 Northeast Wisconsin counties: Brown, Door, Florence, Kewaunee, Marinette, Menominee, Oconto and Shawano.

The NFLS members consist of 42 public library locations made up of 15 independent main libraries with 27 branches among them. There is a total of 45 service points when counting the Brown Co. Library Bookmobile, the library station at the Aurora Financial Center in Aurora for the Florence Co. Library, and the Bay Lakes Bank in Luxemburg for the Algoma Public Library.

Mission Statement: NFLS provides member libraries cost effective and efficient access to resources, support, and expertise, resulting in vibrant community resources that better serve their patrons. 

Vision Statement: Nicolet will be a visionary, adaptable, and member-focused organization that facilitates and encourages networking, partnerships and delivering state-of-the-art services to enhance the potential of member libraries.

Name: Homeless Oxford Literature Drive - HOLD
Description:
HOLD is a non-profit charity based in Oxford. Motivated by our desire to support the increasing number of homeless people seen sleeping rough on the streets, we work to distribute books amongst the homeless community in the hopes that we can offer enjoyment and respite.
While homelessness continues to rise in the United Kingdom, we know that the problem cannot be solved in a few simple steps. However, we feel that steps can be made to better the wellbeing of vulnerable members of our society. 

Although we are a relatively new charity, we hope to find our feet and make a difference in Oxford. We are currently planning book drives for Trinity, 2019, and hope to continue our work into the next academic year.

Name: Brockworth Link
Description:
Since 2011, Brockworth Link (formerly known as Brockworth Community Project) has provided Youth Work in the village of Brockworth after Gloucestershire County Council made the decision they were no longer able to do so. This has included running a youth club in the community centre and employing youth workers. Over the six years, Brockworth Link has financed this provision from grants from a variety of organisations, one of which is Brockworth Parish Council, who allocated £10,000 toward the total running costs of £25,000 per year.
However, in January 2017 the Parish Council informed Brockworth Link that our current contract to use the youth centre building was ending on the 31st March 2017. In addition, the Parish Council would no longer make any contribution to the Brockworth Link’s costs of running the youth activities in the next financial year.
Our youth work has received excellent praise and comments over the years. We were particularly pleased by comments made by the Rt. Hon. Countess Bathurst, High Sheriff of Gloucestershire in November 2016, when she visited a working youth session.
Clearly, our loss of this contract is very disappointing, but BCP believes that it can deliver an excellent youth service using other routes of finance. Therefore, Brockworth Link has decided to provide youth services under their own name and look elsewhere for funding.
We want to let the young people of Brockworth know they can still find a safe and comfortable place to come in the evening, run by experienced, well qualified staff that they know and trust. The only difference will be the venue. We are running youth work sessions from the Library building and exploring other venues within Brockworth. Sessions are running on Mondays, Wednesdays and Fridays and commence from the 3rd April 2017.

Name: Valley Park Community Library
Description:
The Valley Park Community Library was founded in 1940 when a group of concerned citizens met and decided a library would be beneficial for the town. The library operated on donations from the public; even listing in the newspaper certain books that they would like to have. Donations were received from organizations and citizens of the community to be used to purchase shelving and more books. The library officially opened on Saturday, December 14, 1940, and "despite inclement weather and the approaching Christmas Season, a 'goodly' number of people visited the library and took out reader cards."

The library became tax supported in 1943 when the City levied a one mill tax to finance the library. In 1996, the Valley Park Community Library joined with other independent public libraries around St. Louis to establish the Municipal Library Consortium of St. Louis County. 

The MLC shares reciprocal borrowing with the St. Louis Public Library and the St. Charles City/County Library, and In 2018 the Valley Park Board of Trustees approved a similar agreement with the St. Louis County Library. Any patron that has a library card from an MLC library, can borrow books from any MLC library as well as major St. Louis and St. Charles libraries.

Anyone can get a free library card by bringing in a picture ID and proof of current address. Proof of address can be given via a driver license, utility bill, credit card or bank statement, etc.

Name: Projeto Social Biblioteca Ideal
Description:
O Projeto Social Biblioteca Ideal é uma nobre e visionária iniciativa de caráter pessoal, concebida com o propósito de estabelecer um centro cultural na região de Ibirité, especificamente no bairro Vila Ideal, uma das áreas mais vulneráveis do Município. Este empreendimento visa proporcionar à comunidade local, com um enfoque especial em crianças e adolescentes, o fomento à leitura, a pesquisa acadêmica, a formação cultural, a valorização das artes, o fortalecimento da cidadania e a inclusão digital. Almeja-se, assim, a concretização de um espaço dedicado ao desenvolvimento cultural e educacional, contando com a imprescindível colaboração da sociedade civil, da iniciativa privada e de instituições públicas.

Dentre as ações empreendidas, destacamos uma campanha promovida nas redes sociais para a arrecadação de livros, cuja repercussão motivou a criação de um projeto complementar. Este projeto se baseia na adaptação e reutilização de geladeiras, que, transformadas em "geladeiras literárias", foram estrategicamente posicionadas em locais de grande circulação de pedestres, como o hospital de Ibirité, o presídio municipal, praças e outros espaços de fácil acesso. Ao todo, foram distribuídas 21 "geladeiras literárias" pelas cidades de Ibirité, Belo Horizonte e Brumadinho, constituindo uma rede de disseminação de conhecimento acessível.

A Câmara Municipal de Ibirité, em reconhecimento ao valoroso impacto social do Projeto, realizou, no dia 25 de novembro de 2019, uma solenidade de "Moção de Elogios e Aplausos", celebrando a relevância desta iniciativa para o Município.

Para conhecer mais sobre o projeto, siga-nos em nossas redes sociais no Facebook e Instagram: @projetobibliotecaideal.

Name: The Vancouver Women's Library
Description:
We are a free lending library, resource network, and community space for and by women and girls. We encourage intergenerational, intercultural, multilingual conversations through our reading room, events, and programming, and believe in continuing the legacy of women-run bookstores, presses and libraries. Our membership has grown to over 400 women and girls in just one year, and our collection has expanded from 80 titles to well over 2000 and counting, in a variety of languages very much in demand. We are especially proud of our growing Spanish language collection and programming, with plans to expand multilingual children’s programming through collaboration with multilingual members and volunteers.

Our library differs from other public library institutions in that we do not ask for verification of citizenship, or landed status from members. Our only qualification for books entering our collection is that they are works written or edited by women, of any age, race, class, gender expression, or political views. Our catalogue is accessible to the public here. We see our books and space as belonging to the volunteers and members, not the city or any other institution. We are 100% volunteered powered and donations funded, with plans to establish ourselves as a non-profit community service co-op by year's end.

The use of the photos is not subject to any financial dealings being that the magazine is open to all public. Pluton-Magazine intends to make its heritage contribution by making its publications available to teachers, students, and National Education. The use of documents published by Pluton-Magazine for academic purposes does not require prior agreement.

Distanced from the immediate news, Pluto-Magazine takes an offbeat and critical look at the world, trying to account for its diversity and complexity.
