Name: Solano County Archives
Description:
The Solano County Archives--an all volunteer-run facility--holds the historical government records of Solano County dating back to the the county's inception in 1850. For over 2 decades dedicated citizens have 1) saved many elements of the collection from destruction, 2) worked to organize and preserve the collection, 3) created finding aids, and 4) provided public access to the collection. The collection is currently managed by a professional archivist working pro-bono.

The collection does not yet have official archival status, nor is the Archives on the county organizational chart. However, in July 2008, the County moved the historical records collection out of a surplus warehouse into a  leased temperature-controlled space. 

Currently the County provides nominal financial suppport for supplies and operations costs, resulting in an increased dependance on donations to sustain preservation efforts and research services.

Although library service in Winthrop preceded the erection of the Frost Building, it was the first facility in Winthrop devoted solely to that role.
 
A six-member elected Board of Trustees is charged with the responsibility of managing the Library. It develops policy and delegates daily operation to a qualified director and staff.
 
Board meetings are held monthly at the Library and are open to the public. Minutes of Trustee Board meetings are public record.
 
The Library staff consists of six full-time and four part-time employees, and one part-time custodian.
 
The Library is a proud member of the North of Boston Library Exchange, Inc. (NOBLE), which it joined in 1986, and of the Massachusetts Library System (MLS).

Description:
Firma Sokrates-software i jej system biblioteczny SOWA

Sokrates-software to polska firma z Poznania, która od początku lat 90-tych tworzy i rozwija oprogramowanie dla bibliotek publicznych, naukowych i specjalistycznych oraz innych organizacji pracujących z zasobami bibliotecznymi, wspierając je w codziennym korzystaniu z systemu. Zespół firmy tworzą bibliotekarze, informatycy, programiści i konsultanci, którzy w codziennej pracy łączą wiedzę merytoryczną i techniczną.

Głównym produktem firmy jest zintegrowany system biblioteczny SOWA - pakiet programów wspierających kompleksowe procesy biblioteczne, takie jak gromadzenie i katalogowanie zbiorów, udostępnianie materiałów, ewidencja zasobów czy prowadzenie katalogów online.

System SOWA rozwijany jest z myślą o usprawnianiu i porządkowaniu procesów bibliotecznych oraz o ułatwianiu pracy użytkownikom w zmieniającym się środowisku technologicznym. Uwzględnia obowiązujące standardy bibliotekarskie i realne potrzeby bibliotek, a jego funkcjonalność obejmuje m.in. obsługę katalogowania materiałów, wypożyczeń i rezerwacji, tworzenie katalogów online dostępnych przez przeglądarki internetowe oraz integrację z nowoczesnymi narzędziami i protokołami wymiany danych.

Name: Biblioteca Yarak
Description:
LA CETRERÍA - PATRIMONIO INMATERIAL DE LA HUMANIDAD

La Cetrería, antes llamada Volatería, es el “Arte y Disciplina” de entrenar aves de presa, llamase halcones, gavilanes, águilas e híbridos, con el único objetivo de la caza real. El entrenamiento a grandes rasgos conlleva mucha dedicación y constancia para preparar a la rapaz bajo el Condicionamiento Operante de Burrhus Frederic Skinner.
 
Queda en evidencia que se necesita cierto conocimiento y respeto antes de manejar una rapaz. Es imprescindible aclarar que no se busca humanizar al animal, el concepto de mascota esta fuera de lugar para la cetrería. Así mismo, aunque se utilicen técnicas de cetrería para reintroducir aves a la naturaleza, proyectos de repoblación salvaje y rehabilitación de las mismas, la cetrería nunca dejara de estar catalogada como actividad cinegética.
 
El cetrero o halconero podrá elegir el ave a entrenar dependiendo únicamente de la presa que quiera cobrar como del terreno de caza, pudiendo elegir entre el “Alto Vuelo” o el “Bajo Vuelo”. En la antigüedad estas modalidades de caza separaban claramente solo a los halcones para el Alto Vuelo de las demás aves de presa, por un concepto social de prestigio, nobleza y su predisposición de ¨cazar a la pluma¨. En la actualidad los cetreros del mundo matizan con respecto al uso de ciertas especies de rapaces para las dos modalidades.
 
Como cazadores existe un claro respeto por las presas como por la naturaleza, la cetrería dejo de ser un deporte burgués y se convirtió en una forma de vida que desvela una ¨Conciencia Ecológica¨ de conservación. Es por esto que en el 16 Noviembre de 2010 fue declarada como Patrimonio Cultural Inmaterial de la Humanidad por la UNESCO.

Biblioteca Yarak 

Palabra de origen asiático, describe el estado físico y mental de un ave de presa de bajo vuelo, donde el apetito y las ganas de cazar han llegan a un nivel muy alto. 

Las rapaces demuestran un control del campo absoluto, en donde la mayoría de los lances suelen acabar con captura. Este estado psicológico agresivo es característico de los Accipiters y se manifiesta en una postura muy recta del cuerpo y las plumas prietas.


La Misión  

Brindar acceso gratuito a su biblioteca a todos los cetreros, biólogos, veterinarios y personas naturales, que busquen información sobre la cetrería, las aves de presa y su manejo. Somos partidarios del conocimiento libre y lo que esta representa. 

La Visión

Ser un instrumento para desarrollar y establecer la cetrería en el Perú, así como la de despertar en la población una Conciencia Ecológica que vele por la conservación de la aves de presa y su hábitat natural, en todo el territorio nacional.

Established in 1973, the Delaware City Library is a contract library of the New Castle County Library System and a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization. We primarily serve Delaware City residents, although we have visitors from the entire state of Delaware. 

The library is open forty hours per week.  It is located in a building owned by the City of Delaware City and also houses the Delaware City Community Center.  The library is governed by a Board of Directors. 

We have nine computers and a meeting room available for public use, as well as a collection of over 24,000 items available for checkout. We also offer a variety of programs for children, teens, and adults.

Name: Baitul Hikmah Library
Description:
The Baitul Hikmah Library is a library located at UGM campus mosque. The Baitul Hikmah Library has great aspirations to become the Exemplary Mosque Library in Jogjakarta and the Best Mosque Library in Indonesia. We began to realize this goal with a variety of actions, ranging from routine discussions to the provision of the book itself. Our program consists of at least:
 Discussion and Virtual Learning Groups (using WhatsApp)
 Establish networks with various elements of society, mosques, etc.
 Virtualization, audios, and radio Books (cooming soon)
 Regular / thematic discussion / book review

Starting from October 2017, the library has reviewed several book titles. Just mention Muqadimmah (Ibn Khaldun), Hakadza Dzahara Jil Saladin wa Hakadza ‘Al Quds (Majid Irsan Kailani), or the Rising Crescent on the Banyan Tree (Mitsuo Nakamura).

Provision of library books is weighed in various themes / categories. Among these categories are Islamic Studies (such as aqeedah, fiqh, akhlaq, hadith qur'an, and da'wah), Manhaj (such as NU, Muhammadiyah, Persis, Hidayatullah, HTI, IM, etc.), Siyasiyah and Islamic Science (such as politics, economics , psychology, law, medicine, science in an Islamic perspective), as well as History, Biography, and Civilization.

Until now we have had no less than 2500 books, thousands of members, dozens of administrators, and various kinds of activities aimed at supporting the process of education, research, and community service in campus life.

Name: Boekenliefde.nl
Description:
Het doel van boekenliefde.nl is om boeken lezen nog leuker te maken. Boekenliefhebbers kunnen bijhouden welke boeken ze hebben gelezen en wat ze ervan vonden. Het is ook mogelijk om boekenvriendschappen te sluiten, de virtuele bibliotheek van vrienden te bekijken, en van elkaars beoordelingen, reviews en tips te profiteren.

Boekenliefde.nl heeft een paar handige extra's voor boekenliefhebbers die lid zijn van de bibliotheek. Bij de bibliotheek geleende boeken worden automatisch toegevoegd aan hun boekenlijst. Ook kunnen ze een herinnering ontvangen als boeken bijna terug moeten. En als ze een leuk boek vinden op boekenliefde.nl, kunnen ze direct kijken bij de bibliotheek of het boek, of een andere editie van hetzelfde werk, beschikbaar is (nog niet overal beschikbaar).

Name: Dé-Livre
Description:
Dé-Livre s’inscrit dans une démarche d’économie circulaire. Nous faisons circuler les livres que nous possédons déjà. Chaque emprunt leur donne une nouvelle vie et leur permet de toucher d’autres lecteurs. C’est une autre manière de consommer : plus humaine, plus solidaire. Parce que lire autrement, c’est aussi choisir de consommer autrement.

Notre mission est simple : faire circuler les livres et rendre la lecture accessible et désirable pour tous, sans barrière de prix, de temps ni de perception élitiste. Mais au-delà du livre, c’est le lecteur que nous voulons toucher : celui qui pense qu’il n’a pas le temps, celui qui croit que la lecture n’est pas pour lui, celui qui n’ose pas, ou qui a simplement besoin d’un petit déclic.

Nous croyons que chaque bibliothèque personnelle peut avoir un impact collectif. C’est pourquoi nous permettons à chacun de devenir partenaire, en mettant ses propres livres à disposition de notre communauté, tout en générant un revenu complémentaire. 
Tous les détails sont disponibles sur notre site internet, rubrique « Devenir partenaire ».

Name: Milanof-Schock Library
Description:
Milanof-Schock Library’s mission is to be a community resource that enriches lives through education, information, exploration, and socialization. MSL provides library service to our community in beautiful Lancaster County Pennsylvania and has an official service area that includes the municipalities of Marietta Borough and Mount Joy Borough, East Donegal Township, Mount Joy Township and Rapho Township. 

The Mount Joy Library began as a Girl Scout troop community project in 1962. Demand was so great that the library moved to a former store front at 22 East Main Street two years later. By the early 1990s community interest and demand for library services grew to the point that the need for a new facility was clear. Spearheaded by major donations by community members Anne Milanof and Clarence Schock, a capital fund drive raised $1.1 million dollars successfully establishing the new building located at 1184 Anderson Ferry Road in Mount Joy and changing the library’s name to Milanof-Schock Library.

More recently, Milanof-Schock Library’s dedication to service has earned it local, national, and international recognition being named “Best Small Library in America” by Library Journal and the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation. It cannot be overstated that the success of the library is because of the continued support of the community we serve. The library is here for the community, and the library is here because of the community.

Name: Editura Vidia
Description:
Editura VIDIA (înființată în 2009) şi echipa sa de cercetare AIMGROUP (1993) activează în domeniul terapiilor alternative și complementare, spiritualităţii, dezvoltării personale şi parapsihologiei. Creăm soluții holistice pentru generarea de salturi în evoluția personală, dinamizarea potențialului mental și atingerea stării ideale de sănătate. Editura VIDIA se remarcă prin unicitatea colecției de cărți în domeniul NLP (Programare Neurolingvistică) și notorietatea la nivel internațional a autorilor selectați. Titlurile din această colecție alcătuiesc bibliografia de referință pentru orice trainer de NLP.
Cărți precum „Călugărul care și-a vândut Ferrari-ul” și „Atinge măiestria în leadership” ne propulsează pe primul loc în topul celor mai bune cărți de spiritualitate și dezvoltare personală din lume. În același stil inconfundabil iese în față și colecția de cărți pentru terapii holistice – autorități în domeniu precum Samuel Hahnemann (părintele homeopatiei), Hulda Regehr Clark (cercetătoarea renumită pentru rezultatele sale de neegalat în vindecarea cancerului) și Theodore A. Baroody („Dieta alcalină”) pun la dispoziție metode non-invazive și eficiente pentru tratarea și vindecarea bolilor cunoscute și necunoscute.

"Nu toți cei care care citesc devin lideri. Însă toți liderii citesc!"
Harry S.Truman

Cu respect și apreciere pentru toți cititorii de cărți excepționale și pentru toți liderii în devenire, Editura VIDIA!

Name: Stand Up, Stand Out
Description:
Stand Up, Stand Out is a children's picture book written by Joel Pilgrim & illustrated by Mitch Revs, which explores the world of mental health and well-being for young people.

When Parry decides to dress up in fancy dress and head to the beach, even he’s surprised by what comes next…

Join Parry and Tuey on their exciting adventures of self-discovery. An inspiring story which helps young people adjust to the world around them, this book takes the reader on a fun-filled journey, depicted through stunning illustrations, with one powerful message - it's OK to be different. 

About Parry:
Parry the parrot is the main character of the book, and is based on Grant Trebilco, the Founder of OneWave (www.onewaveisallittakes.com / @onewaveisallittakes). Grant's story is one of triumph in adversity, where following a diagnosis of bipolar disorder, he used surfing a a key to his mental health recovery. In 2013, he decided to go surfing in fancy dress to start conversations about mental health, which evidently kickstarted a global movement called Fluro Friday. Get in touch with Grant to hear his incredible story at your school or workplace. 
www.granttrebilco.com
@granttrebilco

About the author:
Joel Pilgrim is a mental health Occupational Therapist and has worked with Grant and OneWave for the last 4 years in changing the conversation surrounding mental health. He is now CEO of the Waves of Wellness (WOW) Foundation (www.foundationwow.org).Joel is a keen surfer and advocate for the freedom and healing abilities of nature and the outdoors. 
www.joelpilgrim.com
@joelpilgrim

About the Illustrator:
Mitch Revs is a proud Novocastrian, working full-time as a freelance artist. Mitch has had the opportunity to work on a diverse range of projects across Australia and America, with his powerful imagery and signature style. He’s well known for his colourful cartoon caricatures and bright beachside scenes - most of which are a reflection of his lifestyle. 
www.mitchrevs.com 
@mitchrevs

Name: Friends of the Milpitas Library
Description:
The Friends of the Milpitas Library (FOML) is an active non-profit organization dedicated to supporting the Milpitas Community Library.  The Friends is a membership organization of over 200 community members and library patrons. 

FOML receives donations of used books and and resells the books through a variety of channels: Amazon.com, the Book Nook at the library, occasional mini-sales, and three major sales per year.  Proceeds from the sales go directly to the library to support purchases and programs that fall outside of the library's budget.

An active group of volunteers meet regularly in the Friends room at the library to sort and box donations, manage the online sales through Amazon, and restock the Book Nook.  Three times a year, a larger group, including many student volunteers, comes together to stage the major sales.  Each major sale takes almost a week of effort: books are moved from the Friend room to the auditorium on Wednesday, unpacked, and arranged on tables, all before the sale opens on Friday evening.  Following the close of the sale on Sunday afternoon, the remaining books are packed out of the auditorium.  On Monday morning, work begins again in the Friends room to prepare for the next sale in four months time.  FOML is enormously grateful to its crew of dedicated volunteers who work 52 weeks a year to support the ongoing sales.

Name: Gloria and Hans Driessnack Digital Library
Description:
The Gloria and Hans Driessnack Digital Library is a 501c3 non-profit Virginia Nonstock corporation established in honor of Lt Gen (ret) Hans H Driessnack, known as the father of Earned Value Management, and Gloria Driessnack, his wife, who was a librarian.  The organization has been set up exclusively for educational purposes focused on the creation of a digital library.  The library shall promote the digital availability of material relating to the performance management of projects, programs, and/or portfolios as defined by American National Standard Institute (ANSI) standards. The non-profit shall facilitate digital storage for online access of historical information by researchers, educators, and practitioners.  Collections shall include both qualitative and quantitative data.  The library has the goal to use and/or create technology, such as decision science tools and large language models, to enhance the availability and usefulness of material to the broad community of researchers, educators, and practitioners.   
The library has been created approximately 60 years after Maj Hans Driessnack, US Air Force officer, laid out a criteria approach for Earned Value Management while also creating the concept of cost accounts, known today as control accounts.  
The library is being established within a Microsoft Teams site @Library.Driessnack.org.  The site is planned to be active by 1 Sept 2024.  If you are interested in gaining access to the site, please follow the Library's LinkedIN page.  We will contract you with further instructions.  If you are interested in providing material within a collection in honor of an individual or organization, please also follow the LinkedIN page and send us a comment.   

You can contact the Executive Library, John Driessnack, at john.d@driessnack.org.   Please, no solicitations, we will not respond.

Name: I'll Make You Think SMART | Books, Book Reviews & Food For Thought!
Description:
*** I'll Make You Think SMART | Books, Book Reviews & Food for Thought ***

===> https://IWillMakeYouThinkSmart.com/ <===

I am a 30-something Ph.D who reads a lot and enjoys sharing his reading notes and book reviews, I'll Make You Think SMART is my food for thought blog!

--- What can you find on I'll Make You Think SMART?

I'll Make You Think SMART regularly publishes food for thought, book reviews and comments on a variety of topics, ranging from business and self-development to politics, society and technology.

--- What is the I'll Make You Think SMART philosophy?

There is more and more information around there, and most of us just can't cope with everything. Still, we need to keep up with the news and we need food for thought here and then. I'll Make You Think SMART is about helping you to feed your thoughts, one great book at a time. I thoroughly read all the books I write about, and I only write on those I found worth my money and my time. 

--- Quick comments?

No, in-depth comments. I fully read all the books I review, and I do it with a critical eye. Hence, my reviews are deliberately lengthy and I'm totally unapologetic about that. I go into the details to help you pick books for the good reasons, because that's how reviews should be.

Description:
Le GRECH TDCLG est le groupe de recherche en Techniques de la documentation du Collège Lionel-Groulx. Il a été créé à l’automne 2016, dans le cadre d’un projet pédagogique, au Collège Lionel-Groulx. Name: Long Ridge Library
Description: The Long Ridge Library, located at 191 Long Ridge Road, was established in 1919 for the members of the Long Ridge Women’s Club. A few years later the library moved to its current building, a former one-room schoolhouse on Long Ridge Road. The New York Public Library, as well as local families, donated books to the initial collection. Today, contemporary books sit next to old volumes on the shelves, making it a fascinating treasure trove. The library, will soon be celebrating its 105th anniversary, serves over 500 families in the area: lending books, running book clubs, and hosting community events. As a public library, their services are free to residents of Danbury, Ridgefield, Redding, and Bethel. It is staffed by a group of dedicated volunteers, most notably, Barbara Fulton, head librarian for over 40 years.  The library is also known for hosting guest readings with local authors and plans on increasing events of various kinds.

Di Perpustakaan Riset kami, kami berkomitmen untuk meningkatkan aksesibilitas informasi dan memfasilitasi pertukaran pengetahuan di antara komunitas akademik kami. Kami juga aktif dalam menyelenggarakan berbagai kegiatan seperti seminar, lokakarya, dan diskusi ilmiah untuk memperkuat jejaring akademik dan meningkatkan kualitas riset di Universitas Islam Negeri Syarif Hidayatullah Jakarta.

Bergabunglah dengan kami di perjalanan pencarian pengetahuan yang tak pernah berakhir, di mana inovasi dan kolaborasi menjadi kunci bagi kemajuan ilmiah dan akademik. Sama-sama kita wujudkan visi pengetahuan yang holistik dan berkelanjutan untuk masa depan yang lebih baik.

Description:
Le Diplôme Universitaire en Sciences de l’Information et des Bibliothèques (DUSIB) est un diplôme universitaire en partenariat international, pouvant être obtenu via une formation à distance. 
Au cœur d’un réseau de partenariat développé au fil des années basé sur la mise en œuvre des actions de coopération internationale, visant à diffuser le savoir ainsi qu’à soutenir les projets et les services servant la communauté professionnelle des bibliothèques, la Bibliotheca Alexandrina (BA), l’École Nationale Supérieure des Sciences de l’Information et des Bibliothèques (ENSSIB), la Bibliothèque nationale de France (BnF), l’Université Senghor d’Alexandrie et l’Association des Amis de la Bibliotheca Alexandrina lancent un Diplôme Universitaire en Sciences de l’Information et des Bibliothèques (DUSIB), destiné aux spécialistes francophones.
DUSIB est un diplôme d’un an accrédité acquis par une formation à distance, composé de huit modules théoriques (36 crédits), donnés par l’Université Senghor et l’ENSSIB, et suivi d’un stage pratique obligatoire de 6 semaines fourni par des spécialistes de la Bibliotheca Alexandrina, de la Bibliothèque nationale de France ou d’une grande bibliothèque francophone professionnellement reconnue. Par le biais de ce stage, les étudiants mettront en pratique leurs connaissances théoriques acquises durant la formation.
DUSIB permettra aux bibliothécaires et aux spécialistes des services d’information de poursuivre leurs carrières dans le domaine de la bibliothéconomie et les sciences de l’information avec efficacité. Il leur offrira également l’occasion de mettre à jour et d’approfondir leurs connaissances et leurs compétences professionnelles nécessaires.
Le contenu académique des cours couvre cinq thèmes essentiels, à savoir:
1. Collections et politique documentaire
2. Service public,
3. Ressources numériques,
4. Gestion du patrimoine,
5. Médiation et ingénierie culturelle.
Le DUSIB accueillera des étudiants francophones sélectionnés en fonctio

Name: Kristine Mann Library
Description:
Focusing on the work of C.G. Jung, the Kristine Mann Library is an important and accessible resource for Jungian studies that is open to the general public.  Established in the 1940’s by the Analytical Psychology Club of New York, the KML is the oldest Jungian library in North America. We collect and catalog books, journals, papers, and audiovisual materials by and about C.G. Jung and others in the field of Jungian psychology.  Our holdings also include materials in related areas of study, such as theology, alchemy, mythology, symbolism, the arts, anthropology, psychoanalysis and general psychology.

Founded to serve members of the Analytical Psychology Club, analysts-in-training with the C. G. Jung Institute of New York, and other members of the Jungian community, the library’s mission is to provide intellectual access, as well as assistance and training in the location and use of its unique collection.  The library is the steward of its present resources and of the information and services that will become available in the future.

Name: Nicholas P. Sims Library & Lyceum
Description:
Nicholas P. Sims donated his estate to build a public library for Waxahachie, and Ellis County. After his death, the library was built during 1903-1904 on land donated by Waxahachie's first Mayor, Capt. W.H. Getzendaner. The building opened to the public April 26, 1905, and was one of the first public libraries built in Texas as well as one of the first privately endowed libraries in Texas. The Nicholas P. Sims Library is a private non-profit that continues to operate as a public library.

The vision of the Nicholas P. Sims Library is a community resource that helps build healthy families, vibrant neighborhoods, and thriving businesses -- the foundation of a strong Waxahachie.

The Nicholas P. Sims Library's mission is to provide the best library services to the residents of Waxahachie in the most efficient and cost effective manner possible by:
   * Promoting reading, reading readiness programs and cooperative literacy programs.
    * Offering the opportunity for self-development to people of all ages and all stages of education.
    * Recognizing that knowledge, information, and entertainment are presented in many formats such as printed word, audio, video recordings, and computerized databases.
    * Providing reference and genealogical specialized resources to sustain, and to preserve the heritage of Waxahachie and Ellis County.
    * Supporting the educational, civic, and cultural activities of individuals, organizations, and groups in the community.
    * Maintaining collections of resources which reflect the present, past, and a vision for the future.
    * Promoting and preserving those materials which chronicle the life and culture of the citizens of Waxahachie.
    * Making library materials available to all library card holders.

Name: Perpustakaan Universitas Islam Negeri Maulana Malik Ibrahim Malang
Description:
Keberadaan perpustakaan sejak berdirinya Fakultas Tarbiyah Malang Cabang IAIN Sunan Kalijaga Tahun 1961. Saat ini Perpustakaan menempati Gedung tersendiri seluas 8840 M2 yang terdiri dari 4 lantai yaitu lantai dasar (area parkir), lantai 1 (area sosial), lantai 2 (area riset), dan lantai 4 (area koleksi).

Dalam perkembangannya, berbagai ikhtiar dilakukan oleh perpustakaan untuk memberikan pelayanan terbaik bagi sivitas akademika dan untuk mendukung tercapai mimpi besar universitas yang telah ditetapkan dalam visi, misi, dan tujuan UIN Maulana Malik Ibrahim Malang. Ikhtiar ini meliputi peningkatan dan perbaikan kualitas layanan melalui peningkatan kuantitas dan kualitas sumber daya manusia, layanan dan fasilitas perpustakaan. Wujud dari ikhtiar dan komitmen perpustakaan untuk melakukan perbaikan dan peningkatan kualitas layanan, di antaranya, telah berhasil dua kali terakreditasi A yaitu periode akreditasi (2017-2020) dan (2020-2025). Selain itu, ikhtiar lain adalah memperluas jejaring melalui kerjasama antar perpustakaan, asosiasi perpustakaan tingkat nasional dan internasional. Sejak 2021, perpustakaan UIN Maulana Malik Ibrahim Malang menjadi anggota IFLA (International Federation of Library Associations and Institutions) dan IATUL (International Association of University Libraries).

Name: Harrisonville Middle School
Description:
Theres a reason that students at Cass Career Center succeed. Cass
provides career training that instructs and guides adults, high school
students and special needs students in every area of their profession,
from the trade itself to the test for licensure.

Part of the Harrisonville Public School District in Harrisonville,
Missouri, Cass Career Center trains adults for careers in such areas as
Automotive Technology, Construction Technology, Fire Science and
Practical Nursing.

Cass also has programs designed specifically for high school students who
wish to investigate or get a head-start on a profession.

For high school sophomores and juniors who have special needs, Cass
Career Centers Life Careers program offers introductory training in
various health care fields such as Health Care and Nursing Assistance.

Students arent just given the tools and knowledge of their trade either,
but also test-prep skills for any licensing exams they will need to take.

Since Cass faculty are current working professionals in their field, all
Cass students are trained in the most current methods and technologies.

With this kind of relevant, high-quality training, its no surprise that
Cass students are so successful in national skills competitions, at which
they consistently win awards.

For information about admissions, tuition and financial aid, contact a
Cass Career Center staff member or representative.

    To promote open access to scientific, technical information and evidence on TCIM
    Promotion  informed decision-making, using the best available knowledge and evidence on TCIM

Name: Théâtrothèque Gaston Baty
Description:
Fondé en avril 1959 par Jacques Scherer, le Centre de Documentation Théâtrale se constitue à ses débuts à partir de l’acquisition par la Sorbonne, avec l’aide du CNRS, de la bibliothèque personnelle du metteur en scène Gaston Baty. Il est depuis l’origine lié à l’Institut d’Etudes Théâtrales, co-fondé par Jacques Scherer et Raymond Lebègue, en 1959.

Avec la création de la Sorbonne nouvelle en 1971 et le déménagement sur le site Censier, le Centre de Documentation Théâtrale, devenu Bibliothèque Gaston Baty, connaît un nouveau développement : politique documentaire axée sur l’actualité, acquisition de fonds patrimoniaux et de l’essentiel des parutions françaises et étrangères, comprenant des monographies, des périodiques et des travaux de recherche, des collections iconographiques, des supports multimédias, des programmes et des brochures, ainsi que des affiches sur les arts du spectacle.

La bibliothèque a été renommée Théâtrothèque Gaston Baty depuis 2012 et réaménagée en 2014. La salle de lecture dispose de 45 places assises, de trois postes informatiques à l’usage des lecteurs, d’un photocopieur et de quatre postes de visionnage. La Théâtrothèque participe au réseau national du SUDOC et au catalogue collectif de l’Université Paris 3 (Virtuose +), en collaboration avec la DBU. La Théâtrothèque participe également au réseau de Prêt Entre Bibliothèque (PEB).

Name: Medfield Memorial Public Library
Description:
Medfield Memorial Public Library is the public library of the town of Medfield, Massachusetts. Our library hosts weekly educational events for children, teens, and adults throughout the year, in addition to a growing makerspace and 'Friends of the Library' bookstore. 

In addition to providing museum discounts and passes, we work in conjunction with our Friends (FOL) organization in order to provide grants and scholarships, as well as annual library funding. Two 3D printers and a growing technology center in our makerspace, with design and crafting lessons, put us on the map with STEAM education.

A member of the Minuteman Library Network, our goal is to provide our resources in a variety of formats, also evolving with digital advances. As one of the first public libraries in the country, we recently celebrated our centennial event; in 1917 the keys to the library were officially handed to the town by town developer Granville Daley, and we make it our mission to keep the library open, free, and relevant to our public.

Name: Teozofska knjižnica in bralnica Alme M. Karlin - Katalizator modrosti
Description:
Leta 2009 je odprla svoja vrata prva in edina teozofska knjižnica v Sloveniji, Teozofska knjižnica in bralnica Alme M. Karlin. Knjižnica je bila ustanovljena kot vir in spodbuda za neodvisne raziskave. Naša knjižna zbirka zajema več kot 11.000 knjižnih enot. Posvečena je spominu na veliko slovensko pisateljico, popotnico, pesnico, zbirateljico, poliglotko in teozofinjo Almo Maximiliano Karlin. Alma M. Karlin, rojena v Celju, je slovenski fenomen pogumne ženske, ki se je leta 1919 podala s svojim pisalnim strojem, znamenito “eriko”, na osemletno nevarno in tvegano pot okoli sveta, o čemer je tudi pisala v svojih delih.

Poleg del, zbirk in fotografij Alme M. Karlin knjižnica ponuja javnosti tudi številna dela in zbirke s področja teozofije, filozofije, psihologije, sociologije, antropologije, svetovnih religij, ekologije, ekološke gradnje, ezoterične tradicije, osebnostnega in duhovnega razvoja. Obstajajo zapisi, da se je Alma M. Karlin družila s celjskimi teozofi, s teozofi se je srečavala tudi na svojih potovanjih po svetu. Teozofske elemente je med drugim vpletala tudi v svoja literarna dela.

Knjižnica Alme M. Karlin podpira raziskavo fizičnih, psihičnih in duhovnih moči, neločljivo povezanih s človeško in kozmično naravo. Knjige s tradicionalnih področij znanosti dopolnjujejo tudi dela o magiji, okultizmu, poganstvu, mistiki, prostozidarstvu, šamanizmu, kabali in drugačnih oblik sobivanja. Knjižne police Teozofske knjižnice polni tudi obilo gradiva o alkimiji ter najrazličnejših področij znanosti, duhovni ekologiji, obsmrtnih izkušnjah, znanstvenih anomalijah in nepojasnjenih fenomenih s področja biologije, evolucije in kozmogeneze.

Bralci lahko sledijo idejam, ki so ljudem različnih kultur pomagale najti globlji pomen v življenju. Poudarek se daje obsežnim zbirkam svetovno znanih teozofov, kot so Helena Petrovna Blavatsky, C. W. Leadbeater, Annie Besant, Alice Bailey, Mabel Collins in drugim avtorjem, katerih dela so v Sloveniji težko dostopna.

Name: Biblioteca Luna
Description:
Biblioteca Luna es un proyecto que nació en Madrid, en el año 2016, con la firme intención de actualizar y redescubrir ciertas obras que, por cuestiones de tiempo, han caído en el olvido, lo que permitirá al lector, curioso e interesado en la cultura antaño floreciente, el acercamiento a estas obras que intentaremos que sean entendibles, amenas y, por supuesto, rigurosas. Se compone de un equipo de jóvenes entusiastas, apasionados de la literatura y con el convencimiento de que su proyecto será innovador y una gran aportación al mundo de las letras.

Uno de los objetivos de la Biblioteca Luna ha sido reeditar las obras que iremos ofreciendo a lo largo de nuestro proyecto, dándoles una mayor amenidad y facilidad en su lectura, respetando, siempre, las obras y traducciones originales. Otro de los objetivos fundamentales es fomentar la visibilidad y el conocimiento de ciertas obras que, por causas dispares, han ido cayendo lentamente en el olvido y suelen ser desconocidos para el gran público.

Nuestra andadura empezó con la obra del historiador Polibio “Historia”, cuyo primer tomo supuso el comienzo de la Biblioteca Luna. Nuestra labor no paró aquí, sino que comenzó a desarrollarse con nuevas obras como “El Amor Criminal”, de Marie-François Goron o “Amor Vendado”, de Salvatore Farina, entre otras tantas. Todo ello supone una aportación positiva al lector, al cual queremos, y confiamos poder hacer, acercar a estas obras con la idea de que vuelvan a tomar contacto con estos autores.

Desde Biblioteca Luna agradecemos a todo aquel que se acerque a nuestro proyecto, al cual deseamos larga vida, con la intención de conocer las versiones más actualizadas y modernas de aquellos autores más desconocidos o clásicos.

Name: Mille Gru - PoesiaPresente
Description:
Mille Gru è un gruppo di ricerca nato nel 2006 a Monza dall’incontro tra Dome Bulfaro, Simona Cesana, Enrico Roveris, Patrizia Gioia e Cristina Spagna per sostenere principalmente lo sviluppo del progetto PoesiaPresente dedicato alla poesia contemporanea. Si è riunito in associazione culturale senza scopo di lucro nel 2007.

Da allora realizza incontri, serate poetiche classiche ed innovative, produzioni editoriali, teatrali e multimediali, mostre, seminari e percorsi didattici, individua e promuove i giovani talenti. Ha il compito di creare azioni culturali capaci di donare un senso poetico al vivere quotidiano, cooperando con altre realtà e cercando di favorire gli sguardi ad un tempo locali e globali; è un cantiere culturale che costruisce a cielo aperto voli reali, perché ogni vivere, se privato di Poesia, non può essere davvero Presente. 

I suoi progetti più rappresentativi riguardano le ricerche e le pratiche di Poetry Therapy e la valorizzazione della poesia performativa, e in dialogo con le altre arti, promossa grazie alla prima stagione poetica realizzata in Italia, PoesiaPresente, che ha debuttato il 15 novembre 2006.

Dal 2008 è anche casa editrice indipendente  e, con le sue pubblicazioni, diffonde le proprie ricerche rivolte principalmente alla poetry therapy e alla poesia orale e performativa.

Attualmente il Presidente di Mille Gru è Anna Castellari, vicepresidente Simona Cesana, consiglieri Dome Bulfaro, Simonetta De Donatis, Giacomo Nucci.

Il gruppo di Mille Gru è composto anche da: Patrizia Gioia, Nicole Bizzotto, Amelia Bortini, Barbara Colombo, Sara Elena Rossetti, Paolo Manzalini, Pepe Carbonell, Raffaella Fossati, Chiara Musso.

Name: RAPBRARY
Description:
Rapbrary is a nonprofit literary arts organization founded by rapper and librarian Roy Kinsey.

At Rapbrary, we believe reading came first. Our mission is to preserve rap and hip-hop as culturally significant literary art forms while building libraries and sanctuaries for banned books, marginalized authors, and stories that shape our communities.

Through performances, book clubs, exhibitions, and community programming, we bridge music and literature to inspire literacy, creativity, and liberation.

Rapbrary partners with cultural institutions and grassroots organizations across Chicago to curate libraries, launch literacy initiatives, and host transformative events that honor the written and spoken word.

Name: Albion District Library
Description:
Our Adult Room has a large and growing collection of both fiction and non-fiction. Our fiction collection is divided into General Fiction, Mystery, Romance, Western, Science Fiction/Fantasy, Inspirational, African-American Fiction, and Large Print sections.

The Children’s Room’s books are divided by age and reading levels. We currently have thousands of books, including board books, picture books,  readers (for children learning to read), chapter books,  graphic novels, popular series, and a large non-fiction section that is especially helpful for school assignments.

The Library’s Adult Room has a collection of over 7,500 DVDs, which grow monthly with new releases and additions of old favorites.
    This collection includes feature films, documentaries, exercise and health videos, as well as season sets of your favorite television series on DVD.  We also have a small, but growing, collection of movies on Blu-Ray.
    Our Children’s Room has over 1,000 DVDs, including family and kid-friendly feature films, and educational videos for children.
    Our DVDs and Blu-Rays check out for one week, with a maximum of 5 per patron, and a limit of 2 current month new releases, at a time.
    We have over 600 season of television series available, including children’s series! Ask about your favorite series.

eBooks and eAudiobooks in every genre (fiction, non-fiction, and children’s) are available free with your Albion District Library card to read on your computer or your favorite portable device through a service called OverDrive.

Name: Howe Library
Description:
The Howe Library was established as Hanover, New Hampshire’s public library in 1900 through a gift from Emily Howe to the newly-formed non-profit Howe Library Corporation.  Miss Howe dedicated the first Howe Library in her former family home on West Wheelock Street with “a prayer that this library may prove a blessing to this community to the remotest generation.”

The Howe Library Corporation managed the library and its operating budget until 1973 when Hanover residents voted to assume responsibility for the operating budget and the library became a department of the town. At the same time, the library was outgrowing its home on West Wheelock Street and plans began for a new building.  Constructed on town land in 1973, this new facility was financed by The Howe Library Corporation with a loan from the town and donations.  A large addition was completed in 2005, almost doubling the size.

The Howe Library continues to be a partnership between the Howe Library Corporation and the Town of Hanover.

Počátky historie Ústřední knihovny VŠB-TUO jsou spojeny se zahájením výuky na příbramské báňské škole v roce 1849. V tomto roce byla založena i knihovna. Od roku 1991 sídlí v nové budově v porubském kampusu, který patří k největším ve střední Evropě.

Své služby poskytuje Ústřední knihovna studentům, pedagogům, vědeckým pracovníkům a také zájemcům z řad veřejnosti ve výpůjčním oddělení a studovně v Porubě a na pracovištích na Fakultě bezpečnostního inženýrství a Fakultě stavební.

Nossa missão é oferecer às instituições de ensino superior o melhor conteúdo acadêmico possível, usando a mais atual tecnologia em nossa Biblioteca Virtual.

---

Multinacional

Fundada em 1998 a e-Libro foi a primeira empresa a publicar livros digitais em espanhol. Pouco depois, desenvolveu plataformas digitais para livros em castelhano e português. 

Nos últimos anos, a e-Libro expandiu suas atividades para atender os Estados Unidos, fornecendo títulos em espanhol. Hoje, a e-Libro atende também a América Latina, em países como Argentina e Chile. 

Em 2017, a e-Libro fundou a e-Livro, plataforma desenvolvida para atender o público brasileiro.

Nossa biblioteca

A e-Livro é uma Biblioteca Virtual, que reúne livros, artigos, teses, periódicos, revistas e outros conteúdos de diversas áreas acadêmicas. Nossa plataforma possui a tecnologia digital mais avançada do mercado. A nossa biblioteca:

• É compatível com qualquer navegador. 
• Permite leitura on & offline 
• É compatível com programas de conversão de texto em fala
• Oferece uma excelente assistência ao cliente

Name: EPICo (European Print Initiatives Collaboration)
Description:
EPICo (European Print Initiatives Collaboration) is an informal network of European academic and national libraries working together to keep vital printed research materials accessible—despite increasing pressure on physical library space and the shift to electronic formats.

In response to these challenges, EPICo brings together repository libraries and shared-print preservation initiatives to safeguard collections for today’s users and future generations.

Vision: To support the reliable and sustainable preservation of printed scholarly collections across Europe, ensuring long-term accessibility for researchers.

Mission: To facilitate shared print library collaborations across Europe by providing a forum for libraries to share expertise around storage, preservation, descriptive metadata, and sustainable policies.

OUR MISSION
The mission of OPUS Archives and Research Center is to preserve, develop and extend to the world the archival collections and libraries of eminent scholars in the fields of depth psychology, mythology and the humanities. OPUS is a “living archive” and offers scholarships, research grants, educational programs, community events, and research access to the collections, both physically and digitally.

OUR PURPOSE
OPUS is an active site for wonder and participation in the great questions of our time.

In addition to serving as a destination research facility, OPUS sponsors interdisciplinary dialogues, community networks, public forums, and creative conversation among researchers, scholars, students, and faculty from various disciplines on a broad series of topics relevant to the issues of the day and how they are informed by eminent scholars in the fields.

Michigan’s library cooperatives are funded by a portion of state aid based on the population served by the cooperative and a portion of state aid paid to member libraries for the purchase of cooperative services. Name: Biblioteca Girasol - Matapalo Community Library
Description:
Biblioteca Girasol is a small community library located in Matapalo, Nicaragua. Matapalo is a rural community of about 500 people in the mountainous region of Matagalpa, in the northwest part of the country. 

We believe lending libraries harness children’s early love for reading while also providing a safe and supportive learning environment. The children in our community love reading, but books are extremely hard to come by in this area. When you do come across them, the selection is limited and the prices are inaccessible. 

Since opening the library, we’ve built a collection of 200 books ranging from picture books to novels. In addition to operating as a lending library, we offer English classes, art classes and dance classes. On the weekends, we open as a free space for homework help, crafts and games. 

Youth leadership is central to our approach. Inspired by the Tipitapa-based Nicaraguan youth organizing team, el Equipo Arco Iris (The Rainbow Team), our team of 13 youth (ages of 12-17) is involved in the administration of the library at every level. We meet every Thursday to plan for upcoming events, reflect on the past week’s challenges and delegate tasks for the week ahead. They work as librarians checking out books to other children, support classes as teaching assistants, care for the library by cleaning up and organizing books, read to younger children, and sell snacks to fundraise for new materials.

Our mission is to inspire, empower, and enrich the lives of readers of all ages; to be a space where the community comes together to share ideas; and to promote and defend intellectual freedom.

Name: NLI USA
Description:
The National Library of Israel USA (NLI USA) builds support in the United States for the National Library of Israel to enhance and expand the impact of the Library’s collections, cultural programs, and educational resources. This is done through increasing public awareness, broadening relevant partnerships, engaging in programming, and strengthening philanthropic support. NLI USA is located in New York, and works closely with the National Library of Israel in Jerusalem.

A diverse board of directors from the fields of diplomacy, medicine, public policy, academia, business, and philanthropy lead the work of NLI USA. Jack Lew and David Makovsky serve as Co-Presidents. The board is supported by a staff who carry out the vision of renewal of the National Library of Israel.

Adina Kanefield is the CEO of NLI USA.

NLI USA, Inc. is a registered not-for-profit 501(c)(3) organization in the United States, rated 97/100 on Charity Navigator.

Name: Loudonville Public Library
Description: Established in 1905, the Loudonville Public Library began as a "reading room" organized by a group of dedicated citizens who raised funds and performed the tasks necessary to create a formal public library. The library moved to various locations throughout the village before finding its current location next to Central Park in the heart of Loudonville. The current 26,000+ square foot facility is the result of a 1976 building project that has been enhanced through exdpansions in 1982 and 2001 respectively. The library's materials and services havae evolved to meet patron demands and to account for changes in technology and the delivery of information, resulting in a modern and professional organization that is devoted to serving the public in a meaningful and valuable way. The library is currently a member of the Serving Every Ohioan (SEO) Consortium, sharing materials and an online catalog with public libraries across the state. This relationship expands the materials available to our patrons to over six million items coming from approximately 175 locations.

Name: Adilam Inc.
Description: Adilam is a library materials handling company offering, software, RFID solutions and equipment. Our full spectrum solution encompasses intelligent return chutes, automated sorting, self leveling bins, patron self checkout kiosks, staff stations, security pedestals and stocktaking.  Adilam Arcus, the world's first Cloud-based Barcode/HF/UHF RFID solution has been bridging the gap between traditional ILS systems and RFID technology for over 15 years.  With a global network of technology partners, and a highly skilled team of engineers, Adilam provides a range of custom solutions to suit a variety of applications.

Name: Macquarie Regional Library
Description:
Macquarie Regional Library (MRL) is a consortium of three local government areas, Dubbo Regional Council, Warrumbungle Shire Council and Narromine Shire Council, providing library and information services to residents and visitors. Under a Library Service Agreement, Dubbo Regional Council is the 
delegated Executive Council. 
Member Councils make an annual contribution based on an agreed funding formula. Additionally, a State Government annual library subsidy is paid to each Council and transferred to MRL for library service operations. Each 
member Council is responsible for building and maintaining the libraries and service points in their Local Government Area.

The Library serves a population of 69,511 (ABS, 2021) across 25,180 square kilometres. The Library is well positioned to cater for people’s needs at all ages and life stages, providing a wide range of programs and services for a diverse clientele spread across a wide geographical area.

Name: Valatie Free Library
Description:
The Valatie Free Library serves the Village of Valatie, the hamlet of Niverville, Kinderhook Lake, and the surrounding area of rural Kinderhook in Columbia County along the scenic Hudson River Valley of New York State. 

Begun in 1928 as a casual circulation of collected books, the Valatie Free Library Association was chartered in 1931 by the Valatie Parent Teachers Association. Housed in a local Church, the original library owned two-thousand books by 1939 when it moved to its current location—a 600 sq ft cottage.

During the past ten years, the Valatie Free Library has seen a literal explosion of activity; in 2002, the library offered just six programs for the public; last year, almost 140. Our need for a larger space to properly attend to this growth is solved by our proposed Sustainable Library project, which will increase our operational space without increasing our operations budget.

Our proposed Sustainable Library project caught the attention of the United Nations' recent Earth Summit (Rio+20) during 2012. Created through the retrofit and expansion of  an historic freight barn, the new library will provide a centralized location and facility that meets multiple community needs, i.e. 'community hub' for the chartered district .

The Valatie Free Library is a registered 501(c)(3) organization.

Name: Neva Lomason Memorial Library
Description: Neva Lomason Memorial Library is a libraries company based out of 710 ROME ST, Carrollton, Georgia, United States.

Name: Lyons Regional Library District
Description: Lyons Regional Library District is a friendly modern library focused on serving its patrons and all who visit. We offer a variety of programs for all ages and stages including themed storytimes; STEM events; informational, educational, and entertaining events; writing support and events; and we promote financial literacy through Common Cents of Colorado. Our wifi is strong and free to patrons, we have stand alone pc and Macintosh computers as well as IPads and laptops for use. Any of our librarians can assist with technology questions. We also have an excellent collection of materials including books, DVDS, Audio-books, e-books and objects (such as umbrellas and American Girl Dolls) for check out. We are part of the AspenCat consortium and we have access to materials at over 100 libraries in Colorado. We are a small but mighty district! We are in the process of building a new library facility and community center. This building will be flexible and is built with our district and the future in mind!

Name: La Fanzinothèque genevoise
Description:
La fanzinothèque genevoise est une gérée par association à but non lucratif.
Elle rassemble, préserve et met en valeur les fanzines, livres d’artistes et microéditions locales dans le but de promouvoir la création et la rencontre entre le public et les créat-eur-ice-s.
Les fanzines et la microédition sont des moyens d’expression libre et permettent aux petites voix de transmettre leur histoire, les fanzines sont une source d’information socioculturelle importante qui échappe souvent aux institutions, leur préservation et leur mise en valeur permet de sauvegarder un pan culturel minoritaire, diversifié et underground.

Buts :
Récolter, préserver, archiver, promouvoir et mettre à disposition des fanzines, livres d’artistes et microéditions, romandes, suisses et internationales
Soutenir les productrices et producteurs locaux en achetant, cataloguant et préservant les fanzines et livres d’artistes.

La fanzinothèque est un espace d’accueil ouvert pour la consultation de fanzines et microéditions. Elle propose des permanences de consultation, des rencontres et des ateliers autour des fanzines et de la microédition.

Valeurs : Inclusivité / Créativité / Culture / Partage

Les permanences et rendez-vous sont annoncées sur le site et sur la page instagram de la fanzinothèque.
La fanzinothèque est soutenue financièrement par ses membres et fonctionne grâce au bénévolat. En 2024, la Fanzinothèque a bénéficié d’un soutien de la Ville de Genève.
La fanzinothèque est une institution publique ouverte à tou-x-t-e-s

Le fonds comporte de près de 2’700 fanzines/livres d’artistes, dont quelques ouvrages documentaires, DVD, CD et livres objets, mais aussi un peu d’infokiosque.
Le fonds est principalement issu de donations de membres, d’artistes et collectifs, d’échanges et d’achats lors de vernissages et salons.

La fanzinothèque accueille et intègre volontiers vos dons dans sa collection, contactez-nous !

Name: West Liberty Public Library
Description:
Our Mission

The mission of the West Liberty Public Library is to provide open access to cultural, intellectual, educational, and recreational information and ideas to enrich the community.

We house books, magazines, newspapers, and movies, we provide literacy and educational based programming for adults and children as well as host several online databases full of digital information. As a public entity, all of our resources are provided for free to patrons within the West Liberty Community School District, including community members of West Liberty, Atalissa, and Nichols.

Vision Statement

The West Liberty Free Public Library serves the needs of the community and surrounding areas by providing and maintaining access to current resources for information and technology. We strive for continual program development, expanding collection materials, maintaining the highest administrative standards, increasing financial independence, and preserving our historical documentation for future reference. We also recognize the specific need for a central gathering space for community discussion and exchange of ideas to educate and identify the unique cultural diversity of our populace.

Name: Librairie Santé Intégrale
Description:
Bienvenue à la Librairie Santé Intégrale, votre destination privilégiée pour découvrir et acquérir une sélection soigneusement choisie d'ouvrages modernes et pertinents. Nous croyons fermement au pouvoir de la lecture pour inspirer, éduquer et transformer votre vie et celle de votre famille dans tous ses aspects.

Que vous recherchiez des conseils pour prévenir ou traiter les maladies, des outils pour surmonter le stress et l'anxiété, des clés pour épanouir votre vie familiale ou sociale, des ressources pour réussir professionnellement, des guides pour enrichir votre vie spirituelle, ou simplement des lectures divertissantes qui nourrissent votre esprit, nous avons ce qu'il vous faut.

Notre gamme d'ouvrages est conçue pour répondre aux besoins de tous les âges et de toutes les étapes de la vie, des enfants aux personnes âgées. Nous sommes là pour vous accompagner dans votre quête de bien-être et de développement personnel à travers les pages de nos livres.

Faites un pas vers une vie plus épanouie et équilibrée en explorant notre collection dès aujourd'hui en ligne ou à travers l'un de nos agents dédiés.

Description: WPML is a small but vibrant public library serving the residents of Ho-Ho-Kus, NJ. Our mission is to provide and promote open and equal access to the resources and services of the library in order to meet the informational, educational, and cultural needs of the community. The library seeks to encourage reading and the use of current technology for life-long learning and the enhancement of the individual's quality of life.

Name: Pound Ridge Library
Description:
Opened in 1952, the Pound Ridge Library has a collection of over 50,000 items including books, magazines, DVDs, music CDs, and CD audio books. The library’s collection is broad and diverse, but is particularly strong in the arts, biographies, mysteries, and cookbooks. The library offers electronic database searching and Internet access for reference and research.

As the cultural center of the town, the library hosts 100+ events and activities a month such as art and craft exhibits, driver safety programs, films, concerts, lectures, and book-discussion programs year-round. The library also sponsors a myriad of children’s activities including winter and spring vacation programs, story hours, holiday happenings, arts and crafts workshops, musical performances, and the annual Summer Reading Program.

Pound Ridge Library is a member of the Westchester Library System (WLS), a cooperative of 38 independent public libraries in Westchester County. As a patron of our library, you have access to materials from any of the member libraries through WLS’s online public access catalog. Materials ordered from other WLS libraries can to be picked up at the Pound Ridge Library. Patrons may also check out books and return materials at any WLS library.

Name: Studijní a vědecká knihovna v Hradci Králové
Description: Studijní a vědecká knihovna v Hradci Králové byla založena v roce 1949. Od roku 2008 sídlí v nadčasové budově ve tvaru písmene X v centru města. Již od svého počátku věnuje velkou péči svému knihovnímu fondu, který se každým rokem rozšiřuje o nové knihy, časopisy a další dokumenty. Knihovna SVK HK ale není jen půjčovnou knih, je to také komunitní místo, prostor pro sdílení nápadů nebo také obývák města. Knihovna nabízí řadu míst ke studiu, četbě či relaxaci. Nabízí také řadu služeb. Například osobního knihovníka, vzdělávací lekce a workshopy pro školy, workshopy a kurzy pro dospělé, digitální knihovnu, rešerše, meziknihovní výpůjčky a další.

Name: Prakrith Arivagam
Description: Prakrith Arivagam is a non-profit children's library that aims to replicate the impressive library system I experienced during my 17 years in the US. Inspired by the impact of libraries on my own children's reading habits, I want to create a similar system in India. Prakrith Arivagam is a small step towards that goal, currently charging a nominal monthly fee of Rs. 200 while allowing members to read unlimited books. However, my vision is to provide free access to all books. Currently, I have around 2000 books on display, with another 2000 stored in boxes due to limited space. All the books in my library are in English as they were acquired from the USA. To expand, I aspire to secure sufficient funding and members, enabling me to showcase the remaining books and introduce Tamil, Hindi, Telugu, and other local language books. Ultimately, the library's main objective is to help children cultivate and maintain a reading habit while minimizing screen time.

Name: Polytechnic College of Davao Del Sur Inc., Digos City, Davao Del Sur
Bibliothèque Centrale de la Province de Hainaut
Description:
La Bibliothèque centrale du Hainaut a été reconnue dans le cadre du décret de 2009 de la Fédération Wallonie-Bruxelles comme opérateur d’appui.
Son territoire de compétence c’est donc toute la province et ses bénéficiaires, les opérateurs directs – ou bibliothèques locales – des 49 communes qui organisent un service public de la lecture reconnu dans le cadre de la même législation.
Comme son nom l’indique, l’opérateur d’appui, l’OA pour les intimes, est un opérateur de seconde ligne dont le rôle principal est d’apporter son soutien au réseau des bibliothèques hainuyères dans l’exécution de leurs missions auprès des publics.
Travailleurs de l’ombre, les membres de la petite équipe de l’OA sont peu connus du grand public. En effet, leurs tâches, pourtant essentielles tant à leurs usagers directs – les bibliothèques – qu’à travers ceux-ci, à leurs usagers finaux – les citoyens, ils les réalisent dans leurs bureaux, derrière leur PC, dans des hangars de stockage ou en sillonnant les routes de Comines à Thuin en passant par Ath, La Louvière ou Charleroi.

N'hésitez pas à nous contacter : bibliotheque.centrale@hainaut.be

Name: Marion E. Wade Center
Description: The Marion E. Wade Center promotes cultural engagement and spiritual formation by offering a collection of resources available nowhere else in the world. We emphasize the ongoing relevance of seven British Christian authors who provide a distinctive blend of intellect, imagination, and faith: C.S. Lewis, J.R.R. Tolkien, G.K. Chesterton, George MacDonald, Dorothy L. Sayers, Owen Barfield, and Charles Williams. The Wade Center implements these goals by assisting scholars in their study of unique materials by and about these seven authors in order to generate new understandings, sharing insights with a broader audience through our numerous programs and publications, supporting artistic works inspired by our authors, and welcoming visitors to our museum.

Name: UHM Hamilton Library
Description:
Welina mai e nā hoa mai ka hiki ʻana a ke kau ʻana o ka lā.

Welcome! The Library at the University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa is an intellectual and cultural hub at the crossroads of campus. The Library’s world class collections and librarian and staff expertise enable the learning, teaching, research, and service activities of the university community. Whether you are a student writing your first research paper, a faculty member using digitized primary sources or large data sets, a graduate student exploring digital scholarship, or a visiting scholar drawn here by our extraordinary special collections, our librarians and staff are ready to connect you with the materials you need and lend their expertise to help you leverage new technologies to their full advantage.

As vital partners in the University’s research, teaching and learning, the Library supports UH Mānoa’s land-grant, sea-grant, and space-grant status enabling UH Mānoa to create, refine, disseminate, and perpetuate human knowledge. The Libraries build, sustain, and make discoverable print and digital collections; with more than 3.2 million print volumes, as well as archives and manuscripts, maps, rare books, audiovisuals, art, and millions of digital resources. The Library has world-class collections related to Hawai’i, Oceania, and the Pacific Islands, as well as China, Japan, Korea, Okinawa, the Philippines, Russia, South Asia, and Southeast Asia which support campus programs and researchers from across the globe. With a commitment to excellence, innovation, diverse ideas, global understanding, as well as western, non-western, and indigenous ways of knowing, we select and create, organize and protect, provide and teach access to resources that enable the creation of new knowledge in all its forms.

Everyone is welcome here. Everyone is encouraged to interact with one another in order to build a deeper and better understanding of each other, our cultures, and ideas.

- تشجيعا لكل الطاقات الفكرية والإبداعية المغربية، وإيمانا برسالة خدمة الإبداع والفكر المغربيين، وإسهاما منا في التعريف بعطاءات المبدعين والمفكرين المغاربة، حرصنا على نشر مجموعة من الكتب الثقافية في مجالات القصة والرواية، والمسرح والشعر والنقد الأدبي، إلى جانب سلسلة من الكتب التربوية التي تجدون أسفله بعضا من عناوينها، وأسماء كتابها ومؤلفيها

- على رأس هذه الكتب، يأتي إصدارنا للمصحف الشريف برواية ورش عن نافع الذي حظي بمراجعة وموافقة مؤسسة محمد السادس لنشر المصحف الشريف، وإلى جانب كتاب الله العزيز، تتوفر مكتبة السلام الجديدة على مجموعة كبيرة من المؤلفات المتعلقة بتفسير القرآن الكريم وعلومه لكبار العلماء المسلمين قديما وحديثا، فضلا عن كتب الصحاح المتعلقة بالحديث النبوي الشريف بمختلف الطبعات وأجملها إخراجا وتنسيقا، ناهيك عن الدراسات الفقهية والعقيدة الإسلامية…

Name: Always Reading Caravan Association
Description:
Always reading Caravan (ARC) is a non-governmental, not-for-profit, and non-religious organisation based in Suphanburi province Thailand, committed to promoting joy of reading and learning through various activities including mobile library services to children both with and without disabilities in rural areas of the country.

The organisation was founded in Jan. 2010 by Yoshimi Horiuchi, and currently conducts activities in two areas: Oothong District, Suphanburi Province, and Phrao District, Chiangmai Province.

ARC's mission is to promote reading for pleasure, provide equal reading opportunities for children from various backgrounds, and encourage children with and without disabilities to mingle with one another and remove the mental barrier between them.

ARC has 2 community libraries in Thailand, and a mobile library in the north of Thailand, which visits schools around the district and people who find it difficult to come to the library for various health and disability reasons. 

ARC also has 3 early childhood centres in the mountainous regions of northern Thailand, where hill tribe children from the age of 2 years attend, to teach basic Thai language skills and English, in preparation for their move to school.
A new initiative has been created known as Move Lanna, which is dedicated to connecting small rural projects and NGOs in Chiang Mai province, with volunteers from around the world. We have projects in education, medical care, community development and organisational development.

Name: Franklin Parish Library
Description:
The Franklin Parish Library seeks to foster a life-long love of reading in all its patrons, facilitate research and study, to ensure equal access and service to all, and to enrich the parish by providing opportunities for information, education, culture, recreation, inspiration, and imagination. The library further seeks to be a faithful steward of the resources entrusted by the taxpayers of Franklin Parish. We currently serve an estimated population of 19,308 according to the United States Census Bureau (2022). Our library is a community-based resource whose general purpose includes:
1.	Acquiring, organizing, administering, preserving, and making available books, periodicals, DVD’s, microfilm displays, and other materials for the communication of ideas that contribute to an enlightened citizenry and enriched personal lives
2.	Maintaining an up-to-date, non-circulating collection of appropriate reference material
3.	Assisting individuals to reach personal goals
4.	Making available library-related programs, events, activities, and exhibits

We also offer public computers for patron use, printing services, Wi-Fi, and genealogical resources with an ever-growing Franklin Parish historical collection available to our residents so they can search family history or answer questions related to Franklin Parish history.

Our Learning Center is a space for educational programs such as the Children’s Summer Reading, Adult Summer Reading, events such as veteran’s programs, various themed activities throughout the year, and other library programs that we’re hoping to begin in the coming year. It’s also available for non-profit organizations in our community to use for their meetings or activities when the library is not using the space for an event.

Recent studies indicate reading aloud and parent-child interaction that have very vital impacts on the child’s development, including language skills, social development, trust, empathy and an invaluable bonding experience. We believe that early childhood education and parenting are important ways to get Vietnamese children ready succeed in school and have a life better in future.

Name: Western New York Library Resources Council
Description:
The Western New York Library Resources Council (WNYLRC) is a not-for-profit consortium of all types of libraries and library systems serving six counties: Cattaraugus, Chautauqua, Erie, Genesee, Niagara, and Orleans. The Council was chartered by the New York State Board of Regents in 1966. 

WNYLRC is one of the nine regional 3Rs (Reference, Research, Resources) councils that comprise the Empire State Library Network (ESLN). The 3Rs councils, including WNYLRC, were formed to promote resource sharing among diverse types of libraries and library systems (academic, public, school, special, hospital, and other repositories.

The current service model, developed in 2011, allows WNYLRC to be open to change, letting WNYLRC stay ahead of technological advancements. WNYLRC encourages experimentation within WNYLRC’s office and in partnership with our members. Therefore, the continuing incubator service model of questioning existing practices and testing new ideas allows WNYLRC to: 

   - Operate on the premise of specific principles that consider the end users and the library community as beneficiaries of its actions rather than relying on a mission statement that specifies current services and has the potential to become outdated.
   - Provide seed money when available for new initiatives and avoid supporting in perpetuity programs and services that are core to library services and should be supported by member libraries.
  -  Remain nimble, adaptable, and proactive, and therefore better able to support its members.
  -  Assess and evaluate the rapid change in the library industry and position WNYLRC to lead and assist its members in meeting the challenges and opportunities that are the consequence of change.

Through reading circles, creative writing workshops, author interactions, and thematic discussions, Kagaj ko Katha explores literature alongside issues that matter — from climate change and social justice to identity and entrepreneurship.

Name: York Public Library
Description:
York Public Library was founded by the York Public Library Association. Incorporated in 1922, YPLA is a nonprofit entity that owns and governs the Library. 
For over three quarters of a century, a 1,500 square foot building served York’s population as it grew from 2,600 to 13,000. During this same time, the workplace and society itself went through drastic changes – typewriters became word processors, became computers, and then: the Internet. Library workflow became automated and card catalogs became Online Public Access Catalogs, or OPACs.
We have served York’s 13,000 residents and estimated 30,000 annual visitors from our beautiful ‘new’ facility, located in the heart of York Village, since 2001. We are both a literal and figurative community center. The Library provides access to materials, services and collections dedicated to people of all ages, income levels, ethnicities, and abilities. We offer a wealth of ongoing educational, recreational, and cultural programs – all geared to enrich, entice, and inspire – and all free and open to the public.
The Library’s 25,000 square feet provides ample space for community groups and organizations to meet, and our Community Meeting Room hosts the official gatherings of the Town’s Boards of Selectmen, Planning, Zoning, and Appeals as well as those for most municipal committees.
Our programming attracts people from across the southern Maine and Seacoast area. In a typical year, over 8,000 people will come through our doors each month to check out a book, get help with a new gadget, read the papers, enjoy a quiet workspace with free wifi, piece together a puzzle, attend a story hour, check their investments, craft with friends, attend a town meeting, research family history, catch a concert, take in an exhibit, get help with a resume, learn to play the ukulele, meet their neighbors, and tell their story.

Name: Accademia Georgica
Description:
L’Accademia Georgica di Treia visse il suo periodo più fecondo tra la fine del Settecento e l’inizio dell’Ottocento, proprio nell’età dell’Illuminismo le cui idee giunsero nelle Marche ancor prima dell’ Armata Napoleonica.
Il notevole incremento demografico di quel periodo causò una forte crisi economica che investì tutta l’Europa. Rimedi efficaci potevano essere  il progresso e lo sviluppo dell’ agricoltura. Questa esigenza trovò terreno fertile a Treia  dove nel 1778 alcuni intellettuali innovatori e appassionati di agronomia trasformarono l’ Accademia dei Sollevati (sec.XV) in un centro per lo studio e la sperimentazione in agricoltura  cui diedero il nome di Accademia Georgica dei Sollevati.
L’Accademia Georgica ha sede  nella prestigiosa palazzina ottocentesca disegnata dall’architetto Giuseppe Valadier e fa corona alla Piazza principale della città.
Essa conserva un prezioso patrimonio librario con 12.000 volumi, un ricco patrimonio archivistico costituito dall’Archivio storico comunale e dal fondo diplomatico-pergamenaceo comprendente 1200 pergamene, l’Archivio degli Accademici con manoscritti relativi agli studi nel settore dell’agricoltura, incunaboli, codici, conii, sigilli, la collezione di foto con dedica e autografo di personaggi famosi donata da Raffaele Simboli, quadri del pittore futurista Giacomo Balla, i ritratti di personaggi illustri (Bartolomeo Vignati, Giulio Acquaticci, Ilario Altobelli, Luigi Lanzi, Fortunato Benigni).
Sono, inoltre, custoditi i fondi archivistici dei Filippini, delle Clarisse, il fondo musicale Montebello,le carte relative al Teatro Comunale di Treia.
Ancora oggi l’Accademia offre un importante sussidio allo sviluppo culturale della realtà regionale e  rimane il punto di riferimento di studiosi e di ricercatori.
Lo sforzo che il Magistrato Accademico sta sostenendo in questi ultimi anni è quello di offrire alla collettività un servizio sempre più rispondente alle nuove esigenze.

Name: Newtown Library Company

The historic library offers many programs and community events, as well as Ebooks and AudioBooks for its members. Wystartowaliśmy w 2015 roku, sukcesywnie poszerzając portfolio rozwiązań dla bibliotek. Jego pierwszym elementem był hosting systemów bibliotecznych. Description:
The Club Of Serial Readers este locul in care te intalnesti cu prietenii tai sau cu alti pasionati de lectura si discuti despre cele mai citite carti, cele mai interesante povesti. Dar sa o luam in ordine!

Pe SR:
- Poti sa iti faci cont. In momentul in care ai devenit Serial Reader esti oficial unul de-al nostru, este declaratia ta ca ai devenit un Serial Reader "cu acte in regula" si poti sa activezi in club la toate nivelurile, fara restrictii!
- Serial Reader fiind, ai dreptul:
sa propui carti spre a fi citite,
sa votezi carti,
sa participi la discutii,
sa comentezi cartile,
sa scrii recenzii si, de ce nu,
sa iti faci prieteni!

The Club Of Serial Readers este locul unde nu intalnesti decat Serial Readeri ca si tine. Si ce poate fi mai interesant decat sa discuti ultima carte citita cu noii tai prieteni?

The library possesses fully computerized all routine activities, a functional virtual library service, which gives faculty, staff and students, access to the Web Public Access Catalogue (WebPAC), and other electronic resources to aid research and learning.

Description:

We are an all-volunteer organization with no paid staff, but we are open seven days a week, and one evening. Please go to our website for more information.

Name: Stillwater Public Library, NY
Description:
The Stillwater Public Library’s Mission is to provide educational, informational, and recreational resources to patrons of all ages, cultural, and economic backgrounds. The library is committed to protecting intellectual freedom, promoting lifelong learning, and providing materials and resources that accomplish these objectives.

In 1949, Elizabeth Beiter and Barbara Alexik were designated captains of an important journey. Armed with a love of reading and the desire to share, they solicited book donations, latched onto a few volunteers and set off to institute a library. The library was first located in a one-room cabin in the center of the village of Stillwater. After ten years, they purchased St. John’s Episcopal Church (originally built in 1874) for $500 and were provisionally named The Stillwater Free Library.

Elizabeth stayed on as our volunteer librarian for many years. Many townspeople served as volunteer clerks under her guidance. Open hours were pretty regular, although Elizabeth did walk down the street to her house for lunch each day. When Elizabeth returned after lunch, everyone knew it. How? When the library was ready for business, the light over the front door would be turned on… The same as today!

Since then, we have followed the trend of America’s libraries. Libraries are not only about reading for pleasure. They are also about distributing information, providing access to technology, serving as a meeting place, and offering the perfect environment for bettering ourselves. Thanks to these women, their tireless volunteers and the many directors, staff members and trustees since them, the Stillwater Library remains at the heart of our community; an essential guiding force for all of our futures.

In 2016, the residents of the Stillwater Central School District voted to create a taxing district for the Stillwater Public Library.  With strong funding and public support, the path for the library’s future gets better and better!

Name: Boekenwolk
Description:

Name: Union Library Company of Hatborough
Description:
In existence since 1755, The Union Library Company of Hatborough is the second oldest library still operating under its original charter in the state of Pennsylvania.

In August, 1755, Joshua Potts, Charles Beatty, John Lukens and Joseph Hart met with other interested local citizens at the Crooked Billet Tavern. Thirty- eight men signed an ‘Instrument of Partnership’ bringing into existence “The Union Library Company of Hatborough”. Each member agreed to an annual donation of ten shillings to be used for the purchase of books and by August 1756, the first shipment of fifty-nine books arrived from England. Some of these original books are still in the library’s possession.

In 1848, Nathan Holt, one of the oldest Company members, died. In his will, Mr. Holt bequeathed $5,000 to the Library Company with the provision that $2,000 was to be used to purchase books. Three thousand dollars was used to purchase a lot and build a permanent library structure. The Greek Revival style building was completed in 1850 at what is now 243 South York Road.

On May 8, 1978, the building was placed on the State Registry of Historical Buildings. On November 27, 1979 it was placed on the National Register of Historic Places.
