NCC provides open access to individuals and groups who do not have Japanese resources available, and develops online instruction on the efficient navigation and use of those resources for such users. 

NCC serves as a vocal advocate for broader access to Japanese information for all users, and stands ready to work with librarians, faculty, students, and others to develop new initiatives to meet emerging library and information resource needs to advance Japanese studies scholarship and to benefit understanding internationally.

Name: Sadie Pope Dowdell Library
Description:
The City of South Amboy's Public Library.

Sadie Pope Dowdell was born in the year 1900. At the age of 23, she became Head Librarian of the South Amboy Free Public Library.

Miss Dowdell was also Secretary and Treasurer of the Board of Trustees for the library. She relinquished the position of Secretary of the Board in 1941; and relinquished the position of Treasurer in 1949.

In May of 1960, after 37 years of service, the Board voted to give Miss Dowdell a three-week vacation. They closed the library for the last two weeks in July and the first week in August to accommodate her vacation.

In November of 1965, the Board received a letter of resignation from Miss Sadie Pope Dowdell; but she agreed to stay on until a qualified replacement could be found. In 1966, Miss Dowdell had arranged for a 15-minute Storytime with a storyteller for the summer program.

Unfortunately, in early 1970, Miss Dowdell became ill and was unable to continue in her post. This caused the Board to be forced to accept her resignation. A new Library Director wasn’t found until June of 1971.

In 1972, the Board voted to change the name of the library from South Amboy Free Public Library to Sadie Pope Dowdell Library to commemorate her 48 years of service as Library Director. Sadly, after being very ill for ten years, Miss Sadie Pope Dowdell died in February 1980.

2004 fick personalen på Dieselverkstadens bibliotek möjligheten att själva driva biblioteket. Detta ledde till en hel del nytänkande och innovativ verksamhet som blev väldigt uppskattad. Dieselverkstadens bibliotek fick studiebesök från olika delar av världen och inbjudningar att föreläsa kring hur bibliotek kan drivas. 2013 anslöt sig Saltsjöbadens bibliotek och Älta bibliotek till samma personaldrivna organisation. Vi driver biblioteken på uppdrag av Nacka Kommun och kulturnämnden bestämmer ramarna för verksamheten. Det finns totalt sex bibliotek i Nacka med en gemensam mediesamling som ägs av Nacka Kommun.

Hos oss finns inga chefer. Alla jobbar ute på golvet och träffar besökare och vi har alla hand om inköp. Vi är lika många kvinnor som män och vi lägger vikt vid att rekrytera personal med olika bakgrund och erfarenhet. Detta för att skapa en dynamisk och kreativ verksamhet för våra besökare. Vi jobbar aktivt med demokrati, mångfald och miljö vilket genomsyrar vår verksamhet både vad gäller utbud och arrangemang.

Biblioteken har en central plats i samhället. Kultur, samhällsengagemang och eftertanke står i centrum. Och framförallt står våra besökare i centrum.

Varmt välkommen till oss.

Studio12 accredited courses are in partnership with the Breeze Arts Foundation, or via funded programmes of work that enable us to fund qualifications. Name: Library of Chinese Academy of Sciences
Description:
The library of the Chinese Academy of Sciences(CAS), also named as the National Science Library, Chinese Academy of Sciences (NSLC),  is the public library service system of CAS as well as the National Library of Sciences in Chinese National Science and Technology Libraries (NSTL) system.  Under a Board Trustees appointed by CAS, The library consists of a Main Library (based in Beijing) and two branch libraries, respectively Chengdu Branch Library and Wuhan Branch Library. The library also co-operates, together with selected CAS research institutes, a number of special branch libraries. 
The library functions as the key library nationally for collecting information resources and providing information services in natural sciences, inter-disciplinary fields, and high tech fields, for the researchers and students of CAS and for the researchers around the country.  It also conducts services such as information analysis, digital library system development, scientific publication (with its 17 journals), and promotion of sciences.
The library is actively participating and leading national efforts to build a powerful National Scientific Information Infrastructure,  and collaborates with major domestic and foreign libraries for resources sharing and research collaboration. It is credited to grant doctor and master degrees in library science and information science, with a yearly enrollment more than 50 students.  The library also hosts senior visiting scholars and organizes vocational training and continuing education programs.
The library, aiming at developing a world first-class information service ability and leadership in library development in the country, strives to strengthen its resources, improve its systems, and innovate its services, to best suit its users and its scientific research communities.

Description:
The Mulvane Public Library was organized by the Federated Women’s Club in 1921. After an election in 1928, it officially became the City’s Library. In 2016 the Library relocated to a brand new building at 408 N. 2nd Ave. The new building features a multipurpose room, computer lab, genealogy area, study rooms, comfy seating for people of all ages, and windows!

The Library offers great programs year-round to all ages.  Call us, stop by, or browse our website for the complete listing of services and resources the Library provides the community.

The Library welcomes donations of books and other items. The donated items that cannot be utilized in our collection will go in the Friends Book Sale and the proceeds benefit the Library.

Mission Statement
The mission of the Mulvane Public Library is to provide a well balanced collection of materials and services for community residents of all ages for personal enrichment, enjoyment, and educational needs. The Library is dedicated to promoting literacy and lifelong learning within the community.

Name: Knowledge for All
Description:
Knowledge for All is a collaborative project to develop the first free, open tool of its kind for discovering, sharing and accessing scholarly journal literature. Based out of the University of Prince Edward Island with support from the Council of Atlantic University Libraries, the project invites all members of the international library and academic community, along with creators, managers and seekers of scholarly literature across the board to participate in building an open index and search tool that will truly meet their needs.

The tool will allow institutions, universities, libraries, archives, researchers, academics, students and members of the public at large to search across all academic disciplines and freely access a comprehensive collection of scholarly journal content. Its software and data will be open and freely available for the benefit of the community. The tool is being developed using a collaborative model - academic and library institutions around the world share in project costs, content creation and system development. They contribute funding, time and skills to build Knowledge for All for their own benefit and eliminate dependence on expensive proprietary databases and products. As an open source, customizable tool with robust search features, Knowledge for All will give institutions and individuals control over their content and technology.

Visit http://www.k4all.ca/ to learn more and join the Knowledge for All community

Name: Off-the-Shelf Library Services
Description: Off-the-Shelf is a full service library consulting firm. In addition to traditional library services such as cataloging, acquisitions advice, library set-up, and library evaluation, it offers workshops designed to give students in institutions without regular library instruction an exciting and entertaining way to learn how to access, evaluate, and process information from print and non-print sources.

Description: Rahway Public Library is a library that offers books, movies, and audio.

Name: Readers Den
Description:
Readers Den is an exclusive lending library for kids located in JP Nagar (South Bangalore).  Readers Den was founded with a mission to promote reading at an early age, and encourage learning through fun-based activities and variety of engagements like puppet shows, storytelling sessions and reading clubs.  Accordingly, Readers Den houses books for all age group of kids - from toddlers to teens! 

For early readers, Readers Den helps parents kick-start their child's reading journey through board/picture books initially, phonics/leveled reading books next (to supplement kindergarten learning), and early chapter books subsequently to instill the habits of reading.  

For mid-level and advanced readers, Readers Den provide a wide collection of books to choose from.  The collection of books spans across all genres including fiction, mystery, mythology, humor, adventure, fantasy and non-fiction written by both Indian and foreign authors.  

As a part of the service, Readers Den provides book recommendations based on the child's reading level and help the child to constantly upgrade their reading level.  Members can be rent books from Readers Den based on the subscription plan opted.  There are multiple subscription plans available to choose from.  Plans vary from two books at time to 10 books at a time, limited exchanges in a month to unlimited exchanges, etc.  Readers Den also offers home delivery and pick-up options for certain plans.

Readers Den also houses an engagement centre for kids where parents can get their toddlers/young kids and engage them learning based activities with puzzles, blocks and activity sheets.  Engagement centre is available on pay-by-the-hour basis for non-members too.

Name: Newbury Town Library
Description:
The mission of the Newbury Town Library is to provide free and open access to materials, services, and new technologies supporting life-long learning, as well as to encourage and enable the pursuit of knowledge and the satisfaction of curiosity in our community.

First opened in September 1926, the Newbury Town Library was housed over the general store on Main Street in Byfield. It was eventually relocated to Lunt Street and was the first public building in Massachusetts to be built by vocational high school students. The current building was completed in 2000 and is located on the Lunt Street site. 

The Newbury Town Library is a member of the Merrimack Valley Library Consortium (MVLC) — an alliance of 36 Massachusetts libraries embracing the sharing of materials, resources, and services. Some of the things our library offers are discounted museum passes, study areas, computers, research resources, magazines, and newspapers, as well as books, eBooks, eAudiobooks, eMagazines, and eCourses.  

We also offer a variety of adult and children's programs throughout the year such as lecture series, author visits, Book Club, Knitting Club, film screenings, Genealogy Club, Computer/Tablet/Mobile Device Tech Help, special events and more! 

For our open hours, please visit our website at newburylibrary.org/about-us/contact

Name: Aaron Cohen Associates Ltd
Description: We are library consultants, designers and facility planners. We recommend and facilitate planning activities for large and small building projects as program consultants. We worked on over 500 planning projects, creating 40 years of library research documentation and visual schematics. We have develop guidelines for academic and government libraries. We have developed ARS systems and master facilities plans for systems, urban libraries, and community libraries. We have helped librarians improve their planning efforts for many different types of libraries  academic libraries, public libraries, medical, law and government and special. Our library consultants are technology specialists who provide efficient and effective designs including Mixed Media, video, lighting, LEED and Green Designs. We work with facility planners, administration and IT managers as an integrated team. Our international experience includes the 14 years consulting for the National Library of Singapore, 10 years consulting for New York Public Library, 8 years working for the University of Illinois, Chicago, 10 years working for Long Island University (CW post). We have performed over 500 workshops on library space planning. These include: Stanford University, Northeastern University, New Orleans Public Library, Federal Library and Information Center (LOC) and Infopeople workshops. Library building workshops include ebook research, automation, technical services, acquisitions, management and studies on the library of the future. Our library consultants use a solid methodology to provide for sound decisionmaking. The outcomes from our work include physical and technical design solutions. For example, we developed new combinations of library services and technology centers. It was a multiyear master planning framework that included distance learning, automation, succession planning, staff workflows, master planning. It included options for the library management to consider. Indeed, the building requirements for a library can be complex. Our clients benefit from past research; library building, library service planning, efficiency studies and organizational development.

Name: Community Archiving Workshop
Description:
Community Archiving Workshop (CAW) seeks to share skills, knowledge, and resources to empower under-resourced, community-held audiovisual collections to increase visibility and support shared authority. The acronym CAW is used interchangeably to describe the training model and the group of people who developed and delivered this model since 2011. 

CAW Collective is comprised of the core organizing members who are growing the organization to expand the CAW model and create new tools and workshop modules for underserved collections held by communities that have been historically excluded from the archival record. The mission of CAW Collective is to use the CAW model to facilitate grassroots audiovisual preservation, build peer to peer learning as a foundation for long term preservation, promote community networking, develop free resources to scale and share widely, and maintain communication platforms for access to these resources.

The CAW model is an open source event and accompanying tools and resulting resources are available to all through www.communityarchiving.org.

Name: NIPR, The National Collection of Northern Ireland Publications
Description:
NIPR (formerly known as The Northern Ireland Publications Resource) is Northern Ireland’s National Collection of Northern Ireland Publications.

Northern Ireland has no national library, so NIPR aims to identify, collect, preserve every book, pamphlet, periodical and report published in Northern Ireland since January 2000.

Bibliographic information about the collection is available in the online catalogue at www.nibooks.org. The Linen Hall Library, Belfast is our lead partner, providing assistance with collecting and preserving the collection, and a dedicated repository to house the collection.

Access to the NIPR Collection is initially through our web-based catalogue, which can be browsed over the Internet. You may consult material in the NIPR collection personally by asking at the counter in the Linen Hall Library.

Because NIPR does not have legal deposit status we rely heavily on the generosity of the local publishing community and most publishers are delighted to oblige by donating one copy of each publication.

In return for donating their material, NIPR helps publishers market their work by advertising the publications internationally through our online catalogue, a quarterly list of acquisitions, which in addition to being published online, is sent to libraries and universities throughout the world, and on social media.

We collect all types of publications on all subjects! They just need to be published in Northern Ireland from January 2000 onwards. 

NIPR is grateful to receive any material published in Northern Ireland since January 2000. Donations of publications should be clearly marked “FAO NIPR” and addressed to NIPR, c/o The Linen Hall Library, 17 Donegall Square North, Belfast BT1 5GB. Please contact us on nipr@nibooks.org if you require any further information.

NIPR now also accepts works published elsewhere provided that they have a NI distributor. Please check with us.

Name: Vihara Study Hall

Vihara Study hall Interiors have been designed in such a way to reduce Students difficulties of long hour sittings while studying. lightings have been chosen to scientifically enhance the study atmosphere.

Vihara reading hall has helped many IAS Aspirants to clear civil service / IAS Exam with flying colours.

We have Separate Reading hall for NEET UG/PG, CAT, GATE, XAT, MAT, USMLE and  CA  Aspirants. Students are provided with Serene Ambiance for their Exam Preparation. We Provide Hygenic reading room for Everyone. Vihara Study Hall has the best Automated System in Chennai for Providing Study hall services.

Name: WibShopp.com
Description:
WibShopp.com, a prominent company based in the province of Nampula, Mozambique, since 2024, has as its main objective to establish itself as the country's leading e-commerce platform. Our fundamental mission is to simplify and facilitate access to quality products and services throughout Mozambique, with just a few clicks.

We aim to offer our customers an uncomplicated and unique lifestyle, saving time and financial resources. Our commitment is to simplify the purchasing process, allowing our customers to purchase high quality products and receive them at their locations, with a secure and accessible payment system.

In a scenario of increasing growth in online shopping in Mozambique, our goal is to provide an exceptional shopping experience, connecting our customers to the best global suppliers. We guarantee ease of payment and ensure the quality of products and delivery services, seeking the full satisfaction of our customers. Our business model is based on retail, in which we do not keep products in stock.

Instead, when a customer purchases on our platform, we source the item from a third party, usually a manufacturer or wholesaler, and ship it directly to the customer. Our focus is on sales, marketing and customer service, ensuring your complete satisfaction

Name: Read & Write Pakistan
Description: Read & Write Pakistan (R&RP) is the project of Pakistan Librarians Welfare Organization (PLWO) and it was founded on August 22, 2015 at Islamabad. Literal backwardness in Pakistan has resulted in the failure of Pakistan’s producing quality thinkers, philosophers, authors, and theologians. Such lack of emphasis on the literary growth marks questions on our prospects of our emerging has an inhabitant of renowned nation and at the same underlines the intellectual decadents of the masses. The culture of reading and writing is the bedrock of any nation’s educational system and any educational system that fails to provide the said culture is doing sheer injustice to the otherwise aspiring youth. The concept of falling in love with books as per interest and the manifestation of it through writing is something Pakistan badly needs at this stage. Realizing the essence and vitality of it, Read & Write Pakistan is dedicatedly working to encourage and promote the much-neglected area of reading culture in Pakistan. Reading habits are not given due status in Pakistan which has resulted in disarraying the minds of the young generation. The initiative “Read & Write Pakistan” is committed to make this oversight area, a priority, to stimulate reading habits in Pakistan through organizing different programs. The main purpose of this program is to engage more and more people in reading and writing habits in order to make Pakistan an intellectual place. Read & Write Pakistan since its inception initiating many programs, which are best serving its objectives. We started different campaigns and programs like Aao Aik Kitaab Parain, Reading demonstration programs, Mobile libraries and building community libraries across Pakistan, etc., which yielded the best results and helped us in the promotion of reading culture in Pakistan. We also started a very unique campaign Build Little Free Library Around You" in order to build a free book exchange program.

Name: Louis Bay 2nd Library & Community Center
Description:
The Louis Bay 2nd Library provides current and historical cultural, entertainment, information, social, and technological services.  The library contains areas for adults, children, and teens, as well as a comprehensive reference department.

Materials offered at the library include fiction and non-fiction books, biographies, periodicals, DVDs, video games (xBox, Wii, WiiU, PlayStation, Nintendo Switch) and CDs.  We have an extensive children's and parenting collection.  Large print books and audiobooks are also available. Many adult, children, and teen programs are offered at the Louis Bay 2nd Library on a regular basis. Some amenities offered to the public include Internet access, color printing, free wi-fi, faxing, and photocopying.  

The Louis Bay 2nd Library is a member of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS).  This membership allows resident cardholders of the Louis Bay 2nd Library to use not only this library, but to borrow materials from any of the 77 libraries that belong to the consortium.  

Your library card also allows you to request books and periodicals from the online databases, as well as access downloadable audio and e-books from Libby.

Name: Cambridge Library Group
Description: The Cambridge Library Group (CLG) is a society for those with an interest in books, libraries and information. Members enjoy a varied programme of talks, visits and social events at roughly monthly intervals.

Name: Libros&Co.
Description:
Queremos dar una segunda vida a los libros, que las maravillosas historias que se esconden en el interior de cada página llenen la fantasía de otras personas, que la llama de los sueños que una vez encendieron, siga siendo faro que alumbre más noches. Libros que alimenten nuestro pensamiento y nos hagan decir en la noche, “un capítulo más y a dormir”

Somos una librería, de las que llaman de antiguo, con un desafío a los libros digitales, pero a la vez llevando el libro físico con la tecnología hasta cualquier sitio. Con miles de aventuras en estantes o en la web, a la espera de que los adoptes, de que sientas la llamada de ellos, que te están esperando para ser el vehículo que te lleve a donde quieras soñar.

Nuestro sueño es poner al alcance de todos, poder vivir esas mil vidas que nos dan los libros.

Entra en esta web y saca tu billete para la apasionante aventura de leer.
